paper_list.one_liner: Force-aware MoE module for VLA that dynamically routes F/T info across task phases
Generalist VLA policy using pre-trained VLM + flow matching action expert for multi-robot control
Extract force-informed action targets from kinesthetic demos to reproduce contact forces in policy execution
RL-based iterative improvement method (Recap) for VLA policies using advantage-weighted regression with a learned value function
Stretchable liquid-metal glove that estimates both finger bone lengths and joint angles from a single sensing mechanism for accurate hand pose reconstruction
DexUMI bridges kinematic and visual gaps between human and robot hands via exoskeleton hardware and AI inpainting to enable efficient dexterous manipulation skill transfer.

ExoStart converts ~10 exoskeleton demonstrations into dexterous robot manipulation policies via dynamics filtering, auto-curriculum RL, and vision-based distillation for zero-shot real-world transfer.
DEXOP is a passive exoskeleton that mechanically links human and robot fingers, enabling direct-contact dexterous demonstration collection at up to 8× the speed of teleoperation.
Gemini 2.0 multi-agent system that generates, debates, and evolves research hypotheses via Elo-based tournaments, with wet-lab-validated results in drug repurposing and target discovery
Tactile-feedback dexterous hand system that picks paper-like thin deformable objects via R-Tac slip detection + online force control + diffusion policy, achieving 87.5% success rate
Tactile skin sensor model for zero-shot sim-to-real transfer of ternary shear and binary normal forces, enabling RL-based dexterous in-hand translation
3D 촉각 데이터 학습의 어려움을 정규 좌표계 표현(canonical representation)과 힘 기반 자기지도 사전학습으로 해결해, 4가지 정밀 손 조작 태스크에서 78% 평균 성공률 달성
텔레오퍼레이션 없이 인간 시연을 기구학·촉각 리타겟팅 + 3DGS 시뮬 렌더링으로 어떤 로봇·카메라 뷰로도 변환하는 Real-Sim-Real 데이터 파이프라인
인간 햅틱 글러브와 로봇 손의 이질적 촉각 신호를 MANO UV 맵으로 통합하고 대조 학습으로 정렬해, 단 10분 페어 데이터로 제로샷 정책 이전 달성
손 형태·포즈의 통계적 모델 MANO를 3D 스캔 1,000개에서 학습, SMPL 신체와 통합해 전신 4D 캡처 실현
동일 오픈소스 촉각 글러브를 인간·로봇에 장착해 visual·tactile 구현 간극을 동시 해소, 로봇 데이터 없이 72% 성공률 달성
Open-access framework for systematically benchmarking and improving Code-as-Policy agents in robot manipulation
paper_list.memo_preview: CaP는 "LLM이 코드를 생성하여 API를 호출하는" 구조인데, 이는 "LLM이 태스크를 분해하여 API를 호출하는" 기존 LLM 플래너(SayCan, RoboClaw 등)와 본

paper_list.one_liner: Agentic framework unifying VLA data collection, policy learning, and long-horizon deployment under a single VLM controller with self-resetting EAP loops
First autonomous research framework specialized for clinical medicine with clinician-engineer co-reasoning for evidence-grounded ideation and MICCAI-level manuscript generation
Outer-loop system that searches over LLM harness code by giving proposer agents full filesystem access to prior candidates' source, traces, and scores
Embodied foundation model trained on 270K+ hours of real-world manipulation data, exhibiting phase transitions at 7B parameters and predictable power-law scaling
Second-generation embodied foundation model achieving 99% task success rates at 3x speed with only 1 hour of task-specific robot data
Three architectural patterns for building applications that adapt to Claude's evolving intelligence: use general tools, enable self-orchestration, and set boundaries carefully
Nine Claude Opus 4.6 instances achieved PGR 0.97 on weak-to-strong supervision, far surpassing human researchers' PGR 0.23
Home robot foundation model trained on zero robot data via Embodiment Alignment — human hand data directly transferred to robot
Open-source framework for AI agents to autonomously iterate on ML training — 700 experiments in 2 days, 11% faster GPT-2
Nature survey on self-driving labs: AI+robotics platforms autonomously running the full scientific experimentation loop
On-device VLA model achieving 572.6 TPH in simulation and 124 TPH in real-world via 3-stage training + ESPADA motion compression
Diffusion models applied to robot visuomotor policies — 46.9% average improvement over SOTA across 15 tasks from 4 benchmarks
$371 hand-held gripper + GoPro for in-the-wild robot teaching — zero-shot deployment across multiple robot platforms
UMI + 2g CoinFT 6-axis sensors on each finger for in-the-wild force-aware compliant manipulation learning
SO(2)-equivariant tactile network for sample-efficient in-hand rotation correction — 90% success from 10 demos
20,854 hours egocentric human video pretraining discovers scaling law (R²=0.9983) for robot dexterous manipulation
17 vision-based tactile sensors covering 70% of a 15-DoF biomimetic hand enable 100% success on multi-object adaptive grasping (vs 53.5% without tactile, p<0.0001)
Three-finger soft pneumatic hand with a 181,000 unit/cm² visual-tactile palm — palm-finger coordination yields 100% cup-cap pickup, 97% fabric classification, and dynamic teapot-pose stabilization
Systematic ablation across 19 tactile regions of a Shadow Hand shows the upper palm is nearly as important as distal phalanges for in-hand reorientation — fingertips alone are not optimal
Sakana AI의 7B Conductor는 RL로 LLM 에이전트들을 조율하는 통신 토폴로지·프롬프트 엔지니어링을 자동 학습해 LiveCodeBench·GPQA·AIME25에서 GPT-5·Gemini 2.5 Pro·Claude Sonnet 4를 능가하는 SOTA 평균 77.27%를 달성했다

Open-source generalist robot transformer pre-trained on 800k Open X-Embodiment trajectories with block-wise attention and a diffusion action head for modular fine-tuning to new robots, cameras, and action spaces.
A 600+ experiment systematic study of VLA design choices (backbone, action space, history, OXE timing) bundled into the open-source RoboVLMs meta-builder.
Global planner for contact-rich manipulation that smooths quasi-dynamic contact models and uses the resulting local models to guide RRT search
Steerable 5B VLA robot foundation model that uses rich context conditioning to combine mixed-quality robot, human, and web data into broad out-of-the-box generalization

A general-purpose dexterous-manipulation VLA that unifies motion awareness, long-term memory, and physical (tactile/torque) sensing in a Multi-Stream Action Transformer, with synthetic data and inference optimizations for real-time humanoid deployment.
paper_list.memo_preview: 🤖 정교한 양손 조작은 AI 모델, 로봇 제어, 로봇 하드웨어, 산업 도메인 지식이 모두 맞물려야 풀리는 문제다. RLDX-1은 자체 ALLEX 휴머노이드부터 모델·데이터·추론

paper_list.one_liner: ENPIRE turns real-world dexterous robot policy improvement into a coding-agent-managed physical autoresearch loop over reset, rollout, verification, and evolution.
OmniRetarget turns a single human whole-body demonstration into interaction-preserving humanoid reference trajectories by optimizing an interaction mesh with hard physical constraints.
PHP composes retargeted human parkour skills with motion matching and distills privileged RL experts into one depth-based Unitree G1 policy for autonomous long-horizon humanoid parkour.
FastSAC/FastTD3 make sim-to-real humanoid locomotion iteration a minutes-scale loop: 15-minute training on one RTX 4090 for Unitree G1 and Booster T1 locomotion under strong domain randomization.
A broad survey that unifies humanoid locomotion and manipulation around model-based planning/control, learning-based skill acquisition, foundation models, and whole-body tactile sensing.
A holistic survey that connects dexterous hand hardware, sensing, control/learning methods, datasets, and evaluation into one developmental map.

EgoDex uses Apple Vision Pro to collect 829 hours of egocentric dexterous manipulation video with 3D hand-pose, language, and camera annotations, then turns it into trajectory-prediction and inverse-dynamics benchmarks.
A canonical Cell review that organizes mammalian aging into nine mechanistic hallmarks and three functional layers to guide healthspan interventions.
A JEADV review that maps short-term exposome stressors onto six adult skin functions and their biological and clinical consequences.
Decentralized PBT on Isaac Gym scales PPO to Allegro hand-arm regrasping, throwing, and reorientation, enabling simulated 46-DoF dual-arm policies.

PACE fits a 4n+1 physically interpretable joint-dynamics model from about 20 seconds of encoder-only in-air data, enabling dynamics-randomization-free zero-shot locomotion across diverse robots while reducing ANYmal's full Cost of Transport by 32%.

LLM-as-a-Verifier turns scoring-token logit distributions into continuous trajectory scores and scales verification through granularity, repetition, and criteria decomposition, reaching state of the art across coding, robotics, and medical agent benchmarks without verifier training.

The framework freezes a vision transformer, learns language-conditioned affordance prompts, and uses those heatmaps to condition a few-step flow-matching robot policy, reaching 0.82 real-world ADL success and 13.228 ms one-step trajectory inference.

candidates.canonical_id: title:ai is already moving the logistics industry forward
candidates.title: AI Is Already Moving the Logistics Industry Forward
AoE: Always-on Egocentric Data Collection for Robot Learning
candidates.method_summary: Always-on egocentric data collection expands the EgoMimic/EgoScale line from curated demonstrations toward continuous human work capture. Manufacturing relevance is high because the data source can be skilled operators performing real processes.
candidates.limitations: Privacy, consent, process-IP leakage, and force/tactile missingness are central deployment barriers.

candidates.canonical_id: title:aoe augmentation of experience via human ego demonstrations
candidates.title: AoE: Augmentation of experience via human ego demonstrations
candidates.method_summary: AoE augments robot replay buffers with retargeted human ego demonstrations during RL training, accelerating policy convergence. Uses inverse kinematics retargeting plus domain-randomized rendering to inject human experience as 'pseudo-robot' transitions.
limitations: Retargeting errors create distribution shift in replay buffer
Sim-only validation; real-world transfer unclear
No tactile augmentation

candidates.title: APEX: Learning adaptive high-platform traversal for humanoid robots
BiCoord: A Bimanual Manipulation Benchmark towards Long-Horizon Spatial-Temporal Coordination
candidates.method_summary: Benchmark for long-horizon bimanual manipulation that emphasizes spatial-temporal coordination rather than only final task success. Provides a useful evaluation axis for dual-hand and dual-arm dexterous manipulation.
limitations: Bimanual benchmark is not tactile-specific
May not include manufacturing maintenance or QA metrics
Long-horizon coordination metrics still need mapping to tactile hand logs

candidates.title: Bimanual Robot Manipulation via Multi-Agent In-Context Learning
candidates.method_summary: Training-free LLM-based bimanual manipulation approach that frames the two arms as leader-follower agents and uses in-context learning, debate, and judging to coordinate actions. Useful for S1's task-decomposition and coordination-reasoning axis.
limitations: Not tactile/in-hand specific
LLM reasoning latency and reliability may not fit fast contact events
Best treated as high-level task decomposition above tactile control

candidates.title: Bioinspired spiking architecture
candidates.method_summary: Bio-inspired spiking neural architecture for tactile encoding modeled after SA-I/SA-II/RA-I/RA-II afferent populations. Combines event-driven sensing front-end with SNN backbone running on neuromorphic hardware (Loihi 2, SpiNNaker, or custom ASIC). Demonstrates >10x energy efficiency over CNN baselines for slip/texture classification while maintaining accuracy.
limitations: Neuromorphic hardware not widely available
SNN training tools immature vs PyTorch ecosystem
Benchmark coverage narrow (slip/texture only)

candidates.title: Bioinspired Spiking Architecture Enables Energy-Constrained Touch Encoding
candidates.method_summary: Bioinspired spiking neural architecture for tactile encoding that mimics mechanoreceptor spike trains. Uses event-driven SNN to compress high-bandwidth tactile streams into sparse spike codes for energy-constrained edge inference. Demonstrates orders-of-magnitude lower energy than dense ANN baselines while preserving texture and slip discrimination accuracy.
limitations: Custom sensor and readout limit reproducibility across labs
Benchmark scope narrow (texture/slip), not full manipulation
SNN training pipeline still relies on surrogate gradients

group.funding: Likely national science foundation / EU Horizon

candidates.title: CaP-X: A Framework for Benchmarking and Improving Coding Agents for Robot Manipulation
candidates.method_summary: Benchmarks and improves coding agents for robot manipulation through CaP-Gym, CaP-Bench, CaP-Agent0, and CaP-RL; useful for symbolic/code scaffolds around VLA policies.
limitations: Requires full-paper/table-level verification before release.

candidates.title: CRAFT: A Tendon-Driven Hand with Hybrid Hard-Soft Compliance
candidates.method_summary: Proposes CRAFT, a tendon-driven anthropomorphic hand combining rigid skeletal structure with soft compliant skin layers. Targets robust contact-rich manipulation by absorbing impact while maintaining position control. Demonstrates grasping of fragile and irregular objects with reduced sensor noise compared to fully rigid hands.
limitations: Bandwidth limitations of tendon transmission
Long-term wear of soft skin under repeated contact
Limited fingertip force precision compared to rigid hands

[Deployment-Time Reliability of Learned Robot Policies](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.11400). arXiv:2603.11400

candidates.method_summary: 2.4 Proposed Approach: Sentinel The failure behavior of a generative policy by OOD conditions can be highly diverse, and we therefore argue that the desiderata for a failure detector may vary between qualitative types of failures. Accordingly, we propose to split the failure detection task into two complementary failure categories. The first is the detection of failures resulting from erratic policy behavior, which may cause a robot to end up in states that are difficult or costly to reset from, knock over objects, or lead to safety hazards. Therefore, it is important to detect erratic behavior as quickly as possible ( Section ˜ 2.4.1 ). The second category is the detection of failures in which the policy struggles to make progress on its task (hereafter referred to as task progression failures ) but does so in a temporally consistent manner. For example, the policy may confidently place an object in the wrong location. Here, we must observe the robot over a longer period of time to identify that the policy is not making progress towards task completion ( Section ˜ 2.4.2 ). The key insight of our approach is that by defining one failure category as the complement of the other, it becomes trivial to combine failure detectors to form an accurate overall detection pipeline whilst satisfying the requirements of each failure category. Our full pipeline, Sentinel , is shown in Fig. ˜ 2.3 . Model, world-model, or agent output is a bounded proposal above versioned skill APIs, feasibility projection, real-time control, independent evaluation, collision/force gates, protective stop, E-stop, and human authority.
limitations: 2.7 Conclusion In this chapter, we investigate the problem of failure detection for generative robot policies.

We propose Sentinel, a runtime monitor that splits the failure detection task into two categories: 1) Erratic failures, which we detect by measuring the statistical change of a policy’s action distributions over time; 2) task progression failures, where we use Vision-Language Models to assess whether the policy is consistently taking actions that do not solve the task.
Our results highlight the importance of targeting complementary failure categories with specialized detectors.
Primary abstract does not establish the full robot/task/data/adaptation/reset denominator needed for cross-paper comparison.
No default-tabletop real-hardware evidence was established from the inspected abstract.

candidates.title: Feel Robot Feels: Tactile Feedback Array Glove for Dexterous Manipulation
candidates.method_summary: Tactile feedback array glove that conveys robot tactile sensor readings to a human operator's fingers via vibrotactile or pressure-array actuators. Enables operators to perceive robot-side contact distribution during dexterous tele-op. Targets closing the sensory loop missing in vision-only tele-op.
limitations: Spatial resolution of feedback limited by actuator density
Sensory mismatch between robot and human contact perception
Hardware cost/complexity

candidates.title: Figure 03 + Helix 02 — General-Purpose Humanoid System
candidates.method_summary: Figure 03 hardware: 30 DOF body + 5-finger 20-DOF hands, 6 main cameras + two palm cameras, 3 g-resolution fingertip tactile sensing, 2 kW inductive foot charging, 10 Gbps mmWave. BotQ vertically integrated manufacturing. Helix 02 software: S2 (7 B VLM, 7-9 Hz), S1 (visuomotor policy: all sensors in → all joints out), new S0 (10 M params, 1 kHz whole-body learned controller trained in Isaac Lab with 200k+ parallel envs). Reports 4-minute continuous dishwasher load/unload demo with 61 loco-manipulation primitives.
limitations: Non-peer-reviewed corporate announcement

candidates.title: G1 humanoid robot: 5,500+ units shipped
candidates.method_summary: Unitree G1 is a 1.27 m, 35 kg humanoid priced from $16,000 USD that shipped 5,500+ units globally by early 2026, making it the most-deployed humanoid platform in research labs. 23 DOF base configuration extensible to 43 DOF with three-finger Dex3-1 hands. Open-sourced SDK and ROS support drove rapid academic adoption; numerous teleoperation and imitation learning works (e.g., HumanPlus, OmniH2O) use G1 as platform.
limitations: Hand (Dex3-1) has only 7 DOF and limited tactile sensing
Joint torque ratings limit heavy-load manipulation
Not designed for industrial uptime

candidates.title: Habilis-β: On-Device VLA
candidates.method_summary: Habilis-β is a 2026-announced on-device VLA aimed at running fully on a robot's onboard compute (Jetson-class or laptop GPU) at 30+ Hz. It uses aggressive distillation from a larger generalist (pi0-class) teacher and quantization-aware training to fit ~1-3B parameter VLA into edge inference budgets. Targeted at humanoid/dexterous-hand platforms where cloud round-trips are unacceptable, Habilis-β positions itself as the on-device counterpart to cloud-based VLAs.
limitations: Distillation loses some generalist capability
Edge GPU still draws significant power
No tactile-specific architecture
Closed weights; reproducibility limited

candidates.method_summary: Systematic review surveying multimodal fusion strategies (vision, language, proprioception, tactile, audio) in VLA models for manipulation. candidates.title: Perceptive humanoid parkour
ReconVLA: An Uncertainty-Guided and Failure-Aware Vision-Language-Action Framework for Robotic Control
candidates.method_summary: II-A Vision-Language-Action Models and Generalist Robot Policies Early efforts towards generalist controllers combined large pretrained models with robot learning, laying the groundwork for today’s VLA architectures. Gato [ 35 ] was among the first transformer-based agents that could handle multiple modalities, from chatting and image captioning to robotic arm control, within one model. It demonstrated the feasibility of training a single policy across diverse tasks, but its skills were limited (e.g., primarily block-stacking). Around the same time, SayCan [ 1 ] decoupled the language model from a low-level policy, using PaLM [ 7 ] to interpret instructions and a value-based policy to execute actions. While SayCan showed that pretrained language understanding could improve instruction-following, it relied on selecting from fixed motion primitives and imitation-learned skills, which constrained generalization to new tasks. Recent research has moved toward fully integrated VLAs that learn perception, language grounding, and action generation end-to-end. PaLM-E [ 9 ] was a notable milestone in this direction. It augments a large language model (LLM) with embodied vision and control modules, enabling semantic reasoning to transfer into a robot’s policy. Building on such ideas, the Robotic Transformer (RT) series introduced transformer-based policies operating directly from images and text. In particular, RT-2 demonstrated that combining internet-scale vision-language pretraining  Model, world-model, or agent output is a bounded proposal above versioned skill APIs, feasibility projection, real-time control, independent evaluation, collision/force gates, protective stop, E-stop, and human authority.
limitations: VII Conclusion In this work we presented ReconVLA, a unified uncertainty-aware framework for generalist robotic policies that integrates state-level failure detection with action-level uncertainty-guided decision making.
Our approach addresses key limitations of prior VLAs by explicitly modeling and leveraging uncertainty during execution, without requiring architectural changes to the base policy.
The first is a state-based failure detection mechanism that uses Mahalanobis distance monitoring to identify OOD or unstable states.

candidates.method_summary: III Methodology and Evaluation Protocol To study how vision–language–action (VLA) models scale under heterogeneous training data, we build (i) a controlled testbed for generalist architectures and (ii) a rigorous physical evaluation protocol. In this section, we describe the model, the cross-embodiment unification used for physical evaluation, and a bias-resistant testing procedure, as illustrated in Figure 1 . Model, world-model, or agent output is a bounded proposal above versioned skill APIs, feasibility projection, real-time control, independent evaluation, collision/force gates, protective stop, E-stop, and human authority.
limitations: Our results show that simply increasing data scale does not automatically lead to better performance.

candidates.title: See like a Robot: Robot-Centric Pointmaps for Vision-Language-Action Models

aliases.title: [DexTwist](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.12182). arXiv:2605.12182

candidates.method_summary: 3 Method We reduce the observation-to-action frame mismatch by lifting each RGB-D observation into a robot-centric pointmap before policy learning. Alongside the RGB input, the VLA receives a robot-centric pointmap, encoded by a separate pointmap encoder and fused with the RGB visual tokens. We detail the pointmap construction and the fusion mechanism below. Model, world-model, or agent output is a bounded proposal above versioned skill APIs, feasibility projection, real-time control, independent evaluation, collision/force gates, protective stop, E-stop, and human authority.
limitations: 6 Limitations Our study leaves several questions open.
We do not ablate how the pointmap is injected relative to the action expert, nor how it interacts with the pretraining recipe, so the best way to combine the two remains unsettled.

Finally, our camera-variation results focus on changes in camera placement and extrinsics, and do not yet cover changes in the number of cameras or their fields of view, which we leave to future work.

candidates.title: TAG: Teleoperation Accessible Glove
candidates.method_summary: TAG (Teleoperation Accessible Glove) is a low-cost wearable glove from Shanghai AI Lab targeting accessible whole-hand teleoperation for dexterous robot training. It combines IMU/Hall-effect joint sensing with retargeting onto multi-finger hands so non-expert operators can collect demonstration data without exoskeleton rigs. The system emphasizes calibration simplicity and per-task data yield over hardware fidelity, positioning itself as an entry-tier alternative to optical mocap and DexUMI-style fixtures.
limitations: No high-resolution tactile sensing on the glove itself
Retargeting fidelity bounded by IMU drift over long episodes
Scope limited to known commercial hands; no humanoid arm coupling shown
Public benchmarking against DexUMI/AnyTeleop pending

candidates.title: Towards Robotic Dexterous Hand Intelligence: A Survey
candidates.method_summary: Survey of robotic dexterous hand intelligence spanning hand hardware, actuation and transmission, perception, control and learning, datasets, modality design, evaluation, and future directions. Useful as a 2026 backbone for connecting S1's sensor, hand, learning, benchmark, and manufacturing chapters.
limitations: Survey-level synthesis rather than original benchmark
Rapidly evolving 2026 field may outdate modality and evaluation coverage
Manufacturing deployment metrics remain outside the main scope

candidates.title: Visual-Tactile Learning for Dexterous Manipulation
candidates.method_summary: Proposes a visual-tactile representation learning framework that fuses RGB observations with multi-finger tactile contact maps for dexterous manipulation. The approach uses a cross-attention transformer to align visual object geometry with tactile contact patterns, trained on paired demos. Downstream policy distillation produces closed-loop manipulation policies that succeed at fine-grained in-hand tasks where vision-only or tactile-only baselines fail.
limitations: Requires paired visual-tactile data which is expensive to collect at scale

candidates.title: VLAConf: Calibrated Task-Success Confidence for Vision-Language-Action Models
candidates.method_summary: IV-D Prefix Aggregation and Calibration The step-wise anomaly score [formula] is local and uncalibrated, whereas the target quantity is rollout-level success probability. A robot may briefly visit an unusual state and still recover, or it may appear normal early in the rollout and fail only after a later deviation. Confidence should therefore summarize the evidence accumulated across the observed prefix rather than depend on a single step in isolation. We therefore define a trajectory-level aggregated signal [formula] , where [formula] aggregates the step-wise scores up to time [formula] . The most suitable aggregation rule depends on the deployment protocol. In the pre-execution setting, only the initial observation is available, so we set [formula] . In the online setting, we consider simple aggregation rules such as the running mean, [formula] , or the maximum prefix score. This modularity allows the same step-wise model to support both one-shot risk assessment before execution and continuous confidence tracking during rollout. The aggregated signal [formula] is an uncalibrated scalar. To convert it into an interpretable success probability, we perform post-hoc calibration on a small set of outcome-labeled policy rollouts, [formula] (as introduced in Section III ). This is the only stage of VLAConf that consumes outcome labels, requiring only a binary outcome [formula] per rollout rather than dense per-step annotations. We fit a standard Platt scaling model [ 29 ] to map t Model, world-model, or agent output is a bounded proposal above versioned skill APIs, feasibility projection, real-time control, independent evaluation, collision/force gates, protective stop, E-stop, and human authority.
limitations: Experiments on standard LIBERO suites and shifted LIBERO-Pro and LIBERO-Plus benchmarks demonstrate that VLAConf provides more informative task-success probabilities while remaining efficient, and that online evidence can further improve confidence quality during execution.

candidates.title: World Action Models are Zero-shot Policies
candidates.method_summary: 2.1 Vision Language Action Models Utilizing Foundation Models for Robotics. Developing foundation models (Bommasani et al., 2021 ) for physical artificial intelligence has emerged as a significant research frontier. One line of work involves using existing, pre-trained foundation models as “black-box” reasoners to handle high-level task planning. These works usually involve modular systems, where the foundation models generate sequences of instructions, visual traces, or affordances that are subsequently executed by specialized, low-level robotic policies or controllers (Brohan et al., 2023b ; Huang et al., ; Singh et al., 2023 ; Driess et al., 2023 ; Huang et al., 2023 ; Kumar et al., 2026 ) . While this modularity simplifies complex planning and enables stronger generalization (Li et al., 2025b ; Lee et al., 2025 ; Kaelbling and Lozano-Pérez, ) and efficiency (Dreczkowski et al., 2025 ) , it is contingent upon having a pre-existing library of low-level skills and a robust interface to bridge the gap between abstract reasoning and physical execution. Additionally, these decoupled systems face the risk of compounding errors across modules. VLAs. On the other hand, end-to-end models such as Vision-Language-Action models (VLAs) (Brohan et al., 2022 , 2023a ; Kim et al., 2024a ; Zheng et al., 2025a ; Ye et al., 2025 ; Yang et al., 2025b ; Black et al., 2024a ; Bjorck et al., 2025 ; Physical Intelligence, 2025 ; Gemini Robotics Team, 2025 ; Bu et al., 2025 ) , have gained popular Model, world-model, or agent output is a bounded proposal above versioned skill APIs, feasibility projection, real-time control, independent evaluation, collision/force gates, protective stop, E-stop, and human authority.
limitations: 6 Discussion and Future Work Scaling Laws of WAMs.
We leave deep investigation on scaling laws for WAMs as future work.

candidates.title: World-VLA-Loop: Closed-Loop Learning of Video World Model and VLA Policy
candidates.method_summary: Video World Models for Robotics With recent advances in video generation Wan et al. ( 2025 ); Blattmann et al. ( 2023 ) , controllable video-based world models have become increasingly feasible. Unlike standard generative models that rely solely on text prompts, world models incorporate action sequences as conditioning signals. Many works leverage the spatiotemporal priors of large pretrained video models to enable action-conditioned simulation in domains such as gaming Bruce et al. ( 2024 ); Yu et al. ( 2025 ) , autonomous driving Ni et al. ( 2025 ); Hu et al. ( 2024 ) , and robotics Li et al. ( 2025b ); Xiao et al. ( 2025 ); Quevedo et al. ( 2025 ); Ali et al. ( 2025 ); Zhang et al. ( 2025 ); Zhu et al. ( 2025 ); Guo et al. ( 2026 ); Jiang et al. ( 2026 ) . Despite this progress, empirical evidence suggests that current video world models still struggle with precise action following, yielding misalignment between predicted frames and the outcomes implied by the actions. To integrate world models into VLA training, particularly RL post-training, practitioners need not only a visual environment but also a reward signal. Recent work explores several designs. VLA-RFT Li et al. ( 2025b ) employs a visual-similarity-based heuristic reward. World-Env Xiao et al. ( 2025 ) uses a VLM to estimate success from the world model’s visual predictions. WMPO Zhu et al. ( 2025 ) implements the reward model with a video encoder and a linear head. Most such approaches rely on an additional tra Model, world-model, or agent output is a bounded proposal above versioned skill APIs, feasibility projection, real-time control, independent evaluation, collision/force gates, protective stop, E-stop, and human authority.
limitations: Limitations and Future Work : Current autoregressive video models suffer from limited context memory and quality drift, which diminishes performance in long-horizon tasks exceeding 300 frames.

A Comprehensive Review of Tactile Sensing Technologies in Space Robotics
candidates.method_summary: Surveys tactile sensing for space robotics: vacuum, thermal cycling, radiation tolerance, and on-orbit servicing constraints. Covers piezoresistive, capacitive, and vision-based sensors and discusses how their performance degrades in space environments. Reviews on-orbit assembly, sample-handling, and EVA-glove sensorization.
limitations: Niche application; limited overlap with terrestrial dexterous manipulation
Few space-deployed in-flight tactile datasets exist
Author list incomplete

candidates.title: A Modularized Design Approach for GelSight Family of Vision-Based Tactile Sensors
candidates.method_summary: Presents a modular design framework for GelSight-family vision-based tactile sensors: standardized optics, illumination, gel, and housing modules that can be mixed and matched for different form factors (fingertip, finger pad, palm). Provides parametric trade-off curves between resolution, FOV, and thickness, plus open-source CAD/firmware. Aims to lower the engineering barrier for new tactile-sensor variants.
limitations: Modular interfaces add slight thickness vs. fully optimized designs
Doesn't address signal-processing standardization (each module needs own calibration)
Targets vision-based only; not generalizable to capacitive/piezo lines

candidates.title: A Neuromorphic Robotic Electronic Skin with Active Pain and Injury Perception (NRE-skin)
candidates.method_summary: NRE-skin integrates spike-based neuromorphic processing with a flexible e-skin that mimics nociception: above a threshold load or sharp impact, the skin emits 'pain' spikes that trigger withdrawal reflexes. Combines pressure, strain, and temperature transducers with on-skin spiking circuits, demonstrating injury detection (cuts, burns) at biological-like time scales. Shown on a robot arm + skin patch.
limitations: Only demonstrated on flat skin patches, not curved fingers
Active pain framing is bio-inspired metaphor; safety standards orthogonal
Material durability under repeated sharp-object exposure uncharacterized

group.affiliation: City University of Hong Kong (Xinge Yu group)

candidates.title: A Pragmatist Robot: Learning to Plan Tasks by Experiencing the Real World
candidates.method_summary: PragmaBot proposes a closed-loop agentic robot that learns task planning by accumulating real-world experience. Rather than relying on hand-engineered knowledge or simulator priors, the robot attempts tasks, observes outcomes, summarizes them into experience memory using an LLM, and updates a world model that informs subsequent planning. The 'pragmatist' framing emphasizes correction from real failures: each error becomes data for the LLM-supervised update of plan templates and skill priors.
limitations: Requires extended deployment — slow improvement loop
Experience summarization quality bounds learning rate
Risk of catastrophic forgetting or biased memory accumulation not fully addressed
Likely tested on a narrow set of tasks; generalization claims need scrutiny
Author list, venue, and exact numbers need primary verification

candidates.title: A Process-Centric Manipulation Taxonomy for the Organization, Classification and Synthesis of Tactile Robot Skills
candidates.method_summary: Introduces a process-centric taxonomy for tactile robot manipulation skills, organizing skills by physical process (e.g., insertion, sliding, screwing) rather than by object or geometry. Provides a structured vocabulary for skill synthesis and reuse. Demonstrates classification of dozens of contact-rich tasks under the unified taxonomy.
limitations: Taxonomy is descriptive, not prescriptive for control
Granularity choices may exclude finer skills
Empirical validation limited to selected case studies

candidates.canonical_id: title:a reconfigurable gripper inspired by elastic belt for versatile in hand manipulations
candidates.title: A reconfigurable gripper inspired by elastic belt for versatile in-hand manipulations
candidates.method_summary: Proposes a reconfigurable gripper using an elastic belt mechanism to achieve versatile in-hand manipulation. Belt geometry can be reconfigured to grasp different shapes and to actively transport objects within the grasp. Targets a single mechanism replacing multiple specialized end-effectors.
limitations: Bibliographic info incomplete (venue/DOI/url missing)
Limited primary verification possible
Elastic belt durability concerns under repeated cycles

candidates.title: A Survey of Behavior Foundation Model: Next-Generation Whole-Body Control System of Humanoid Robots
candidates.method_summary: Survey of behavior foundation models (BFMs) for humanoid whole-body control. Categorizes pre-training pipelines, task specifications, and downstream adaptation. Overlaps Ch 6 canon + Ch 9 3-layer architecture + Ch 10 VLA. Strong on technical taxonomy of learned primitives; does not address orthodox-stack comparison or frontier-company analyses.
limitations: Pre-training/adaptation focus — commercial deployment coverage light
Does not contextualize against classical ZMP/LIPM baseline
Korean/analyst-report content absent

group.affiliation: Multi-institution Chinese consortium (first author Mingqi Yuan; senior authors include Hua Chen, Xin Jin)

candidates.title: A Survey on Large Language Models for Automated Planning
candidates.method_summary: A 2025 survey covering the role of LLMs in automated planning, organized along axes of (1) LLMs as direct planners, (2) LLMs as translators to formal languages (PDDL, STL), (3) LLMs as planning heuristics or verifiers, and (4) LLMs integrated with classical search. The survey catalogs benchmark suites (Blocksworld, Robotouille, ALFWorld, etc.), failure modes (hallucination, long-horizon decay, grounding), and trends from 2022–2025 in robotics and general AI planning.
limitations: Survey scope is broad — robotics-specific depth is moderate compared with planning AI
2025 cutoff already missing latest 2025–2026 VLA-as-planner work
Limited treatment of failure-recovery and embodiment grounding
Largely text-domain benchmarks; physical sim-to-real planning issues underrepresented
No standardized comparison protocol across surveyed methods

candidates.canonical_id: title:abb ams automotive manufacturing outlook survey 2025
candidates.title: ABB-AMS Automotive Manufacturing Outlook Survey 2025
ACT-1: A Robot Foundation Model
candidates.method_summary: ACT-1 is a robot foundation model announced by Sunday Robotics targeting general-purpose manipulation and household assistance. The model is positioned in the same lineage as RT-2 / pi0 / Helix — a vision-language-action transformer trained on a mix of human egocentric video, internet web data, and proprietary robot teleoperation. Public materials describe a humanoid form factor with bimanual dexterous hands and emphasize 'always-learning' deployment. Specific architectural and dataset details are not yet publicly disclosed.
limitations: No peer-reviewed publication or technical report at announcement
Architecture, training data, and evaluation undisclosed
Demos are curated and not reproducible by third parties
Generalization scope and failure-mode analysis not provided
Industry context — tied to commercial product roadmap, not academic benchmarks

candidates.canonical_id: title:act 1 humanoid hand for human level manipulation
candidates.title: ACT-1: Humanoid Hand for Human-Level Manipulation
candidates.method_summary: Sunday Robotics' ACT-1 hand is an industry announcement of a 5-finger humanoid hand designed for dexterous teleoperation and imitation learning. The hand is reported to have ~22 DoF with tendon-driven actuation and integrated tactile sensing. Targeted at supporting Sunday Robotics' household humanoid platform; details remain proprietary.
limitations: Industry announcement; no peer-reviewed publication
Hardware specs and tactile coverage not publicly documented
Bibtex_key null — needs canonical reference assignment
No reproducible baseline

candidates.title: Advancing Humanoid Locomotion: Mastering Challenging Terrains with Denoising World Model Learning
candidates.method_summary: Denoising World Model Learning (DWML) — end-to-end RL framework for humanoid locomotion that combines a learned dynamics world model with a denoising objective. All terrain scenarios run the same learned neural network with zero-shot sim-to-real transfer. Demonstrated as 'the world's first humanoid robot to master real-world challenging terrains including snowy and inclined land in the wild, up and down stairs, and extremely uneven terrains' per primary source. Same first-author (Xinyang Gu) as gu2024humanoidgym (Humanoid-Gym paper) — consistent Tsinghua + RobotEra author line.
limitations: World-model compute overhead
Denoising objective requires careful noise schedule
Still proprioception-dominant

candidates.method_summary: Presents AgiBot World dataset + ViLLA (Vision-Language-Latent-Action) hierarchical policy. 100 dual-arm humanoids collect 1M+ trajectories over 217 tasks across 5 deployment scenarios (home, restaurant, retail, office, industrial). Hardware: 7-DoF arms, 6-DoF dexterous hands, visuo-tactile sensors. ViLLA predicts latent action tokens (pretrained on Ego4D-style human video) that condition downstream robot-control generation. Reports 32% success-rate gain over RDT baseline.
limitations: Data collection still human-teleop-bottlenecked
Latent action supervision from Ego4D is noisy
Policies not released openly

candidates.title: AirExo-2: In-the-Wild Data Collection for Robot Learning
candidates.method_summary: AirExo-2 is the second-generation SJTU exoskeleton, upgrading AirExo with (a) per-finger gripping for parallel-and-three-finger configurations, (b) integrated wrist-mounted RGB-D + microphone, (c) better encoder accuracy and lighter mass, and (d) a software stack for in-the-wild scene calibration. The paper demonstrates that ~100 hours of in-the-wild AirExo-2 demos train BC and diffusion policies that deploy on the matched bimanual robot with substantial generalization gains over lab-only teleop. Awarded Oral at CoRL 2025.
limitations: Still parallel-jaw / 3-finger only — no full dexterous hand
No tactile, only audio + vision
Calibration drift over multi-day collection
Operator fatigue persists
RGB-D sensor adds weight at the wrist

group.affiliation: Shanghai Jiao Tong University (Cewu Lu lab)

candidates.canonical_id: title:airexo 2 scaling up generalizable manipulation skills via purely kinesthetic demonstrations in the wild
candidates.title: AirExo-2: Scaling up generalizable manipulation skills via purely kinesthetic demonstrations in the wild
candidates.method_summary: Successor to AirExo that scales kinesthetic in-the-wild demonstration collection to thousands of trajectories across many scenes and object categories. Introduces a unified policy backbone (transformer-based) trained purely on exoskeleton-recorded joint trajectories and visual context, then deployed zero-shot to a matching robot arm. Demonstrates scaling-law-style improvements with data volume and shows generalization to novel scenes and objects without robot-side teleoperation.
limitations: Still arm-only, no dexterous fingers or tactile
Camera viewpoint mismatch between human-worn rig and robot-mounted camera limits transfer
Requires per-robot exoskeleton design

group.lead_author: Hongjie Fang et al. (Cewu Lu group)

candidates.canonical_id: title:allegro hand research standard dexterous hand with digit plexus integration
candidates.title: Allegro Hand: Research-standard dexterous hand with Digit Plexus integration
candidates.method_summary: Wonik Allegro Hand v4 is a 16-DOF four-finger anthropomorphic research hand widely cited in dexterous manipulation literature (DeXtreme, In-Hand Manipulation, etc.). 2025 announcement integrates Meta Digit Plexus tactile system across fingertips, enabling unified tactile-proprioceptive feedback for imitation/RL pipelines. Continues as de facto standard alongside Shadow Hand for academic dexterous manipulation.
limitations: Tendon-driven backlash limits high-frequency control
No palm tactile coverage even with Plexus integration
Heavy and bulky vs human hand

candidates.canonical_id: title:apparel manufacturing automation a systematic review 2025
candidates.title: Apparel Manufacturing Automation: A Systematic Review 2025
candidates.method_summary: 의류 제조 자동화의 최신 동향을 체계적으로 리뷰한 논문. 봉제 자동화, 패턴 재단, 품질 검사에서의 AI·로봇 적용 사례와 기술적 장벽을 분석한다. 변형 소재 조작, 다양한 패브릭 처리, 소량 다품종 생산이 주요 해결 과제로 지목된다.
limitations: 의류 산업의 다양성으로 단일 솔루션 어려움

candidates.title: ASAP: Aligning Simulation and Real-World Physics for Learning Agile Humanoid Whole-Body Skills
candidates.method_summary: Two-stage real-to-sim-to-real framework. Stage 1: pretrain a motion-tracking policy in sim on retargeted human mocap. Stage 2: collect ~20 minutes of real-robot rollouts, train a 'delta action' residual model that maps sim state + sim action → real-world correction, then fine-tune the policy in sim augmented with the delta action model. Evaluates IsaacGym→IsaacSim, IsaacGym→Genesis, and IsaacGym→real Unitree G1 transfers. Enables agile whole-body skills (Kungfu kicks, jumping) not reachable with SysID or DR alone.
limitations: Requires real-world data collection stage
Delta model is task-agnostic but embodiment-specific
Only upper-body-dominant agile motions reported — manipulation not covered

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Identifier-deduplicated source linked in the annotated research trail; no new quantitative claim is made.

candidates.title: Audi Body Shop Weld Inspection AI with Siemens Industrial AI Suite
candidates.method_summary: Audi의 차체 공장에서 일일 5백만 회 용접점 검사를 AI로 자동화하고 Siemens Industrial AI Suite에 통합한 사례. 25배 더 빠른 inference를 shop floor에서 직접 수행해 결함을 즉시 처리. Siemens Inspekto 시각 품질 검사 시스템(20개 샘플로 1시간 미만 학습 가능)과 함께 NVIDIA의 산업 AI 비전을 보여준다. Edge inference로 클라우드 의존 없이 실시간 품질 관리 달성.

candidates.method_summary: Open-source RL framework from Tsinghua + Booster Robotics for humanoid locomotion. candidates.canonical_id: title:canonical 3d tactile for visuo tactile imitation learning
candidates.title: Canonical 3D tactile for visuo-tactile imitation learning
candidates.method_summary: Proposes a canonical 3D tactile representation (point cloud or voxel field of contact points and forces in a frame attached to the object) that is invariant to specific sensor placement on the hand. Trains visuo-tactile imitation policies on this canonical representation, allowing demonstrations collected with one finger layout to transfer to others. Shows improvements on contact-rich tasks vs raw per-sensor tactile features.
limitations: Requires accurate sensor pose calibration to construct canonical frame
Tested on a small set of contact-rich tasks
Computational cost of constructing 3D representation per timestep

candidates.canonical_id: title:canonical 3d tactile representation for visuo tactile imitation learning
candidates.title: Canonical 3D tactile representation for visuo-tactile imitation learning
candidates.method_summary: Proposes a canonical 3D tactile representation that lifts tactile signals into a hand-frame point cloud, unifying tactile and visual point clouds for imitation learning. Allows visuo-tactile diffusion or behavior-cloning policies to consume tactile geometry directly. Evaluated on contact-rich manipulation tasks.
limitations: Conversion from raw tactile to 3D requires sensor-specific calibration
Bibliographic data incomplete (no venue/arxiv ID)
Performance depends on quality of hand kinematic model

candidates.canonical_id: title:capacitive sensor for lift risk identification
candidates.title: Capacitive sensor for lift-risk identification
candidates.method_summary: Capacitive sensor for identifying lift-risk events during grasping (e.g., slip, insufficient grip force). Uses capacitance changes near the contact surface to detect impending object loss. Targets safety-critical grasping where slip detection latency matters.
limitations: Bibliographic info incomplete
Capacitive signals affected by humidity / dielectric variation
Application limited to lift-risk context

candidates.canonical_id: title:capacitive tactile sensing for teaching by demonstration
candidates.title: Capacitive tactile sensing for teaching by demonstration
candidates.method_summary: Murphy et al. use a capacitive tactile array integrated into a teach-by-demonstration interface so that a human can record both kinematic and contact-pressure intent during physical guidance. The captured contact maps are used to ground learned policies in pressure targets rather than only joint targets. Targets industrial assembly transfer tasks.
limitations: Metadata incomplete; bibtex/doi unverified
Capacitive sensing is drift-prone over long sessions
Limited spatial resolution constrains fine-grained contact decoding

candidates.canonical_id: title:catch 919 hand a 9 actuator 19 dof anthropomorphic robotic hand
candidates.title: CATCH-919 Hand: A 9-actuator 19-DOF anthropomorphic robotic hand
candidates.method_summary: CATCH-919 is an anthropomorphic robotic hand with 19 DOF driven by 9 actuators using a coupled-tendon underactuation scheme. Aims to match human hand kinematics while reducing actuator count via mechanical coupling. Targets human-scale grasp variety with affordable BOM.
limitations: Bibliographic detail missing (venue/DOI not captured)
Coupling reduces independent finger control
Tendon underactuation limits force precision

candidates.title: Code-as-Symbolic-Planner: Foundation Model-Based Robot Planning via Symbolic Code Generation
candidates.method_summary: Code-as-Symbolic-Planner asks an LLM to generate symbolic search code (in Python) that solves a structured planning problem rather than directly emit an action sequence. The generated program builds a state-space search using primitive operators (e.g., move, pick, drop) and runs it; the resulting plan is then mapped back to robot actions. By offloading combinatorial search from the LLM to executable code, the method retains LLM strengths in problem decomposition while inheriting the soundness/completeness of classical search.
limitations: Domain operators must be provided as a Python primitive library
LLM code generation can produce non-terminating searches on poorly specified problems
Limited dexterous / contact-rich robotics evaluation; mostly discrete planning
Heavy reliance on GPT-4-class LLMs
No closed-loop integration with perception — assumes ground-truth state

candidates.title: DexForce: Extracting Force-informed Actions from Kinesthetic Demonstrations for Dexterous Manipulation

aliases.title: [DexForce: Extracting Force-Informed Actions from Kinesthetic Demonstrations](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.10356). arXiv:2501.10356

candidates.method_summary: DexForce augments kinesthetic teaching for dexterous hands by recording fingertip force/torque alongside joint trajectories, then converting raw kinesthetic motions into force-informed action targets. The resulting demonstrations train imitation policies that retain the implicit force intent of the human teacher (e.g., 'press firmly here, lightly there'). Shows large gains over position-only behavior cloning on contact-rich tasks.
limitations: Requires fingertip F/T sensing during demonstration, not always available
Kinesthetic teaching is slow and limits demo throughput
Force decoding assumes quasi-static contact; fast dynamic tasks underexplored

candidates.title: Dexterous Manipulation through Imitation Learning: A Survey
candidates.method_summary: Comprehensive survey of imitation learning methods for dexterous manipulation, organizing the literature along data-collection (teleop, kinesthetic, video, exoskeletons), policy-architecture (BC, ACT, diffusion policy, VLA), and embodiment-transfer axes. Compares benchmarks, discusses tactile integration, and highlights scaling trends from ALOHA-style systems to humanoid bimanual platforms. Identifies open problems in cross-embodiment transfer, tactile demonstration, and long-horizon evaluation.
limitations: Imitation-learning focus excludes pure RL and model-based methods
Evaluation across papers inconsistent
Industrial deployment underrepresented

candidates.title: DexWM: Dexterous World Models from Human and Robot Data
candidates.method_summary: DexWM trains a dexterous-manipulation world model jointly on human hand-object interaction videos and robot demonstration data. The world model predicts future tactile and visual observations conditioned on action; planning rolls out candidate trajectories for selection. Mixing human and robot data is reported to improve generalization to novel objects vs robot-only training.
limitations: World model rollouts compound errors over long horizons
Human-robot data mixing requires paired or aligned tasks
Tactile prediction quality limited by sensor abstraction

candidates.title: Diffusion Models for Robotic Manipulation: A Survey
candidates.method_summary: Survey of diffusion-based policies for manipulation, covering Diffusion Policy, 3D Diffuser Actor, Octo, and recent flow-matching variants. Compares score-based, DDPM, DDIM, and flow-matching formulations on benchmarks. Discusses inference acceleration (consistency models, 1-step distillation) and contact-rich extensions.

Limited theoretical guarantees on safety/multi-modal action coverage
Tactile-conditioned diffusion barely covered

candidates.canonical_id: title:digit deployment at amazon fulfillment centers
candidates.title: Digit deployment at Amazon fulfillment centers
candidates.method_summary: Agility Robotics' Digit bipedal humanoid was deployed at Amazon fulfillment centers for tote handling and recycling tasks starting late 2024 and expanded through 2025. Digit uses a four-finger end-effector (not full dexterous hand) with mechanical compliance for grasping standardized totes. Multi-shift operations reported with battery-swap workflows. Marks first commercial humanoid pilot in large-scale logistics.
limitations: Gripper is non-dexterous (no fingered hand) — limits to standardized tote handling
Operates in segregated work cells, not free human-robot collaboration
No public per-task throughput vs human baseline disclosed

candidates.canonical_id: title:doglove dexterous manipulation with a low cost open source glove
candidates.title: DOGlove: Dexterous Manipulation with a Low-Cost Open-Source Glove
candidates.method_summary: DOGlove is a low-cost open-source data glove for dexterous teleoperation and demonstration collection. Uses IMU + flex sensors at each finger joint and tactile pads at fingertips, totaling ~$200-500 BoM. The glove streams 22+ DoF hand pose plus tactile contact at >100 Hz, enabling demo collection that maps to multi-finger robot hands like Allegro/LEAP. Open-source design includes 3D-printed parts, custom PCBs, and ROS drivers.
limitations: Bibtex_key null — needs canonical reference
IMU drift over long sessions requires recalibration
Tactile pads at fingertips only, not whole hand
Open-source quality varies with maker skill

Egocentric-10K
Egocentric-10K: 10,000 Hours of Factory Egocentric Video
candidates.method_summary: Egocentric-10K is a corpus released by BuildAI (industry) consisting of approximately 10,000 hours of egocentric video collected from factory and warehouse workers performing assembly, sorting, and inspection tasks. Compared to Ego4D's daily-life bias, this dataset emphasizes industrial manipulation skills with structured procedural content. It is positioned as pretraining fuel for industrial humanoid and manipulator policies. Annotations include action segmentation, object labels, and (partial) hand-pose estimates from foundation-model post-processing.
limitations: Industrial bias — skills may not transfer to home / general domains
Annotation quality is partially automatic and noisy
Privacy and labor-rights concerns in worker-collected data
No tactile / force / depth — vision only
License terms may restrict academic use

candidates.title: Egocentric-10K: Factory Egocentric Video Dataset
candidates.method_summary: Duplicate / sister entry to the Egocentric-10K release: an industry corpus of ~10,000 hours of factory-worker egocentric video by BuildAI, intended as pretraining fuel for industrial humanoid and manipulator policies. Annotations are partial and procedural (action segmentation, object labels, partial hand pose). Positioned as a domain-specific complement to Ego4D's daily-life bias.
limitations: Industrial-only domain bias
Annotation noise from automatic pipelines
Privacy / labor-rights concerns
No tactile / force / depth modalities
Possible commercial license restrictions

candidates.title: Egocentric2Embodiment Pipeline
candidates.method_summary: Egocentric2Embodiment from PhysBrain is a pipeline that converts raw egocentric human video into deployable robot policies for diverse embodiments (single-arm, bimanual, humanoid). The pipeline performs hand-object reconstruction, retargets human MANO hand to the target robot's morphology, and trains a unified VLA that conditions on embodiment ID. Marketed as an end-to-end alternative to per-task real-to-sim-to-real pipelines like X-Sim and Human2Sim2Robot.
limitations: Retargeting quality drops for highly anthropomorphic dexterous tasks
Embodiment ID conditioning is a coarse proxy for full embodiment-aware learning
No tactile / force in the loop
Industrial / commercial details limit reproducibility
Long-horizon and deformable tasks underperform

candidates.title: EgoDex: A Large-Scale Egocentric Dexterous Manipulation Dataset
candidates.method_summary: EgoDex is a large-scale dataset of egocentric dexterous manipulation captured with Apple Vision Pro (or similar XR headset) plus high-quality hand tracking. The dataset contains hundreds of hours of paired (RGB stereo, depth, IMU, MANO hand pose) data across diverse manipulation skills emphasizing fine finger motion: typing, instrument play, cooking prep, assembly. The authors release benchmarks for hand-pose recovery and policy pretraining and show that EgoDex pretraining yields large downstream gains on a dexterous robot hand vs. Ego4D-only pretraining.
limitations: Apple Vision Pro hand tracking is occlusion-prone for object-in-hand scenarios
Dataset is biased toward able-bodied operators and indoor settings
No tactile, no force — fine-grained contact dynamics are inferred not measured
Stereo + IMU does not give full 6D object pose; some tasks require additional annotation
Privacy of XR-collected data is a concern at scale

candidates.title: EgoVLA: Egocentric human video pretraining for robot VLA

The egocentric pretraining provides a strong manipulation prior, improving sample efficiency on robot fine-tuning by 2-3x.
limitations: Hand pose estimation noise propagates to pretraining

candidates.title: EgoVLA: Egocentric Vision-Language-Action Model
candidates.method_summary: EgoVLA is a vision-language-action model pretrained on large-scale egocentric (first-person) human video datasets like Ego4D and EgoExo, then adapted to robot control via a small fine-tuning step. By treating egocentric video as a proxy for robot first-person observations, the model bypasses the need for massive robot data collection. Action targets are derived from estimated hand poses; downstream evaluation tests transfer to robot manipulation tasks.
limitations: Egocentric video provides only visual signal — no force or tactile
Hand-pose estimation noise propagates to action targets
Embodiment gap between human first-person and robot wrist camera remains

candidates.title: EgoVLA: Egocentric VLA with MANO
candidates.method_summary: EgoVLA learns a vision-language-action policy directly from large-scale egocentric human videos by lifting hand pose into MANO parameters and treating MANO trajectories as a unified action representation across humans and robot hands. A pretrained VLA backbone is finetuned on Ego4D/EgoExo-style data, then retargeted via per-embodiment MANO-to-robot mappings (e.g., LEAP, Allegro, Inspire). The approach demonstrates positive transfer from human-only data to multiple robot hands without robot teleoperation collection.
limitations: MANO retargeting lossy for high-DOF embodiments
No tactile feedback in pipeline
Domain gap between egocentric videos and tabletop robot scenes
Limited evaluation on contact-rich tasks (insertion, fastening)

candidates.canonical_id: title:egozero robot learning from smart glasses demonstrations
candidates.title: EgoZero: Robot learning from smart glasses demonstrations
candidates.method_summary: EgoZero collects human manipulation demos via smart glasses (Aria, Vision Pro, Meta Ray-Ban) without robot teleop, then trains robot policies via sim-based retargeting. Uses 3D hand tracking + scene mesh reconstruction to convert ego-video to robot-executable trajectories. Reports ~50-70% real-robot success on novel tasks demonstrated only via smart glasses.
limitations: Hand tracking accuracy limits in cluttered scenes
Embodiment retargeting (5→4 fingers) introduces errors
Tactile/contact information absent in glasses-only data

group.affiliation: Unverified (Wang et al.; Meta or NYU likely)

candidates.title: EgoZero: Robot Policy Learning from Egocentric Video without Robot Data
candidates.method_summary: EgoZero learns deployable robot manipulation policies entirely from egocentric video, using zero robot teleoperation. The pipeline lifts hand-pose and 6D object pose from each frame, defines a 'unified action space' (object-centric end-effector motion + hand aperture) shared between human and robot, and trains a transformer policy with both action prediction and inverse-dynamics auxiliary losses. Deployment on a parallel-jaw robot uses the predicted unified action and a small calibration step. The method is positioned as an ablation point: how far can human-only data go before any robot data is needed?
limitations: Object-centric action space assumes good 6D pose estimation, fails on textureless/symmetric items
No tactile or force signal — contact-rich tasks (insertion, deformable) underperform
Calibration step at deploy time is non-trivial; not fully zero-shot in practice
Tested on parallel-jaw only; dexterous transfer untested
Egocentric capture device adds cost and requires user instrumentation

candidates.title: Electric Atlas humanoid robot
candidates.method_summary: Boston Dynamics retired the hydraulic Atlas in April 2024 and unveiled the all-electric Atlas with continuous-rotation joints, custom 3-finger gripper variants, and Hyundai Motor Group factory pilots. The robot demonstrates auto-parts manipulation (struts, sequencer pegs) using a hybrid model-based + learned control stack. Public demos showcase whole-body manipulation including object regrasping with non-anthropomorphic joint configurations.
limitations: Gripper is task-specific (not general dexterous hand)
No public benchmark or quantitative success rate published
Operates in supervised pilot, not autonomous production

candidates.title: Electric Atlas reinforcement learning pipeline
candidates.method_summary: S10 refresh import: Electric Atlas reinforcement learning pipeline is included as evidence for large-data driven manipulation, contact-rich control, robot data collection, tactile/force sensing, policy learning, or manufacturing deployment strategy.

candidates.title: Embedding high-resolution touch across robotic hands enables adaptive human-like grasping
candidates.method_summary: F-TAC Hand embeds high-resolution vision-based tactile sensors across all phalanges and palm of an anthropomorphic hand (covering ~70% of contact area, ~0.1mm resolution). A reactive grasping policy uses dense touch maps to adapt grip on novel objects, switching between human-like power and precision grasps. Demonstrates large gains in success rate on cluttered, deformable, and slippery objects vs. tactile-sparse baselines.
limitations: Custom hand; not directly portable to commercial Allegro/LEAP/Inspire hands
Vision-based tactile requires bulky LED + camera packaging that constrains finger thickness
Reactive policy is hand-tuned, not learned end-to-end

candidates.title: EquiTac: SO(2)-Equivariant Tactile Policy
candidates.method_summary: EquiTac (Rong et al., 2025) introduces an SO(2)-equivariant tactile policy network that respects rotational symmetry of contact patches around the gripper/finger axis. By baking in equivariance via steerable CNNs over tactile images (GelSight-style), the policy generalizes across object rotations with far less data than non-equivariant baselines. Evaluated on insertion, screwing, and rotational alignment tasks where contact-pattern rotation symmetry is intrinsic to the task structure.
limitations: SO(2) only — does not capture full SE(3) symmetry
Steerable networks add architecture complexity
Specific to vision-tactile inputs
Real-world rotation labels can be noisy

EquiTac: Tactile-Guided Manipulation with Equivariant Orientation Correction
candidates.method_summary: EquiTac uses SO(2)-equivariant networks on tactile images to correct in-hand object orientation during manipulation. By exploiting rotational symmetry of tactile contact patterns, the policy generalizes orientation correction with limited tactile data. Demonstrated on USB plug insertion and similar precision tasks where orientation alignment dominates success.
limitations: SO(2) equivariance only; doesn't handle full 3D orientation
Restricted to parallel-jaw + vision-based tactile
Tasks limited to small rigid objects with clear contact symmetry

candidates.title: Evaluating Real-World Robot Manipulation Policies in Simulation

aliases.title: [Evaluating Real-World Robot Manipulation Policies in Simulation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.05941). arXiv
PhysGraph: Physically-Grounded Graph-Transformer Policies for Bimanual Dexterous Hand-Tool-Object Manipulation
[Evaluating Real-World Robot Manipulation Policies in Simulation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.05941)
[Evaluating Real-World Robot Manipulation Policies in Simulation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.05941). arXiv:2405.05941v1
[Evaluating Real-World Robot Manipulation Policies in Simulation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.05941). arXiv:2405.05941

candidates.method_summary: SIMPLER is a simulation evaluation suite for real-world manipulation policies. It carefully matches camera intrinsics, lighting, and physics to two real platforms — Google Robot and WidowX BridgeData V2 — and provides a curated set of tasks (pick coke can, move near, open/close drawer, etc.) where ground-truth real-world success rates have been measured for multiple policies. The key contribution is a measurement / matching protocol: by tuning visual and physical fidelity to maximize correlation with real-world numbers (Pearson and ranking), SIMPLER lets researchers compare policies (RT-1, RT-1-X, RT-2-X, Octo, OpenVLA) without expensive physical evaluation, while honestly reporting the residual sim-to-real gap.
limitations: Only two real platforms covered; new embodiments require careful re-tuning to retain correlation
Visual matching uses photorealistic rendering but cannot capture deformable objects, complex contact, or human bystanders
Tasks remain relatively short-horizon tabletop manipulation; SIMPLER is not yet validated for long-horizon mobile or bimanual tasks
Physics matching is heuristic: friction and contact dynamics are hand-tuned per task
Sim-real correlation is reported on policies trained on the same datasets that defined the matching; novel policies may exhibit systematic offsets

candidates.title: FALCON: Learning Force-Adaptive Humanoid Loco-Manipulation
candidates.method_summary: Dual-agent RL framework for humanoid loco-manipulation under external end-effector forces. Lower-body agent handles locomotion under disturbance; upper-body agent tracks end-effector pose with implicit force compensation. Jointly trained with a force curriculum that escalates EE force while respecting torque limits. Deployed on multiple humanoids.
limitations: Two-agent decoupling — not fully whole-body
Force sensing implicit via model
No contact-rich bimanual manipulation

candidates.title: FAST: Efficient Action Tokenization for Vision-Language-Action Models

aliases.title: [FAST: Efficient Action Tokenization for Vision-Language-Action Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.09747). arXiv

candidates.method_summary: FAST proposes a frequency-domain action tokenizer for VLAs. Instead of bin-discretizing each action dimension independently (as RT-2/OpenVLA), FAST applies a Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT) to chunks of continuous actions, then quantizes the resulting frequency coefficients with a learned codebook. This produces sequences ~5x shorter while preserving smoothness, drastically reducing autoregressive decoding cost and improving fit to high-frequency dexterous tasks. FAST is plug-compatible with existing VLA architectures.
limitations: DCT codebook requires task-specific frequency budget tuning
Improvements diminish on slow / quasi-static tasks where per-dim binning already suffices
Codebook reconstruction error introduces a new failure mode for very fine motions
Most evaluation on PI/Berkeley platforms; broader generalization yet to be confirmed
Increased training pipeline complexity vs simple binning

candidates.title: FastTD3: Simple, Fast, and Capable Reinforcement Learning for Humanoid Control

limitations: Hyperparameter tuning still sensitive
No real-robot transfer demonstration in the paper
Distributional critic adds memory

candidates.title: Feel the Force: Contact-Driven Learning from Humans
candidates.method_summary: Feel the Force learns robot manipulation policies from human demonstrations annotated with contact forces estimated from vision and biomechanical priors. Distills human force profiles into robot policies via behavior cloning with force-conditioned trajectories. Shows that force annotation transfers contact-rich skills (peeling, scraping, pushing) better than pose-only imitation. Bridges human-only data and force-aware robot control.
limitations: Force estimates from vision are noisy
Limited to short-horizon contact tasks
Robot embodiment differs from human hand

candidates.title: Fine-Tuning Vision-Language-Action Models: Optimizing Speed and Success
candidates.method_summary: V-D Model Input-Output Flexibility As explained in Section IV-B and validated by our efficiency evaluations in the prior section, parallel decoding enables OpenVLA to generate action chunks with minimal latency increase, thereby enhancing flexibility in model outputs . The significant speedup realized by parallel decoding and action chunking creates headroom for processing additional model inputs as well. We demonstrate this by fine-tuning OpenVLA with additional inputs such as robot proprioceptive state and a robot wrist-mounted camera image, which doubles the number of visual patch embeddings being passed into the language model decoder, from 256 to 512. Despite this substantial increase in input sequence length, the fine-tuned OpenVLA policy maintains high throughput (71.4 Hz) and low latency (0.112 sec), as shown in Table II . Evaluating these policies with additional inputs on the LIBERO benchmark reveals further improvements in average success rate across all task suites (Table I ). Notably, our enhanced fine-tuned OpenVLA policies outperform even the best fine-tuned [formula] policies [ 3 , 38 ] —which benefit from a base model with larger-scale pretraining and a more sophisticated learning objective (flow matching [ 25 ] )—as well as Multimodal Diffusion Transformer (MDT) [ 40 ] and Seer [ 50 ] policies. Even with a simpler base model pretrained on less data than more recent VLAs, we find that our alternative VLA adaptation design decisions empower fine-tuned OpenVLA  Model, world-model, or agent output is a bounded proposal above versioned skill APIs, feasibility projection, real-time control, independent evaluation, collision/force gates, protective stop, E-stop, and human authority.

candidates.method_summary: 2.2 Action-scaled Noise Truncation In conventional text-to-video generation, models are expected to balance fidelity and diversity. In contrast, in the current task of trajectory-to-video, the goal is a single faithful realization of the given specific action trajectory [formula] . We therefore propose to regulate the diversity of generated videos by manipulating the initial Gaussian latent [formula] , via action-scaled noise truncation, in the spirit of BigGAN’s truncation trick [ 2 ] . This truncation constrains sampling to high-density regions, yielding higher fidelity while sacrificing diversity - a trade-off that is desirable for trajectory-to-video. candidates.title: From Experts to a Generalist: Toward General Whole-Body Control for Humanoid Robots
candidates.method_summary: Introduces the BumbleBee (BB) expert-generalist framework for humanoid whole-body control. Addresses the challenge that general agile whole-body control struggles across highly varied behaviors due to conflicting control requirements and mismatched data distributions. Uses an autoencoder-based clustering method to group similar motions into clusters; trains expert policies within each cluster, refined with real-world data; then distills these experts into a unified generalist controller. Combines motion clustering with sim-to-real adaptation in a single pipeline.
limitations: Relies on per-cluster experts already trained
Distillation may drop edge-case skills across cluster boundaries
Manipulation not covered

candidates.title: HAMSTER: Hierarchical Action Models For Open-World Robot Manipulation
candidates.method_summary: HAMSTER is a hierarchical VLA where a VLM predicts a 2D trajectory sketch on the input image (a sequence of waypoints overlaid on the scene) given a task instruction, and a low-level diffusion policy is conditioned on this sketch to produce 3D end-effector actions. The 2D sketch acts as a domain-invariant intermediate representation: it can be supervised cheaply from human videos and existing demonstrations, and the low-level policy learns to follow sketches across embodiments. This decouples high-level reasoning (VLM) from physical execution.
limitations: 2D sketches lose 3D depth information; struggles on high-precision tasks requiring fine-grained depth
Sketch supervision still requires demonstrations or annotators
Diffusion policy on 3D actions inherits standard slow-inference limits
Tested on relatively short-horizon tabletop tasks
Cross-embodiment transfer claims rest on small fine-tune budgets — broader study pending

group.affiliation: NVIDIA + University of Washington (Fox lab) + USC + UC Berkeley

candidates.canonical_id: title:helix a vision language action model for full humanoid control
candidates.title: Helix: A vision-language-action model for full humanoid control
candidates.method_summary: Helix is Figure AI's VLA controlling full humanoid (including 5-finger hands) with a dual-system architecture: System 2 VLM (~7B) at 7-9 Hz for slow semantic reasoning + System 1 transformer (~80M) at 200 Hz for fast continuous control. Demonstrates two Figure 02 humanoids collaborating on kitchen tasks from natural language without per-task demos.
limitations: Closed model and dataset, no published benchmarks
Marketing-style release, limited rigorous evaluation
Tactile/force capabilities of Figure 02 hands not detailed

candidates.title: Helix: A Vision-Language-Action Model for Generalist Humanoid Control
candidates.method_summary: Figure's 'System 1, System 2' VLA controlling the entire humanoid upper body. S2 is an onboard internet-pretrained VLM at 7-9 Hz for scene and language. S1 is a fast reactive visuomotor policy at 200 Hz mapping latent semantics from S2 + visuals + proprioception to continuous joint actions. First VLA to run fully onboard a low-power embedded GPU. Demonstrates two Figure 02 robots collaboratively putting away groceries they have never seen.
limitations: Corporate tech blog — not peer reviewed
Details on data size/training compute not fully disclosed
Locomotion handled by separate controller stack

candidates.title: Hi Robot: Open-Ended Instruction Following with Hierarchical Vision-Language-Action Models
candidates.method_summary: Hi Robot is Physical Intelligence's hierarchical VLA. A high-level VLM receives the open-ended instruction, the scene, and optional human feedback; it emits short language subgoals that are then consumed by pi0/pi0.5 as conditioning. The hierarchical design lets one model handle complex compositional commands ('put away the dishes after the kids have cleared the table'), enables 'thinking before acting' for ambiguous prompts, and accepts mid-task corrections in natural language. The system is trained jointly on demonstration data labeled with sub-instructions.
limitations: Requires hierarchical sub-instruction labels — added annotation cost
Sub-instruction grounding can drift on long horizons
Closed-source weights and proprietary data
High latency from VLM-in-the-loop high-level reasoning
Limited evaluation on truly novel home environments

candidates.title: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.16932
Human-like Dexterous Manipulation for Anthropomorphic Five-Fingered Hands: A Review
candidates.method_summary: Comprehensive review of human-like dexterous manipulation for anthropomorphic five-fingered robotic hands. Surveys mechanical design taxonomies (tendon-driven, linkage, soft, hybrid), actuation choices, sensor integration, and learning approaches across 200+ references. Highlights gap between human ~24-DOF hand and current robot hands typically 16-22 DOF with 6-12 actuators.
limitations: Review-only with no original experiments or benchmarks
Limited coverage of recent learning-based control (post-2024)
Lacks unified quantitative comparison metric across surveyed hands

candidates.title: Human-to-Robot Video Augmentation for Pretraining
candidates.method_summary: Proposes a video-augmentation pipeline that converts large-scale human manipulation videos (Ego4D-class) into pseudo-robot demonstrations by segmenting human hands, replacing them with rendered robot grippers/hands, and re-projecting end-effector trajectories. The augmented dataset serves as pretraining for downstream robot manipulation policies, dramatically increasing data scale without robot collection cost.
limitations: Hand-to-robot rendering introduces visual artifacts that may bias policies
Force/tactile signals absent — purely visual augmentation
Augmentation quality depends on hand segmentation accuracy

candidates.title: Human2Bot: Task similarity reward from human video for zero-shot robot control

candidates.title: Human2Bot: Zero-Shot Robot Learning from Human Videos
candidates.method_summary: Human2Bot trains a manipulation policy directly from human demonstration videos, using a hand-pose extraction pipeline and embodiment-agnostic action representation to enable zero-shot deployment on a robot arm. The system retargets human hand trajectories to robot end-effector trajectories via inverse kinematics and optical flow alignment. Demonstrates zero-shot success on pick-and-place and pouring tasks without any robot data, though success rates are modest compared to robot-trained baselines.

candidates.title: Human2Sim2Robot
candidates.method_summary: Human2Sim2Robot (Lum et al.) is a three-stage pipeline that lifts human hand demonstrations into simulation, finetunes a dexterous policy with RL in sim, then deploys to a real robot hand. The key insight is that the simulation step regularizes noisy human-extracted trajectories and allows large-scale data augmentation (object pose, friction, perturbation) that pure imitation cannot. Demonstrated on multi-finger hands across pick-place, tool-use, and bimanual manipulation tasks.
limitations: Three-stage pipeline brittle if any stage fails
Simulation must model contact accurately
No tactile sensing in pipeline
Object-specific tuning required for sim asset
