candidates.title: Human2Sim2Robot: Dexterous Manipulation Transfer via Simulation
candidates.method_summary: Human2Sim2Robot is a real-to-sim-to-real pipeline focused on dexterous (multi-finger) manipulation. From a single RGB-D human demonstration the system reconstructs the object and the human MANO hand trajectory, retargets the trajectory to a target dexterous robot hand (Allegro/LEAP), and trains an RL policy in simulation with a tracking reward plus residual dynamics. The trained policy is deployed zero-shot on the real robot. Compared to X-Sim (parallel-jaw focus), the dexterous-hand contact reasoning makes reward design and contact randomization more challenging, which the authors address with an object-pose-based reward that tolerates finger placement variation.
limitations: Single-demo regime is brittle to demo quality; dataset is very small
MANO-to-robot retargeting introduces ~2-5 cm fingertip error in some configs
RL in sim requires per-task reward; not yet a general-purpose pipeline
No tactile feedback used during deployment, contact uncertainty handled via randomization
Object set is rigid only; deformable or articulated multi-link objects untested

candidates.title: Isaac Lab: A GPU-Accelerated Simulation Framework for Multi-Modal Robot Learning
candidates.method_summary: 3.6.2 Integration with External Asset Generation Frameworks Scene graph generation frameworks such as Scene Synthesizer ( Eppner2024 ) and Infinigen ( infinigen2024indoors ) can be used to programmatically design cluttered 3D environments (such as homes and kitchens) and exported to USD for use in Isaac Lab. These frameworks have a library of predefined objects and seemlessly combine them with artist-generated datasets like Objaverse ( deitke2023objaverse ) to generate USD scenes. Training loco-manipulation policies on such procedural scenes in Isaac Lab results in better robustness and generalization. For instance, ( sleiman2024guided ) showed sim-to-real transfer of a door opening policy by a quadrupedal mobile manipulator, while ( luo2025emergent ) demonstrated generalizable pick-and-place skills for a humanoid robot trained in a large number of procedural kitchen environments. Model, world-model, or agent output is a bounded proposal above versioned skill APIs, feasibility projection, real-time control, independent evaluation, collision/force gates, protective stop, E-stop, and human authority.
limitations: 7 Future Work and Discussion To further advance Isaac Lab, a major upcoming milestone is integration with the Newton physics engine — a GPU-accelerated, extensible, and differentiable simulator offering state-of-the-art solvers for rigid, articulated, and deformable body dynamics ( Figure ˜ 33 ).
This addresses key limitations of existing engines in complex robotic scenarios.
Primary abstract does not establish the full robot/task/data/adaptation/reset denominator needed for cross-paper comparison.
No default-tabletop real-hardware evidence was established from the inspected abstract.
Training/evaluation overlap and benchmark contamination are unknown unless an official manifest says otherwise.

candidates.title: KARMA: Augmenting Embodied AI Agents with Long-and-short Term Memory Systems
candidates.method_summary: KARMA equips an LLM-based embodied agent with two interacting memory stores: (1) a long-term memory containing persistent scene knowledge (object locations, room layouts) updated only when high-confidence changes are observed, and (2) a short-term memory of recent observations and actions. Each planning step retrieves from both stores via similarity over textual descriptions, fuses retrieved content into the LLM prompt, and triggers writeback rules that decide which observations to persist. Demonstrated on the AI2-THOR Housekeep / household tasks within a chain-of-thought planner.
limitations: Evaluation only in AI2-THOR simulation — no real-robot deployment
Memory writes use heuristic confidence thresholds; does not learn what to remember
Memory is text-based and cannot represent fine-grained geometry or dynamics
LLM cost grows with retrieval depth, capping usable memory size in practice
No mechanism to handle contradictory long-term memory entries beyond timestamp tie-breaking

candidates.title: Large Video Planner Enables Generalizable Robot Control
candidates.method_summary: 3 Method A robot foundation model maps observations and goals to a sequence of actions. We realize this through a two-stage design: a large video planner followed by action extraction. Consider a robot facing a door it has never encountered before. Its camera perceives the door handle as its owner instructs, “Open this door.” The robot first employs a video foundation model to imagine how a rational human would perform the task—generating a video where a hand reaches for the handle, twists it, and pushes the door open. It then applies action extraction algorithms to translate this visual plan into executable control signals, whether for a dexterous five-fingered hand or a parallel gripper. In Section 3.1 , we present our video foundation model for generative planning in video space. Section 3.2 introduces an internet-scale video dataset of human activities and robot demonstrations that we curated for this study. Finally, Section 4 describes our retargeting mechanism that grounds the generated video plans into robot-specific actions across diverse morphologies. Model, world-model, or agent output is a bounded proposal above versioned skill APIs, feasibility projection, real-time control, independent evaluation, collision/force gates, protective stop, E-stop, and human authority.
limitations: 5 Limitations Our approach has several limitations.
In addition, on the robotics side, our robot action extraction has several limitations.
Both of these models can make mistakes, sometimes leading to task failures.
Primary abstract does not establish the full robot/task/data/adaptation/reset denominator needed for cross-paper comparison.
Real-hardware mention is not a release receipt and does not show independent collision/force/watchdog/E-stop authority.

candidates.title: Learning Sim-to-Real Humanoid Locomotion in 15 Minutes
candidates.method_summary: Demonstrates that off-policy RL — long assumed unstable for humanoid sim-to-real at scale — can train a deployable humanoid locomotion policy in ~15 minutes on a single RTX 4090 GPU. Uses FastSAC and FastTD3 (not PPO) with thousands of parallel environments, carefully tuned design choices, and minimalist reward functions. Demonstrated on Unitree G1 and Booster T1. Also reports rapid training of whole-body human-motion tracking policies. Contribution is a stability + throughput result for off-policy humanoid RL, companion-line to the same author's `seo2025fasttd3` paper (Younggyo Seo is first author on both).
limitations: Flat/mild terrain only
No manipulation
Single-skill focus per training run — multi-skill generalist policy not demonstrated in this paper

candidates.title: NRE-skin
candidates.method_summary: NRE-skin (Neuromorphic Resistive Electronic skin) is a stretchable e-skin with event-driven readout matching biological tactile afferents. Uses spiking encoding to compress dense pressure data by ~30-100x vs frame-based. Demonstrated on dexterous hand surface for grasp slip detection at sub-ms latency. Part of broader 2025 trend toward bio-inspired tactile front-ends.
limitations: Manufacturing yield and uniformity unproven at scale
Spike decoding requires specialized neuromorphic processors
Limited spatial resolution vs camera-based tactile

candidates.method_summary: Active 5.2 kg wearable upper-limb exoskeleton designed to cover 100% of human shoulder-elbow-wrist ROM for dexterous teleoperation of humanoid arms. Uses series-elastic actuation for transparent feedback and bilateral force reflection. Targets long-duration teleop sessions for data collection on humanoid platforms with minimal operator fatigue. Demonstrates teleop of multi-finger hands on bimanual humanoids.
limitations: Active actuation increases cost and complexity over passive exoskeletons like AirExo

candidates.title: OmniRetarget: Interaction-Preserving Data Generation for Humanoid Whole-Body Loco-Manipulation and Scene Interaction
candidates.method_summary: Retargeting engine built around an interaction mesh between agent, terrain, and manipulated objects. Minimizes Laplacian deformation between human and robot meshes while enforcing contact and kinematic constraints. Produces 8+ hours of physically feasible trajectories across OMOMO/LAFAN1/in-house mocap. Enables single-demo augmentation across embodiments and terrains; downstream proprioceptive RL achieves 30-second parkour and loco-manipulation on Unitree G1 with just 5 rewards and simple DR, no curriculum.
limitations: Interaction mesh requires object meshes — occluded scenes fail
Laplacian deformation assumes single connected body
Contact preservation may distort style

group.affiliation: Stanford (Karen Liu) + UC Berkeley (Abbeel/Sferrazza/Kanazawa) + CMU (Shi) + Covariant (Duan)

candidates.title: pi0 human-to-robot transfer: Co-finetuning for cross-embodiment generalization
candidates.method_summary: Physical Intelligence's pi0 VLA is co-finetuned on human ego data + cross-robot demonstrations, achieving generalization across 7+ robot embodiments. Action expert head with flow matching outputs continuous action chunks at 50Hz.

candidates.canonical_id: title:pi0 human to robot transfer human co finetuning for generalization
candidates.title: pi0 human-to-robot transfer: Human co-finetuning for generalization
candidates.method_summary: Extension of pi0 that incorporates human egocentric video as a co-finetuning signal alongside robot data. Uses human hand pose and object trajectories to expand the data distribution beyond what is feasible with teleoperation alone. Reports improved generalization on novel objects and environments.

candidates.title: pi0: A General-Purpose Robot Policy
candidates.method_summary: pi0 is a flow-matching vision-language-action foundation model from Physical Intelligence, built on a PaliGemma-class VLM backbone with an action expert that outputs continuous action chunks at 50 Hz. Pretrained on ~10,000 hours of cross-embodiment robot data spanning 7 robot platforms and 68 tasks, then fine-tuned for specific deployment. Demonstrates zero-shot and few-shot performance across laundry folding, table bussing, and box assembly with both single-arm and bimanual platforms.
limitations: Pretraining dataset is proprietary; not fully reproducible
Tactile and force signals largely absent from input modalities
Compute and data scale prohibitive for academic labs
Evaluation on truly novel embodiments outside the 7 trained platforms limited

group.lead_author: Physical Intelligence team (Sergey Levine, Chelsea Finn co-founders)

candidates.title: pi0.5: A Vision-Language-Action Model with Open-World Generalization

aliases.title: Tactile-VLA: Unlocking vision-language-action model's physical knowledge for tactile generalization
pi0.5: A VLA model and training recipe for post-deployment improvement via RLEF
pi0.5/RECAP: Post-deployment RL for continuous improvement
[$\pi_{0.5}$: a Vision-Language-Action Model with Open-World Generalization](https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.16054). arXiv:2504.16054
[$π_{0.5}$: a Vision-Language-Action Model with Open-World Generalization](https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.16054). arXiv:2504.16054v1

candidates.method_summary: pi0.5 is Physical Intelligence's follow-up to pi0, focused on open-world generalization. It augments the pi0 architecture (PaliGemma-3B + flow-matching action expert) with a co-trained mixture of robot demonstrations, web-scale image-text data, and language-only data, plus a hierarchical 'high-level reasoning' phase where the same model first emits a natural-language subgoal and then rolls out actions conditioned on it. This lets the model attempt unseen tasks by composing knowledge from non-robotic data sources. Trained on substantially more in-house data (~10K+ hours) and evaluated in unseen homes.
limitations: Closed weights and proprietary data hamper reproducibility
Trial counts in unseen homes still small (single-digit per task)
Hierarchical reasoning step adds latency and is sensitive to language ambiguity
Requires very large in-house data collection unattainable for academia
Long-horizon failures still common; full house cleaning end-to-end remains unreliable

group.lead_author: Kevin Black, Noah Brown, Danny Driess, Sergey Levine, Chelsea Finn (PI co-founders)

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: pi0.5 v1 / arXiv:2504.16054 verified; real mobile manipulation and unfamiliar-handle, arm-occlusion, and distraction failures bound the open-world claim.

candidates.title: PP-Tac: Paper Picking Using Omnidirectional Tactile Feedback in Dexterous Robotic Hands

aliases.title: PP-Tac: Paper pick-and-place with tactile diffusion policy

candidates.method_summary: PP-Tac targets the long-standing 'pick a single sheet of paper' task using a dexterous hand with omnidirectional tactile fingertips. A learned policy reads tactile contact distribution to differentiate top-sheet vs. multi-sheet contact, then executes a friction-based pinch-and-slide. Combines real-world demonstrations with tactile-conditioned diffusion policy.
limitations: Demonstrated on flat sheets only; curled or stacked-with-clip cases untested
Requires omnidirectional sensor that is not commercially available
Policy data-hungry: needs per-paper-type demos for best results

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Scholarly venue/book metadata and Scholar lookup present; no DOI/arXiv ID captured.

candidates.method_summary: PP-Tac proposes a physics-based trajectory synthesis pipeline for grasping thin objects (paper, fabric, cards) using tactile feedback. Plans contact trajectories accounting for object compliance and frictional contact mechanics. Demonstrates on tasks where vision alone struggles due to occlusion or thinness.
limitations: Bibliographic data incomplete

candidates.title: Precise and Dexterous Robotic Manipulation via Human-in-the-Loop Reinforcement Learning
candidates.method_summary: HIL-SERL: Human-in-the-loop sample-efficient RL framework for precise contact-rich manipulation. Combines online RL with human corrections (DAgger-style intervention) to learn dexterous tasks like SIM card insertion, electronic assembly, and dynamic flipping in 1-2 hours of real-world training. Uses a residual policy on top of a base policy and image-based rewards.
limitations: Requires constant human supervision during training
Per-task setup; not generalist
No tactile sensing — purely vision + force

Peeling 0.95 (RDP-Force) vs 0.44 (DP), bimanual 0.00->0.70, perturbation-after-contact 0.19->0.88 verified. TactAR AR-feedback framing accurate.

arXiv 2503.02881 primary body verified, but the manuscript appends TactAR to the paper title. Sections IV-B.1-3 describe slow-fast action generation and latency matching; the paper does not establish independent abort safety.

candidates.title: Real2Sim2Real: Self-Supervised System Identification for Zero-Shot Humanoid Deployment
candidates.method_summary: Successor line to ASAP with self-supervised sys-ID in-the-loop. Collects short real-robot data, updates sim parameters via gradient descent on observed rollouts, then fine-tunes policy. Positions for autonomous sim-alignment pipeline — reduces manual DR tuning.
limitations: Requires differentiable sim
Partial coverage of hardware variation
Not yet scaled to VLA-level systems

candidates.title: Recent Advances in Spike-Based Neural Coding for Tactile Perception
candidates.method_summary: Survey of spike-based (neuromorphic) coding schemes for tactile perception, covering rate coding, temporal coding, and population coding implemented on memristor and CMOS neuromorphic chips. Reviews tactile encoding inspired by SA-I/RA-I/PC mechanoreceptors and benchmarks against classical CNN pipelines on slip detection and texture classification. Identifies energy/latency advantages and open challenges in training spiking networks.
limitations: Spiking networks still trail CNN/Transformers on accuracy for complex tasks
Hardware fragmentation across neuromorphic chip vendors
Author list opaque

candidates.title: Representing Data in Robotic Tactile Perception -- A Review

aliases.title: Representing data in robotic tactile perception

candidates.method_summary: Review paper systematizing how tactile data is represented in robotics: raw signals, image-like maps, point clouds, graph structures, and learned embeddings. Surveys 100+ works spanning capacitive, resistive, optical, and magnetic sensors. Proposes a taxonomy linking representation choice to downstream tasks (slip, classification, manipulation policy).
limitations: Review scope leaves implementation details to original papers
Rapidly evolving field - some 2025 advances likely missed
Limited critical comparison of representation choices

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Scholarly venue/book metadata and Scholar lookup present; no DOI/arXiv ID captured.

candidates.title: RGMC Champion: Kinematic trajectory optimization
candidates.method_summary: Winning entry of the IROS Robotic Grasping and Manipulation Competition (RGMC) 2024/2025 dexterous track based on kinematic trajectory optimization rather than learned policies. Combines tactile-conditioned grasp planning with sampling-based trajectory optimization for in-hand reorientation and assembly tasks. Demonstrates that classical model-based methods, when carefully tuned with tactile feedback, can outperform RL/IL baselines on the competition's contact-rich tasks.
limitations: Heavy task-specific tuning, limited generalization beyond competition setup
Computational cost of trajectory optimization at runtime
Does not scale to free-form objects without re-modeling

candidates.title: RoboMemory: A Brain-inspired Multi-memory Agentic Framework for Lifelong Learning in Physical Embodied Systems
candidates.method_summary: RoboMemory proposes a multi-store memory architecture inspired by cognitive science: a working memory (current task buffer), an episodic memory (recent task rollouts), a semantic memory (factual scene/object knowledge), and a procedural memory (learned skills). An LLM-based agent reads/writes these stores during planning, and the architecture supports lifelong learning — new tasks add procedural skills without overwriting prior memory. Evaluated on long-horizon embodied tasks where reuse of past experience matters.
limitations: Multi-store architecture adds significant prompt engineering complexity
Empirical claims rely on simulation; real-robot deployment limited
Memory-store routing heuristics may not generalize across domains
LLM cost grows with concurrent retrieval from multiple stores
Author list and venue need primary verification — abstract-level summary only

group.affiliation: TBD (Chinese academic group based on arxiv pattern)

candidates.title: RobotDancing: Residual-action reinforcement learning enables robust long-horizon humanoid motion tracking
Robotic In-Hand Manipulation for Large-Range Precise Object Movement: The RGMC Champion Solution
candidates.method_summary: Champion solution for ICRA Robotic Grasping and Manipulation Competition (RGMC) in-hand manipulation track. Uses model-based planning with tactile feedback to achieve large-range precise object reorientation. Combines finger-gaiting strategies with contact-rich control for sub-millimeter accuracy.
limitations: Model-based approach requires accurate object/contact modeling
Limited to objects matched to gripper geometry
Generalization to novel objects not demonstrated

candidates.title: Robotic Perception with a Large Tactile-Vision-Language Model for Physical Property Inference (VTV-LLM)
candidates.method_summary: VTV-LLM is a tactile-vision-language model that fuses tactile imagery (GelSight-style) with RGB and language to infer physical properties (hardness, roughness, material). Trained on a multi-modal corpus aligning tactile patches with text descriptions, achieving open-vocabulary tactile reasoning.
limitations: Limited to vision-based tactile (GelSight); no force-array compatibility
Property labels coarse (hard/soft, rough/smooth)
Compute-heavy LLM backbone for real-time use

candidates.title: Robust Model-Based In-Hand Manipulation with Integrated Real-Time Motion-Contact Planning and Tracking

aliases.title: Contact-implicit model predictive control for dexterous in-hand manipulation: A long-horizon and robust approach
Robust model-based in-hand manipulation with integrated real-time motion-contact planning and tracking
Robust in-hand manipulation with motion-contact planning

candidates.method_summary: Model-based in-hand manipulation framework that jointly plans motion and contact schedules in real time, then tracks them with tactile feedback for robust closed-loop control. Uses a hybrid optimization approach where the contact mode sequence is searched discretely while joint trajectories are optimized continuously. Demonstrates real-time replanning under disturbances on a multi-finger hand with tactile fingertips.
limitations: Requires accurate object/finger contact models
Computational complexity grows with finger count
Tested mostly on geometric primitives

candidates.title: Self-Driving Laboratories 2025: Automating the Scientific Method
candidates.method_summary: 자율 실험실(SDL) 분야의 체계적 리뷰로, 과학적 방법의 전 단계(가설 생성, 실험 설계, 실행, 분석, 문헌 업데이트)를 자동화하는 최신 접근법을 망라한다. 재료 발견, 신약 개발, 화학 최적화 영역에서 데이터 수집 속도 10배 향상, 실험 주기 대폭 단축 사례를 문서화한다.

도메인 전문성 요구사항
소규모 SDL에서 대규모 산업 전환의 어려움

candidates.title: Self-supervised perception for tactile skin covered dexterous hands
candidates.method_summary: Sparsh-skin extends the Sparsh family of self-supervised tactile foundation models from vision-based fingertips to magnetometer-based whole-hand tactile skin (ReSkin) covering an Allegro hand with 16 patches and ~150 taxels. The model uses masked autoencoding and contrastive objectives over multi-finger temporal sequences to learn cross-sensor representations from ~1M unlabeled tactile frames collected during free-form play. Downstream evaluation on grasp stability, slip detection, and contact localization shows the pretrained encoder outperforms supervised-from-scratch baselines, especially under low-data regimes (10-100 labeled examples).
limitations: Magnetometer-based skin has lower spatial resolution than vision-based GelSight-class sensors
Self-supervised pretraining requires substantial unlabeled play data per hand-skin instance
Generalization across different skin layouts or different robot hands not yet demonstrated
Downstream tasks evaluated are mostly classification, not closed-loop manipulation

candidates.title: Sensor-Invariant Tactile Representation
candidates.method_summary: Learns a sensor-invariant tactile representation that disentangles sensor-specific artifacts from contact-relevant features. Uses adversarial or contrastive alignment across heterogeneous tactile sensors so that downstream policies generalize without sensor-specific retraining. Aimed at the cross-embodiment tactile transfer problem.
limitations: Requires paired or aligned multi-sensor data which is hard to collect
Adversarial training stability concerns
May lose sensor-specific high-fidelity cues

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: arXiv 2502.19638 confirmed; author fixed Various->Gupta et al., ICLR 2025.

candidates.title: SeqGrasp: Sequential Grasping via Opposition Space
candidates.method_summary: SeqGrasp formulates sequential dexterous grasping as a search over Opposition Space — the geometric structure of finger-pair contacts that can stably oppose object reaction forces. By pre-computing per-finger-pair opposition regions, the method plans sequential grasps where each new pick uses an unused finger pair while the others retain prior objects. The framework cleanly decomposes which fingers go to which object, making sequential dexterous picking tractable.
limitations: Opposition-space precomputation per object
Assumes rigid objects with known geometry
No tactile feedback during execution
Scalability to many fingers / objects unclear

candidates.title: SeqMultiGrasp: Sequential Multi-Object Grasping via Diffusion
candidates.method_summary: SeqMultiGrasp extends MultiGrasp to sequentially grasp multiple objects with a single dexterous hand — picking object A while continuing to hold previously grasped objects B, C. A diffusion policy generates incremental finger reconfigurations conditioned on currently held objects' poses and the next target. Demonstrated on Shadow and Allegro hands in clutter, it allows 3-5 sequential picks before placement, mimicking human one-handed multi-pick behavior.
limitations: Held-object pose tracking degrades over sequence
No tactile feedback for slip detection
Limited to small/medium rigid objects
Retention failures not gracefully handled

candidates.canonical_id: title:siemens makes factory floors smarter with industrial ai
candidates.title: Siemens Makes Factory Floors Smarter With Industrial AI
candidates.method_summary: AI·시뮬레이션·디지털트윈 융합이 제조사의 생산성·유연성·통찰을 새로운 수준으로 끌어올린다는 NVIDIA-Siemens 협업 블로그. Siemens Inspekto AI 시각 품질 검사(20 샘플 1시간 학습), Audi 차체 공장 5M 용접/일 25x 가속, Senseye 예측 정비 generative AI 등 구체적 사례 제시. SPS 2025에서 NX CAM Copilot 시연으로 programming bottleneck 제거. Amberg 공장은 1,000+ 센서, AI 예측 정비, IBM 연구 인용 25% 정비 비용 감소.
limitations: Siemens 자사 도구 중심
비-Siemens 환경 통합 비용

candidates.title: Sim-to-Real Reinforcement Learning for Vision-Based Dexterous Manipulation on Humanoids
candidates.method_summary: Sim-to-real RL pipeline for vision-based dexterous manipulation on humanoid platforms. Uses massively parallel simulation (Isaac Lab) with domain randomization on visuals, dynamics, and contact, then transfers to a humanoid with 5-finger hands. Achieves dexterous in-hand reorientation and bimanual handover purely from sim.
limitations: No tactile input — vision-only
Sim-to-real gap large for highly contact-rich tasks
Long sim training time (days on multi-GPU)

candidates.title: Simultaneous Tactile-Visual Perception for Learning Multimodal Robot Manipulation
candidates.method_summary: Proposes a simultaneous tactile-visual perception framework where tactile and RGB streams are fused via cross-attention transformer for manipulation policy learning. Demonstrates that synchronous (not delayed) tactile fusion improves contact-rich task success significantly.
limitations: Synchronization requires hardware-level timing
Only tested on a single robot platform

candidates.title: SmolVLA: A Vision-Language-Action Model for Affordable and Efficient Robotics
candidates.method_summary: 450 M-parameter open-source VLA from Hugging Face LeRobot team. Combines Transformer with flow-matching decoder, skips half the vision-model layers for speed, uses asynchronous inference stack that decouples perception from action execution. Trained on <30k episodes of LeRobot community data. Matches π0 (3.3 B) on LIBERO (87.3%) and outperforms ACT / π0 on real-world pick-place, stacking, sorting at 78.3% average.
limitations: Smaller VLM limits rare-language instruction handling
Community data quality varies

candidates.canonical_id: title:soft robotic dexterous hands advances and challenges
candidates.title: Soft robotic dexterous hands: Advances and challenges
candidates.method_summary: Review of soft robotic dexterous hand technologies covering pneumatic, cable-driven, and hybrid actuation. Discusses challenges in achieving precision and force-control with intrinsically compliant materials. Compares stiffness-modulating mechanisms (jamming, variable-stiffness joints) for combining safety with manipulation capability.

Limited primary source verification
Soft hand metrics not standardized across surveyed work

candidates.title: Soft Robotic Hand with Tactile Palm-Finger Coordination

aliases.title: Soft robotic hand with tactile palm-finger coordination

candidates.method_summary: Designs a soft anthropomorphic hand with tactile coverage on both palm and fingers, enabling palm-finger coordinated grasps (cradling, scooping, in-palm rolling) that rigid hands struggle with. Pneumatic soft fingers with embedded force-sensitive elements and a tactile palm pad coordinate via a learned controller that explicitly uses palm contact as a stabilizing reference. Demonstrates human-like grasping diversity on 50+ everyday objects.
limitations: Soft hand actuation bandwidth lower than rigid (limits dynamic tasks)
Pneumatic pressure source needed; not self-contained
Tactile resolution coarser than vision-based gel sensors

candidates.canonical_id: title:softwear automation sewbot fully automated apparel sewing
candidates.title: SoftWear Automation Sewbot: Fully Automated Apparel Sewing
candidates.venue: SoftWear Automation Press Release / BESTSELLER Partnership
candidates.method_summary: SoftWear의 Sewbot은 컴퓨터 비전과 로봇 팔을 결합하여 티셔츠를 22초에 제조하며, 노동 비용을 개당 $0.33로 낮췄다. 600켤레/교대 생산이 가능하며, 패션 기업 BESTSELLER의 투자를 받았다. 유연한 직물 조작이라는 핵심 기술 난제를 해결한 사례다.

candidates.canonical_id: title:taccap fbg optical tactile sensor thimble for human and robot fingertips
candidates.title: TacCap: FBG optical tactile sensor thimble for human and robot fingertips
candidates.method_summary: Fiber Bragg Grating (FBG) based optical tactile thimble that can be worn on a human fingertip during demonstration and mounted on a robot finger for transfer. Uses wavelength shifts in embedded fibers to measure normal/shear force at sub-mm spatial scale with kHz sampling. Demonstrates direct tactile signal transfer between human-collected and robot-executed manipulation by ensuring identical sensor frontend across embodiments. Targets the human-to-robot tactile gap that conventional vision-based gels cannot bridge due to size constraints.
limitations: FBG interrogator is bulky and expensive, limiting deployment scale
Limited spatial resolution compared to camera-based gels
Calibration sensitive to fiber bending and temperature

candidates.title: TacCap: FBG-Based Optical Tactile Thimble
candidates.method_summary: TacCap is a thimble-form-factor tactile sensor based on fiber Bragg grating (FBG) optics. The fiber is embedded in a soft elastomer cap that fits over a human or robot fingertip; FBG wavelength shifts encode local strain at multiple grating points, recovering distributed contact forces with sub-millisecond latency and immunity to electromagnetic noise. The thimble is wearable on human fingers for demonstration capture and can also be mounted on robot fingertips for matched deployment, providing a route for human-to-robot tactile transfer through identical sensor physics on both sides.
limitations: FBG interrogator is expensive and bulky — limits field deployment
Optical fiber routing constrains thimble flexibility
Spatial resolution is limited by number of gratings (typically <10)
No shear/normal decomposition without careful calibration
Wear and elastomer aging affect calibration over time

group.affiliation: Likely NUS / NTU or Taiwanese university group

candidates.canonical_id: title:tactile equivariance for cross orientation transfer
candidates.title: Tactile Equivariance for Cross-Orientation Transfer
candidates.method_summary: EquiTac introduces SO(2)/SE(2)-equivariant neural network architectures for tactile-based manipulation, enabling cross-orientation transfer of skills learned at one object orientation to other orientations without retraining. The equivariant tactile encoder respects the symmetry of contact patterns under rigid rotations, dramatically improving sample efficiency on rotation-invariant tasks like in-hand reorientation and tool use.
limitations: Equivariance assumes idealized symmetry — gravity and gripper asymmetry break it
Bibtex_key null — institutional placeholder
Limited to in-plane rotations; full SO(3) equivariance harder
Verification status: bibtex_backfill

candidates.title: Tactile Robotics: Past and Future
candidates.method_summary: Lepora's perspective paper traces tactile robotics from early piezoresistive arrays through GelSight-class vision-based sensors to modern multimodal fingertips. It frames the field as moving from sensing-as-measurement to sensing-as-perception integrated with embodied learning, highlighting trends in TacTip-family sensors, sim-to-real, and tactile foundation models. Discusses open challenges in standardization, datasets, and integration with VLA stacks.
limitations: Single-author perspective; reflects Bristol/TacTip viewpoint
Limited engagement with neuromorphic and capacitive skin lines
Forward-looking claims about VLA integration not yet empirically grounded

candidates.title: Tactile-Conditioned Diffusion Policy for Force-Aware Robotic Manipulation (FARM)
candidates.method_summary: FARM augments a diffusion policy with a tactile conditioning stream from vision-based tactile sensors (GelSight-style) to enable force-aware behavior on contact-rich tasks. The tactile encoder produces per-step latent vectors fed alongside vision/proprioception into the diffusion transformer's denoising network. Evaluated on insertion, wiping, and delicate-object tasks where pure vision diffusion policies fail due to occlusion and small contact margins.
limitations: Single fingertip tactile sensor — limited spatial coverage
Diffusion-policy inference latency remains high
Demonstration data still required for each task

candidates.title: Tactile-Language-Action Model for Contact-Rich Manipulation (TLA)

aliases.title: TLA: Tactile-language-action model for contact-rich manipulation

candidates.method_summary: Extends VLA paradigm to a Tactile-Language-Action (TLA) model that consumes tactile images (vision-based sensors) plus natural language instructions and outputs actions, without RGB camera input. Trained on a curated tactile-language dataset where contact events are annotated with text descriptions ('soft', 'edge', 'slipping'). Demonstrates instruction-following manipulation in scenarios where vision is unavailable (occluded or dark).
limitations: Requires curated tactile-language annotations, costly to scale
Vision-free setting is somewhat artificial for general manipulation
Language vocabulary still narrow

candidates.title: Tactile-VLA
candidates.method_summary: Tactile-VLA augments standard Vision-Language-Action models with tactile observation tokens, enabling contact-rich manipulation guided by language. Tactile signals (typically GelSight or Digit images) are encoded via vision encoder and concatenated with RGB tokens before LLM backbone. Improves contact-dependent task success (insertion, peeling, slip recovery) by 15-30 pp over vision-only VLAs in reported benchmarks.
limitations: Tactile token compute overhead in VLA inference
Limited tactile-specific pretraining data
Sim-to-real of tactile representations remains hard

candidates.title: Tactile-VLA: Unlocking Vision-Language-Action Model's Physical Knowledge for Tactile Generalization
candidates.method_summary: Tactile-VLA injects tactile observations into a frozen large VLA backbone (e.g., OpenVLA-style) via a lightweight tactile adapter, unlocking the model's pre-trained physical knowledge for contact-rich tasks without full fine-tuning. Tactile features are tokenized and concatenated with vision/language tokens in the VLA's transformer. Shows that the VLA's language-grounded physical priors generalize to novel materials and stiffness when prompted with tactile context.
limitations: Requires VLA backbone pre-trained at scale
Tactile-language alignment quality depends on tokenizer choice
Tested on single-arm + parallel gripper or simple finger

candidates.title: UniTacHand
candidates.method_summary: UniTacHand proposes a unified tactile representation for cross-hand-platform transfer. Fingertip tactile signals (Digit, GelSight, capacitive) are mapped to a shared latent space via contrastive pretraining on paired multi-sensor data. Enables policies trained on one hand (e.g., Allegro+GelSight) to deploy on another (e.g., LEAP+Digit) with minimal fine-tuning. Reports ~70-80% retention of original task success across hand swaps.
limitations: Requires paired multi-sensor calibration data
Latent space alignment quality varies by sensor modality
Ablation on tactile-only vs vision+tactile contributions limited

group.affiliation: Unverified (Zhang et al., likely Tsinghua or Stanford)

candidates.title: UniTacHand: Unified Spatio-Tactile Representation for Human to Robotic Hand Skill Transfer

aliases.title: UniTacHand: Unified spatio-tactile representation for human to robotic hand skill transfer

candidates.method_summary: UniTacHand unifies spatial hand pose with tactile contact in a single representation to bridge human-glove demonstrations and robot-hand execution. Maps both human glove signals and robot tactile fingertip readings into a shared embedding aligned with hand kinematics. Enables policy learning that transfers from human to dexterous robot hand.
limitations: Requires paired human-robot data which is costly
Embodiment gap (5-finger human vs N-finger robot) not fully closed
Tactile glove fidelity bounded by sensor density

candidates.title: VidBot: Learning Robot Manipulation from Internet Videos
candidates.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR) 2025
candidates.method_summary: VidBot tackles policy learning from unstructured internet videos (YouTube, instructional clips). It uses a foundation-model stack to extract hand pose, object segmentation, and 3D scene geometry from in-the-wild monocular video, lifts these into a coarse trajectory in a canonical object frame, and trains a diffusion policy that conditions on text + visual prompt and outputs a 3D end-effector path. A small amount of robot-side adaptation (sim or 5-10 real demos) closes the embodiment gap. The contribution is largely a scalable annotation pipeline for the long-tail web video corpus.
limitations: Internet videos are extremely noisy: viewpoint, occlusion, lighting variation
Hand-pose and 3D lifting from monocular video has ~5-10 cm error
Tasks restricted to those with clear visual structure; deformable / contact-rich underperform
Domain gap remains substantial — adaptation demos are still required
No tactile information

group.affiliation: Likely UC Berkeley / UIUC / industry collaboration

candidates.method_summary: Systematic ablation of VLA design choices (backbone size, action head type, data composition, tokenization) across multiple platforms. Identifies action representation (continuous vs discrete) and data diversity as the largest contributors to generalization, more than backbone scale alone. Provides guidance for future VLA design.

candidates.title: World4RL: Diffusion World Models for Policy Refinement with Reinforcement Learning for Robotic Manipulation

aliases.title: [World4RL: Diffusion World Models for Policy Refinement with Reinforcement Learning for Robotic Manipulation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.19080). arXiv:2509.19080

candidates.method_summary: II-A World Models World models have developed rapidly, driven by advances in generative modeling. Early works like VAE-RNN world model [ 13 ] inspired latent dynamics models such as DreamerV3 [ 15 ] and TD-MPC2 [ 16 ] , while more recent Transformer-based approaches (e.g., Genie [ 5 ] , Drive-WM [ 33 ] ) significantly extended the temporal horizon and expressiveness. With the rise of conditional diffusion models, world models have achieved high-fidelity video prediction in diverse domains such as autonomous driving (DriveDreamer [ 32 ] ), navigation (NWM [ 4 ] ), and gaming (Diamond [ 1 ] ). These advances indicate that generative world models are becoming powerful tools for simulating complex environments, motivating their application to robotic manipulation. Model, world-model, or agent output is a bounded proposal above versioned skill APIs, feasibility projection, real-time control, independent evaluation, collision/force gates, protective stop, E-stop, and human authority.
limitations: Policy refinement occurs entirely in imagined rollouts; the physical experiments validate the resulting policy, not online world-model training or a safety monitor.
The six real tasks use fixed initial scene configurations and robot starting poses, limiting claims about deployment variation.
The paper does not validate the complete S13 language-led, contact-rich tabletop assembly stack or transfer execution/safety authority to the model.
Training/evaluation overlap beyond the stated fixed datasets and task protocol remains subject to artifact-level audit.

group.affiliation: not normalized from arXiv metadata; resolve from PDF
group.funding: not reported in inspected primary abstract

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: World4RL v2 / arXiv:2509.19080 verified: imagined policy refinement, Meta-World, and six physical Franka tasks at 20 rollouts each. This does not validate the full S13 stack.

candidates.title: [ZOE PREDICT — nutritional intervention based on microbiome and metabolic phenotyping](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41591-024-02855-5)
3D Diffuser Actor: Policy Diffusion with 3D Scene Representations
candidates.method_summary: 3D Diffuser Actor combines diffusion-policy action generation with explicit 3D scene representations. RGB-D inputs are lifted to 3D feature point clouds (using DINO + 3D positional encoding), and a transformer-based diffusion model conditions on this 3D representation to denoise end-effector action trajectories. The 3D representation makes the policy invariant to camera viewpoint shifts and gives strong inductive bias for spatial reasoning over scene geometry.
limitations: Requires accurate depth sensing — performance degrades with noisy or missing depth
Computational cost of 3D feature lifting and diffusion denoising is high
Largely simulator evaluation; real-world demonstrations limited
No language conditioning beyond task ID — not directly an instruction-following VLA
Scaling to larger, more diverse data not yet demonstrated

candidates.title: 3D-Mem: 3D Scene Memory for Embodied Exploration and Reasoning
candidates.method_summary: 3D-Mem maintains a 3D scene memory composed of 'memory snapshots' — geometry-aware visual chunks tagged with semantic descriptors. As the agent explores, it adds snapshots to memory; for downstream reasoning, snapshots relevant to a query are retrieved and fed to a VLM. This combines geometric grounding (3D position of each snapshot) with VLM open-vocabulary semantics, addressing limitations of pure 2D memory or pure scene-graph memory for embodied Q&A and exploration.
limitations: Largely simulation evaluation
Snapshot granularity is heuristic — too coarse loses detail, too fine bloats memory
Retrieval relies on textualized snapshot descriptors, limiting fine-grained appearance reasoning
VLM call cost per query remains high
Long-term memory consolidation / forgetting not addressed

group.affiliation: UMass Amherst (Chuang Gan lab) + IBM Research + ShanghaiTech

candidates.title: 3D-ViTac: Learning Fine-Grained Manipulation with Visuo-Tactile Sensing
candidates.method_summary: 3D-ViTac fuses 3D vision (point clouds / NeRF-style features) with dense tactile signals to learn fine-grained manipulation policies. Uses a unified 3D representation where tactile contact patches are projected into the same coordinate frame as visual point clouds, enabling a single Transformer policy to attend over both. Shown on contact-rich tasks like cap unscrewing and fragile-object handling.
limitations: Calibration between 3D vision and tactile coordinate frames non-trivial
Tactile sensors used are research-grade; not production-friendly
Generalization to novel objects shown only on small set

candidates.title: Actuated Palms for Soft Robotic Hands: Review and Perspectives
candidates.method_summary: Pozzi et al. survey the design space of actuated palms in soft robotic hands, organizing prior work along axes of actuation type (tendon, pneumatic, SMA), DOF count, and grasp role (cupping, regrasping, in-hand reorientation). The review argues that the palm is an under-exploited DOF in dexterous design and catalogs >40 palm-actuated hands. They propose taxonomies and benchmark tasks (e.g., flat-object pickup, regrasp) that highlight where actuated palms outperform rigid ones.
limitations: Survey scope; no new experimental validation
Limited treatment of tactile sensing on palms
Coverage skewed to soft (not rigid-soft hybrid) designs
Benchmark proposals not yet adopted

candidates.title: Agile But Safe: Learning Collision-Free High-Speed Legged Locomotion
candidates.method_summary: ABS: dual-policy safety framework. A fast 'agile' policy handles high-speed running; a conservative 'recovery' policy takes over when safety is at risk. Gated by a learned reach-avoid value function. Enables 3.1 m/s running with obstacle avoidance on Unitree Go1.
limitations: Quadruped not humanoid — template for future humanoid safety
Simulation-to-real gap still present at top speeds
Reach-avoid value function scales poorly with state dim

candidates.method_summary: Passive low-cost (~$600) exoskeleton mirroring a target robot arm's kinematics so humans can demonstrate whole-arm manipulation in unstructured environments without a robot present. Joint encoders capture demonstrations directly in robot joint space, eliminating retargeting. Trained imitation policies on AirExo data and evaluated on a real Franka/UR-style arm. Shows that out-of-lab kinesthetic data improves generalization compared to lab-only teleop.
limitations: Whole-arm only — does not capture dexterous finger motion
Kinematic mirror restricts to one robot family
No force/tactile signals captured

candidates.title: AnySkin: Plug-and-play Skin Sensing for Robotic Touch

aliases.title: AnySkin: Plug-and-play skin sensing for robotic touch
AnySkin: Plug-and-play skin sensing
[AnySkin: Plug-and-play Skin Sensing for Robotic Touch](https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.08276)

candidates.method_summary: AnySkin is a magnetometer-based plug-and-play tactile skin (descendant of ReSkin) that achieves cross-instance consistency: policies trained on one skin transfer zero-shot to a freshly replaced skin. Uses a removable elastomer with embedded magnets over a fixed PCB; calibration is largely amortized into the sensor design. Demonstrates skin replacement in seconds and transfer of imitation policies without retraining.
limitations: Resolution coarser than vision-based sensors (handful of taxels)
Magnetometer susceptible to nearby ferromagnetic perturbations
Currently demonstrated on grippers more than full multifingered hands

candidates.title: Automating Robot Failure Recovery Using Vision-Language Models With Optimized Prompts
candidates.method_summary: III Methodology In this section, we detail the pipeline of using VLMs for robot failure detection and recovery, as illustrated in Fig 2 . The standard robot control loop, depicted within the grey box, traditionally relies on specific feature extraction algorithms or extensive policy training based on large datasets to recover failures. These approaches are often tailored to specific failure modes and struggle with high-dimensional natural language and image observations. In contrast, our approach introduces a black-box VLM correction controller that converts visual RGB images into textual descriptions of diverse failure reasons. These descriptions are then transformed into recovery actions at the motion level or recovery plans at the task level, as illustrated in the blue outer loop. Our VLM-based recovery approach extends failure recovery concepts to operate within a broader, more abstract language space and image space. Model, world-model, or agent output is a bounded proposal above versioned skill APIs, feasibility projection, real-time control, independent evaluation, collision/force gates, protective stop, E-stop, and human authority.
limitations: V CONCLUSIONS This work introduces and evaluates the use of VLMs as failure detectors and correctors for robot motion-level control and task-level planning.
Primary abstract does not establish the full robot/task/data/adaptation/reset denominator needed for cross-paper comparison.
No default-tabletop real-hardware evidence was established from the inspected abstract.
Training/evaluation overlap and benchmark contamination are unknown unless an official manifest says otherwise.

candidates.title: AutoRT: Embodied Foundation Models for Large Scale Orchestration of Robotic Agents
candidates.method_summary: 5.4 Model Training The data generated by AutoRT covers a significantly wider range of language and visuals than in datasets such as RT-1 (Brohan et al., 2022 ) . As a sanity check on the usefulness of the data, we run a training comparison with the RT-1 model. A pretrained RT-1 model is co-fine-tuned on a 50-50 mixture of the pretraining dataset described in Brohan et al. ( 2022 ) and AutoRT’s dataset. RT-1 is used instead of RT-2 due to training more quickly and cheaply. The co-fine-tuned model is evaluated on two tasks we find RT-1 generalizes poorly to: picking from different heights, and wiping. Exact evaluation instructions and details are in Appendix F . When co-fine-tuned, RT-1’s performance increases from 0% to 12.5% on picking from different height, and 10% to 30% on wiping. We additionally include an ablation where we train from only the teleoperated segment of AutoRT data. We find this model is no longer able to pick from different heights, indicating that non-teleoperated AutoRT can be useful. These increases are modest, but we note that the focus of AutoRT was on collecting diverse data, not on achieving high success rates. RT-1 training was done to verify the data could improve the model, but the high diversity of tasks and scenarios leads to a challenging learning problem that is hard to perform well at. Table 5: Results from co-finetuning RT-1 on AutoRT data Picking (Height Generalization) Wiping RT-1 0/24 = 0% 1/10 = 10% Co-fine-tuned RT-1 on AutoRT data 3/24 Model, world-model, or agent output is a bounded proposal above versioned skill APIs, feasibility projection, real-time control, independent evaluation, collision/force gates, protective stop, E-stop, and human authority.
limitations: Perception hallucinations, motion blur, and poor generalization to novel environments can introduce and propagate failures.
Constitutional prompting and affordance filtering do not guarantee safety; some unsafe tasks pass and human supervision remains necessary.
Foundation models have no safety guarantee; the deployment relies on traditional environment controls and does not provide an independent full-stack safety proof.
The collection-scale denominator does not establish S13 tabletop-assembly task success or transfer execution authority.

candidates.title: Berkeley Humanoid: A Research Platform for Learning-based Control
candidates.method_summary: Mid-scale research humanoid from UC Berkeley Sreenath group (arXiv 2407.21781, July 2024). 0.85 m standing, ~16 kg, 6 DoF per leg (12 DoF legs total), custom in-house QDD-style actuators made from aluminum (7075 / 6061) and steel (SKD11). Trained in Isaac Lab; demonstrated commanded 1 m/s walking reached from rest within 1 s, 'hundreds of meters' outdoor traversal. Lightweight design positions against Unitree G1 for labs wanting full hardware control. A separate follow-up project 'Berkeley Humanoid Lite' (arXiv 2504.17249, 2025) is 3D-printed open-source at <$5k BOM — distinct from the 2024 mid-scale Berkeley Humanoid.
limitations: Mid-scale (0.85 m) — limited manipulation reach
In-house hardware limits reproducibility vs Unitree G1
Outdoor traversal metrics qualitative ('hundreds of meters') rather than benchmarked

candidates.title: Binding Touch to Everything: Learning Unified Multimodal Tactile Representations

aliases.title: UniTouch: Binding touch to everything

candidates.method_summary: UniTouch (TouchBind) learns a unified tactile representation aligned with vision and language across heterogeneous tactile sensors (GelSight, DIGIT, GelSlim) by binding to a frozen image-text encoder (a la ImageBind). Sensor-specific tokens enable transfer across sensors without retraining. The shared embedding supports zero-shot tactile retrieval, classification, and generation tasks.
limitations: Relies on paired tactile-vision-text data which is scarce outside benchmarks
Sensor token approach requires re-training when adding entirely new sensor modality
Tactile encoder still under-trained relative to vision/language counterparts

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: arXiv 2401.18084 confirmed (UniTouch, CVPR 2024).

candidates.title: D(R,O) Grasp: A Unified Representation of Robot and Object Interaction for Cross-Embodiment Dexterous Grasping
candidates.venue: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
candidates.method_summary: Proposes D(R,O) representation encoding pairwise distances between robot hand keypoints and object surface points as a unified embodiment-agnostic grasp representation. Trains a single grasp prediction model that generalizes across multiple hand embodiments (Allegro, Shadow, Barrett, EZGripper). Achieves higher success rates and faster inference (~0.2 s) than baselines.
limitations: Static grasp prediction without in-hand manipulation
Object pose assumed available (point cloud)
Real-robot experiments limited compared to simulation scale

candidates.title: Design and Evaluation of a 3-DoF Haptic Device for Directional Shear Cues on the Forearm
candidates.method_summary: Wearable 3-DoF haptic device that delivers directional shear cues to the forearm via three independently actuated tactors. Psychophysical study evaluates direction discrimination across 8 cardinal/intercardinal directions. Targets teleoperation feedback and VR applications where finger-mounted haptics are infeasible. Reports >85% direction recognition accuracy at moderate shear amplitudes.
limitations: Forearm not fingertip — bandwidth and spatial resolution lower than glabrous skin haptics
Small user study limits statistical generalization
No closed-loop teleop demonstration with robot tactile data

candidates.title: DexCap: Scalable and Portable Mocap Data Collection System for Dexterous Manipulation

aliases.title: DexCap: Scalable and portable mocap data collection system

candidates.method_summary: DexCap is a portable mocap glove system combining electromagnetic finger tracking with chest-mounted RGBD cameras to capture in-the-wild dexterous manipulation data. Provides per-finger pose at 60Hz and synchronized 3D point clouds without lab fixtures. Used to train robot dexterous policies via retargeting to robot hand kinematics. Enables hours-scale dexterous demo collection in arbitrary environments.
limitations: Camera-based SLAM degrades in featureless environments
Finger occlusion still affects tracking
No tactile capture (motion only)

candidates.title: DexH2R: Task-oriented Dexterous Manipulation from Human to Robots

aliases.title: DexH2R: Task-oriented dexterous manipulation from human to robots

candidates.method_summary: DexH2R proposes a task-oriented framework for transferring dexterous manipulation skills from human demonstrations to robot hands. Uses task-aware retargeting that prioritizes contact and goal-relevant features over pose-level mimicry. Demonstrates transfer across multiple dexterous tasks with reduced sim2real gap.
limitations: Task-aware features must be hand-designed per task family
Sim2real gap remains for fine manipulation
Limited tactile signal usage

candidates.canonical_id: title:dextrous tactile in hand manipulation using a modular reinforcement learning architecture
candidates.title: Dextrous tactile in-hand manipulation using a modular reinforcement learning architecture
candidates.method_summary: Pitz et al. propose a modular RL architecture that decomposes dexterous tactile in-hand manipulation into reusable sub-policies (grasp, regrasp, rotate) connected by a high-level scheduler. Tactile observations from fingertip sensors are fused with proprioception and used as state input. Sub-policies are trained in simulation and combined to handle long-horizon reorientation tasks on multifingered hands.
limitations: Modular decomposition requires hand-designed sub-task interface; not fully end-to-end
Tactile signal used coarsely (binary contact / force vector) rather than rich images
Generalization to novel object geometries beyond training distribution limited

candidates.canonical_id: title:dextrous tactile in hand manipulation using modular rl
candidates.title: Dextrous tactile in-hand manipulation using modular RL
candidates.method_summary: Modular reinforcement learning approach to tactile in-hand manipulation that decomposes the policy into reusable contact-primitive modules (e.g., regrasp, slide, rotate) selected by a high-level controller. Uses tactile feedback as the primary state signal, sidestepping vision occlusion in cluttered finger configurations. Demonstrates cube and object reorientation on a multi-finger hand with improved sample efficiency over monolithic RL baselines.
limitations: Module library is hand-engineered, limiting generalization
Object set narrow (cubes/primitives)
Tactile signal model is coarse compared to high-resolution gels

group.affiliation: DLR / German robotics lab (Pitz et al.)

candidates.title: DiffTactile: A Physics-based Differentiable Tactile Simulator for Contact-Rich Robotic Manipulation

aliases.title: DiffTactile: A physics-based differentiable tactile simulator for contact-rich robotic manipulation
DiffTactile: A physics-based differentiable tactile simulator

candidates.method_summary: DiffTactile is a physics-based differentiable simulator for vision-based tactile sensors (e.g., GelSight). Models the elastomer as an FEM/MPM continuum so gradients flow from tactile images back through contact dynamics. Enables gradient-based policy and control optimization for contact-rich tasks like cable manipulation and tool use.
limitations: Computational cost of differentiable FEM/MPM
Sim-to-real gap for elastomer constitutive model
Limited to vision-based tactile, not magnetic/capacitive

candidates.canonical_id: title:digit gxo robots as a service
candidates.title: Digit × GXO: 최초의 상업적 휴머노이드 Robots-as-a-Service 배치
Digitizing Touch with an Artificial Multimodal Fingertip (Digit 360)
candidates.method_summary: Digit 360 is a hemispherical artificial fingertip integrating >18 sensing modalities (vision-based gel imaging, capacitive, IMU, microphone, thermal, gas) into a single 3D fingertip with on-device neural acceleration. It uses a fisheye camera plus 8M-pixel imaging at 70Hz to recover sub-millimeter contact geometry, normal/shear forces, and vibration. A built-in MCU runs touch-event neural detectors with sub-ms latency. Open-source designs are released alongside the platform.
limitations: Hemispherical form factor still bulky vs. human fingertip; integration onto multifinger hands non-trivial
Cost and manufacturing complexity high due to multimodal stack
Multimodal fusion algorithms still nascent; most downstream tasks use only a subset of modalities

candidates.title: DROID: A Large-Scale In-The-Wild Robot Manipulation Dataset

aliases.title: [DROID: A Large-Scale In-The-Wild Robot Manipulation Dataset](https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.12945). arXiv
[DROID](https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.12945). RSS
[DROID: A Large-Scale In-The-Wild Robot Manipulation Dataset](https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.12945). arXiv:2403.12945

candidates.method_summary: DROID is a 76K-trajectory single-arm Franka manipulation dataset collected in the wild across 564 different scenes, 86 institutions, and 12 months. All sites used a shared hardware setup (Franka + Robotiq gripper + 3 cameras + standardized teleop interface) so the resulting dataset is consistent in action conventions. DROID covers 86 task categories, with strong emphasis on diversity of scene, lighting, and task. The release includes pre-trained policies (BC, Diffusion Policy, RT-1) showing that DROID's diversity translates to better generalization than prior datasets of similar size.
limitations: Limited to single-arm Franka — no bimanual or mobile coverage
Many trajectories are short (<1 minute), capping long-horizon learning
Variable demonstrator quality across institutions
Most tasks are tabletop pick-and-place; contact-rich and tool-use under-represented
Action labeling consistency depends on each lab's calibration discipline

group.affiliation: Stanford + UC Berkeley + Toyota Research Institute + 86 partner institutions
group.funding: Stanford / Berkeley / TRI / Open X-Embodiment partners

candidates.method_summary: Electric Atlas specs: 56 DoF (hyperarticulation — 360° hips/waist/neck beyond human limits), ~1.5 m tall, 89 kg, 2.3 m reach, 50 kg payload, 4 h battery (autonomous swap). 85-90% electrical-to-mechanical efficiency; sub-10 cm foot placement precision. candidates.title: Electric Atlas: 56-DoF 전기 휴머노이드
Embodied-RAG: General Non-parametric Embodied Memory for Retrieval and Generation
candidates.method_summary: Embodied-RAG builds a hierarchical retrieval-augmented memory for embodied agents. As the robot explores, observations are summarized into nodes of a tree (objects → regions → semantic clusters) with text + visual descriptors. At query time, the agent retrieves relevant nodes via a combination of vector similarity and tree traversal, and the LLM generates a grounded answer or plan. The non-parametric structure means the memory can grow indefinitely without retraining, and the same store supports both spatial Q&A ('where did I see the red mug?') and planning ('go pick up the trash by the kitchen sink').
limitations: Hierarchy is built from VLM summaries — errors in early summaries propagate through retrieval
Most evaluation is in simulation; real-robot experiments are limited demos
Retrieval relies heavily on text descriptors; struggles with non-verbal cues (fine-grained appearance, sound)
No mechanism to forget or compress stale memory — long-term scale not demonstrated
LLM cost per query remains high (full prompt with retrieved nodes)

candidates.title: Expressive Whole-Body Control for Humanoid Robots
candidates.method_summary: 'ExBody' — decouples upper-body expressiveness from lower-body locomotion. Upper body tracks mocap (CMU Mocap + AMASS subset retargeted to H1), lower body trained with its own rough-terrain policy. Teacher-student distillation with privileged reference. Demonstrates Unitree H1 dancing, clapping, waving while walking on uneven ground.
limitations: Upper/lower decoupling limits whole-body coordination
No manipulation contact
Retargeting drops foot-penetration via clipping

survey_backrefs.chapter_hint: Ch6 Learning Canon, Ch10 VLA Integration

The policy outputs an action trajectory [formula] in an end-to-end manner: [formula] (1) where [formula] and [formula] denote the length of the observation history and the action trajectory, respectively. candidates.canonical_id: title:high throughput experimentation platform for drug discovery 10x acceleration
candidates.title: High-Throughput Experimentation Platform for Drug Discovery: 10x Acceleration
HOI-Based Cross-Embodiment Robot Learning
candidates.method_summary: Uses Hand-Object Interaction (HOI) representations as an intermediate embodiment-agnostic action space. Human demonstrations are abstracted into hand-object contact graphs and trajectories, which are then retargeted to multiple robot embodiments. Demonstrates cross-embodiment transfer across at least 2-3 robot platforms by pretraining on HOI data and fine-tuning on small robot sets.
limitations: HOI representation loses fine-grained force information
Retargeting to dexterous hands harder than to grippers
Author 'RoboWheel' is institutional placeholder; canonical citation unclear
Verification status: bibtex_backfill

candidates.title: HOVER: Versatile Neural Whole-Body Controller for Humanoid Robots
candidates.method_summary: 1.5-M-parameter unified whole-body controller that supports 15+ distinct control modes (joint PD, torque, IK, footstep command, root velocity, etc.) via policy distillation. Trains an oracle motion-imitation teacher on AMASS + large mocap, then distills a multi-mode student that can seamlessly switch interfaces at run-time. Deployed zero-shot on 19-DoF Unitree H1.
limitations: Relies on oracle imitation — no VLA conditioning
Mode taxonomy hand-defined
Manipulation contact not explicitly modeled

group.affiliation: NVIDIA + CMU (LeCAR/Shi) + UT Austin (Zhu) + UC Berkeley

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Ch10 §10.2 reuses standard RT-2 ~55B PaLI-X variant framing + sub-Hz rate. Community-standard citation.

candidates.title: Humanoid Locomotion as Next Token Prediction
candidates.method_summary: Recasts humanoid locomotion control as autoregressive next-token prediction: the model is a causal Transformer that predicts the next sensor-action token in a stream that multiplexes proprioception, velocity commands, and joint actions. Trains on mixed data: simulated rollouts, neural-network-generated trajectories, prior MPC rollouts, and even human YouTube videos (after retargeting). Zero-shot deployment on Digit in outdoor Berkeley streets.
limitations: Human-video tokens require heavy retargeting preprocess
Autoregressive latency can conflict with 1 kHz PD tracking
Did not scale to manipulation in this paper

candidates.title: Humanoid Parkour Learning
candidates.method_summary: Teacher-student RL with depth vision for humanoid parkour. Teacher trained with privileged terrain info; student distilled with depth camera. Unitree H1 jumps over gaps, vaults parallel bars, climbs platforms autonomously — first demonstration of vision-conditioned parkour on a bipedal humanoid.
limitations: No manipulation
Depth only (no RGB semantics)
Brittle to lighting changes

candidates.title: Humanoid-Gym: Reinforcement Learning for Humanoid Robot with Zero-Shot Sim2Real Transfer
candidates.method_summary: Open-source RL framework building on legged_gym (Isaac Gym) specialized for humanoids. Adds humanoid-specific rewards (stance, step period, upper-body regularization) and a sim-to-sim pipeline that validates Isaac Gym policies in MuJoCo before real deployment. Released trained policies for RobotEra XBot-S/XBot-L that walk zero-shot on flat ground and slopes.
limitations: No manipulation tasks
Ankle-specific rewards require tuning per embodiment
Perception excluded

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Identifier-deduplicated source linked in the annotated research trail; no new quantitative claim is made.

candidates.title: HumanoidBench: Simulated Humanoid Benchmark for Whole-Body Locomotion and Manipulation
candidates.method_summary: First open humanoid benchmark: Unitree H1 + two Shadow Hands in MuJoCo with 14 locomotion + 17 whole-body manipulation tasks (package unload, tool use, furniture assembly). Observation dim 151, action dim 61. Provides reference results for PPO, SAC, DreamerV3, TD-MPC2. Shows flat RL struggles without hierarchical policies.
limitations: Simulation-only
Shadow Hand specific — cross-hand transfer unclear
Reward shaping per task

candidates.title: HumanPlus: Humanoid Shadowing and Imitation from Humans
candidates.method_summary: End-to-end pipeline from single RGB human video → humanoid imitation on Unitree H1. Uses off-the-shelf human pose estimation (HumanPlus stack), retargets SMPL-X poses to H1, then shadowing policy maps kinematic reference to joint commands via a shadow reward in Isaac Gym. A separate Humanoid Imitation Transformer (HIT) supervises the policy from humans demonstrating by teleoperation.
limitations: Monocular pose noisy for feet
Single-arm gripper (not dexterous hand)
Heavy retargeting pipeline

candidates.title: KAIST Humanoid — 12 km/h Biped with HuboLab Lineage
candidates.method_summary: KAIST demonstrated a 12 km/h biped under Prof. Jemin Hwangbo (who co-authored the 2019 Science Robotics actuator-network paper). Positions KAIST as Korea's research engine for learning-based humanoids, distinct from Rainbow Robotics' commercial arm (spinout of earlier HUBO lab).

Short demos — no longitudinal evaluation
Commercialization path unclear

candidates.title: Kalib: Markerless Hand–Eye Calibration with Keypoint Tracking
candidates.method_summary: Tracks natural robot keypoints rather than requiring a calibration target and evaluates against DROID-style real data. This primary source was checked for the 2024 release or experiment and mapped to hand-eye, markerless, droid. It supports only the stated interface, specification, or measured setup; it does not by itself establish application safety or universal simulator fidelity.
limitations: Keypoint detector errors couple directly into extrinsics
Markerless convenience trades away known target geometry

candidates.method_summary: Kraft Heinz의 AI 에이전트 'The Cookbook'은 Azure OpenAI 기반으로 3개월 이내에 구축되었으며, 제품 개발 시간을 50% 단축하고 피클 생산 효율을 12% 향상시켰다. 레시피 최적화, 성분 대체, 소비자 맞춤화에 AI 에이전트를 활용한다.

candidates.title: Language-Grounded Dynamic Scene Graphs for Interactive Object Search with Mobile Manipulation
candidates.method_summary: This work extends 3D scene graphs to be dynamic and language-grounded for mobile manipulation. As the robot navigates and interacts, an open-vocabulary detector populates a hierarchical scene graph (rooms / furniture / objects) and updates it under change. An LLM consumes the textualized scene graph and natural-language search queries to plan navigation + interaction sequences (e.g., 'find my keys, then bring them to the desk'). The key contribution is an update mechanism that handles object placement/removal across long deployments.
limitations: Open-vocab detector errors directly degrade graph quality
Language-only LLM interface loses geometric precision
Restricted to indoor static-furniture environments
Robot-specific motion stack assumed (legged or wheeled mobile platform)
Limited evaluation under heavy clutter or many simultaneous changes

candidates.title: LBH Gripper: Linkage-Belt Based Hybrid Adaptive Gripper Design for Dish Collecting Robots
candidates.method_summary: Linkage-Belt Hybrid (LBH) gripper combines a four-bar linkage finger with conveyor-belt fingertip surfaces to enable both grasping and in-finger object transport. Targets dish collection in restaurants, where heterogeneous shapes and stacking are challenging. Belts on finger surfaces actively translate objects without releasing grip.
limitations: Application-specific (dish collection); limited generalization
Belt mechanism adds mechanical complexity
Non-anthropomorphic; not directly applicable to dexterous tasks

candidates.title: Learning Dexterity from Human Hand Motion in Internet Videos
candidates.method_summary: VideoDex-style approach learning dexterous manipulation priors from large-scale internet videos of human hands. Extracts hand pose and contact information at scale using off-the-shelf hand pose estimators, then retargets to dexterous robot hand. Demonstrates that internet-scale human video provides useful priors for robot dexterity.
limitations: Hand pose estimators have errors that propagate
No tactile/force info from videos
Embodiment gap between human and robot hand

candidates.title: Learning Human-to-Humanoid Real-Time Whole-Body Teleoperation
candidates.method_summary: H2O — real-time whole-body teleoperation of Unitree H1 from a single RGB camera observing the operator. An 'imitation sanitizer' removes infeasible retargeted motions; a teacher-student RL pipeline trains whole-body policies in Isaac Gym tracking retargeted references. Demonstrates real-time teleoperation with ~50 ms end-to-end latency.
limitations: No manipulation dexterity (gripper only)
Camera placement sensitivity
Imitation sanitizer is heuristic

candidates.title: Learning In-Hand Translation Using Tactile Skin With Shear and Normal Force Sensing

aliases.title: Learning in-hand translation using tactile skin with shear and normal force sensing

candidates.method_summary: Develops a magnetometer-based tactile skin (Meta AReal-style) that measures both normal and shear forces over a soft skin, mounted on a multi-finger hand. Trains in-hand translation (sliding) policies in simulation with privileged tactile observations, then transfers to real via student distillation that uses only the real skin signal. Shows that shear sensing is essential for robust slip-aware in-hand translation and that the policy generalizes across object shapes and masses.
limitations: Skin coverage limited to fingertips, not full hand
Magnetometer sensing susceptible to magnetic interference
Only translation studied, not full in-hand reorientation

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Matched to local BibTeX/reference index key.

candidates.title: Learning Quadrupedal Locomotion via Differentiable Simulation
candidates.method_summary: Uses differentiable simulation + analytic gradients to train quadruped locomotion policies. Sample efficiency 10-100× better than model-free PPO. Demonstrates RL for legged robots has alternatives to Isaac-Gym-PPO dominance. Points to a possible future axis alongside the four seed catalysts.
limitations: Contact differentiation noisy
Differentiable-sim stack less mature than Isaac Lab
Not yet demonstrated on humanoids at scale

candidates.title: Mirage: Cross-Embodiment Zero-Shot Transfer via Cross-Painting

aliases.title: Mirage: Cross-Painting Transfer

candidates.method_summary: Mirage achieves zero-shot cross-embodiment transfer by 'cross-painting' the source robot out of camera images and inpainting the target robot, allowing a policy trained on one robot to act on another without retraining. Uses Stable Diffusion-based inpainting and forward-kinematics-aware masking.
limitations: Relies on accurate forward kinematics and camera calibration for cross-painting
Inpainting artifacts degrade for occluded contact-rich phases
Limited to vision-based policies; no tactile transfer

candidates.title: ML-based wearable sensors for real-time hand motion recognition
candidates.method_summary: Survey-style work on wearable sensor systems (IMU, EMG, flex sensors, capacitive arrays) combined with ML classifiers for real-time hand gesture and motion recognition. Reports typical recognition accuracies of 90-98% on 5-15 gesture classes using CNN/LSTM models on multi-modal sensor fusion. Discusses calibration drift, cross-subject generalization, and latency (<50 ms typical). Positions wearable hand-tracking as a complementary data source to vision-based MoCap for robot learning.
limitations: Closed-set gesture vocabularies, limited generalization to free-form manipulation
Sensor drift and per-subject calibration overhead
Lacks force/contact information critical for dexterous manipulation

candidates.title: MultiGrasp: Multi-Object Grasping with Dexterous Hands
candidates.method_summary: MultiGrasp tackles dexterous multi-object grasping — picking up multiple objects in a single grasp — via a learned generative model over hand poses conditioned on cluttered object scenes. The method combines a diffusion-based grasp synthesis network with simulation-based filtering to produce stable multi-finger configurations holding 2-4 objects simultaneously. Evaluated on Shadow/Allegro hands in sim and real, it shows that multi-object grasping increases throughput in clutter sweep tasks.
limitations: Lossy sim-to-real transfer
No tactile feedback during grasp execution
Object set limited to YCB-scale rigid items
Failure recovery not addressed

limitations: Custom sensor not commercially available
Limited to lab housekeeping scenarios
Slow thermal response time

candidates.title: Natural Language Can Help Bridge the Sim2Real Gap
candidates.method_summary: This work proposes that natural-language descriptions of scenes (generated by a VLM) act as a domain-invariant intermediate representation that helps policies bridge the sim-to-real gap. A VLM produces text captions describing the scene; a policy network conditions on these captions instead of (or alongside) raw pixels. Because text captions tend to abstract over rendering / texture / lighting differences, the policy generalizes from sim-trained captions to real-world captions more reliably than pixel-conditioned baselines.
limitations: VLM caption quality bounds the upper limit of policy generalization
Text representation loses fine-grained geometric detail necessary for some tasks
Tested on a small task suite; broader claims require larger-scale validation
Caption generation latency limits real-time control
Choice of VLM (CLIP / BLIP-2) qualitatively affects results

candidates.title: Neo Gamma 휴머노이드와 NVIDIA GR00T 통합
NeuralFeels with Neural Fields: Visuotactile Perception for In-Hand Manipulation
candidates.method_summary: NeuralFeels uses online neural fields (SDF + appearance) to fuse vision and DIGIT vision-based tactile readings into a continuously updated 3D object model during in-hand manipulation. Tactile contact patches act as dense local geometry constraints that refine the field where vision is occluded. The system jointly tracks object pose and reconstructs shape on a 16-DoF Allegro hand, demonstrating dramatic accuracy gains under visual occlusion.
limitations: Requires accurate sensor calibration and contact patch reconstruction from gel images
Online neural field optimization computationally heavy (single-GPU bound)
Limited to rigid objects; deformable/articulated objects not addressed

OpenVLA: An Open-Source Vision-Language-Action Model

candidates.method_summary: 7-B open-weights VLA built on Llama-2 with a fused DINOv2 + SigLIP visual encoder. Trained on 970k real-robot trajectories from the Open X-Embodiment collection. Predicts discretized 7-DoF end-effector deltas (xyz + rpy + gripper). Outperforms Diffusion Policy and RT-2-X by 20.4% in absolute success rate on held-out tasks while being fully reproducible (model, data, fine-tune code released).
limitations: Stationary arm only — no bipedal locomotion in original
Discretized action tokens limit continuous control fidelity
Latency ~100 ms per action on consumer GPU

candidates.title: Real-world Humanoid Locomotion with Reinforcement Learning
candidates.method_summary: Demonstrates fully-learned outdoor locomotion on a full-size humanoid (Digit). Policy is a causal Transformer over a long window of proprioceptive observations and past actions, trained in Isaac Gym with large-scale domain randomization. Key finding: increasing context length from 1 to 16 steps monotonically improves robustness; teacher imitation + RL joint training outperforms either alone; policies deployed zero-shot across San Francisco streets, grass, slopes.
limitations: Locomotion only — no manipulation
Digit's passive-ankle lineage makes transfer to Atlas/G1 nontrivial
Transformer context is quadratic — limits deployment horizon

candidates.method_summary: Demonstrates fully-learned outdoor locomotion on a full-size humanoid (Digit). Policy is a causal Transformer over a long window of proprioceptive observations and past actions, trained in Isaac Gym with large-scale domain randomization. Key finding: increasing context length from 1 to 16 steps monotonically improves robustness; teacher imitation + RL joint training outperforms either alone; policies deployed zero-shot across San Francisco streets, grass, slopes.
limitations: Locomotion only — no manipulation
Digit's passive-ankle lineage makes transfer to Atlas/G1 nontrivial
Transformer context is quadratic — limits deployment horizon
