Limited to static grasp analysis (not dynamic manipulation)

candidates.title: Residual RL
Reward Machine Inference for Robotic Manipulation
candidates.method_summary: DQN on the Inferred Reward Machine Figure 2: Overview of our DQN formulation. Each action consists of a picking operation at pixel coordinates [formula] followed by a placing operation at [formula] . The Q-function [formula] is modeled using two FCN’s: [formula] and [formula] which generate pixel-wise Q-value maps for their respective actions. The action space is defined by a pick-and-place primitive, parameterized by four pixel coordinates: two for the pick location [formula] and two for the place location [formula] . Through camera calibration and depth measurements, each pixel coordinate is mapped accurately to the robot’s coordinate system, ensuring precise execution of these actions. For RM inference, images are captured from a front-view camera, while control actions use a top-down view of the workspace. The top-down perspective aligns directly with the workspace plane, reducing distortion and occlusions and facilitating accurate pixel-to-coordinate mappings. In contrast, front-view images are used for inferring sub-goals, as they avoid occlusions caused by the robot and provide a fuller view of the workspace. Inspired by prior work in robotic manipulation (Camacho et al. 2021 ; Zeng et al. 2022 ) , we define our deep Q-function [formula] using two fully convolutional networks (FCNs) (Long, Shelhamer, and Darrell 2015 ) , [formula] and [formula] . Each FCN receives a top-down image and outputs a dense, pixel-wise map of Q-values for all pick-and-place actions. To stabil Model, world-model, or agent output is a bounded proposal above versioned skill APIs, feasibility projection, real-time control, independent evaluation, collision/force gates, protective stop, E-stop, and human authority.
limitations: Unlike existing approaches, our method does not rely on predefined propositions.

A promising direction for future work is to develop agents that can adaptively select alternative paths, which would improve robustness in environments where certain paths become infeasible due to perturbations (e.g., a block becomes unavailable).

Placement error comparisons show parity between our inferred RM and the ground truth RM agent, suggesting that any remaining error is primarily due to the control algorithm handling the pick-and-place actions.
Primary abstract does not establish the full robot/task/data/adaptation/reset denominator needed for cross-paper comparison.
No default-tabletop real-hardware evidence was established from the inspected abstract.

candidates.title: RoboEXP: Action-Conditioned Scene Graph via Interactive Exploration for Robotic Manipulation
candidates.method_summary: RoboEXP builds an action-conditioned scene graph through interactive exploration. The robot autonomously perturbs scenes (e.g., opening drawers, lifting objects) and a VLM-based perception module records the resulting state changes as edges in a scene graph (e.g., 'cup is_inside drawer1', 'drawer1 contains_after_open cup'). The graph captures hidden affordances revealed only through interaction, enabling downstream LLM planners to reason about objects that are initially occluded or hidden inside containers.
limitations: Exploration policy is heuristic — doesn't learn to explore optimally
VLM perception errors propagate into the scene graph
Time cost of interactive exploration is high (minutes per scene)
Restricted to known interaction primitives (open, lift, push)
Tested on a small task suite

candidates.title: Robot intelligent grasping based on tactile perception
candidates.method_summary: Review of tactile-perception-driven intelligent grasping, covering tactile sensors (resistive, capacitive, optical, magnetic), grasp planning with tactile feedback, slip detection, and material/object recognition. candidates.title: Robot Synesthesia: In-Hand Manipulation with Visuotactile Sensing

aliases.title: Robot Synesthesia: In-hand manipulation with visuotactile sensing

candidates.method_summary: Robot Synesthesia learns in-hand object reorientation policies that fuse visual point clouds with tactile point clouds from soft fingertip sensors on an Allegro hand. Tactile data is converted into a 3D point representation that shares geometric structure with vision, enabling unified PointNet-style fusion. Trained in simulation with sim-to-real transfer.
limitations: Tactile fingertip coverage limited to fingertips only
Sim-to-real gap for soft contact dynamics
Allegro-specific - retargeting to other hands non-trivial

candidates.title: RT-H: Action Hierarchies Using Language
candidates.method_summary: RT-H introduces an explicit two-level action hierarchy via language. A high-level policy predicts a 'language motion' (e.g., 'move arm forward', 'rotate gripper') given the task and image. A low-level policy is conditioned on this language motion to predict the actual robot action. Crucially, the language layer makes interventions easy: a human can correct the high-level language during execution and the low-level policy will follow the corrected motion. The model is trained jointly on the same RT-1/RT-2 data with annotated language motions.
limitations: Requires language-motion annotations; harder to scale than raw teleop
Language motion vocabulary is finite and hand-curated
Two-level decoding adds latency
Evaluated on Google Robot only; cross-embodiment transfer not shown
Like RT-2, weights closed-source

Sparsh: Self-supervised touch representations

candidates.method_summary: Sparsh is a self-supervised foundation-model-style encoder for vision-based tactile sensors (DIGIT, GelSight, ReSkin). Pretrained on 460K+ tactile images using masked autoencoding and contrastive objectives. Provides a unified representation that transfers across sensor types and downstream tasks (force, slip, pose).
limitations: Vision-based tactile only - excludes capacitive/magnetic
Data dominated by Meta-collected sources
Force regression still benefits from sensor-specific calibration

candidates.canonical_id: title:task oriented residual rl for dexterous manipulation transfer
candidates.title: Task-Oriented Residual RL for Dexterous Manipulation Transfer
candidates.method_summary: DexH2R proposes a task-oriented residual RL framework for transferring dexterous manipulation skills from human demonstrations to robot hands. A base policy is initialized from retargeted human motion, then a residual RL policy corrects for embodiment gap and physics differences using small amounts of robot interaction. The residual structure stabilizes training and recovers performance lost in retargeting.
limitations: Requires residual RL exploration on real robot (sample-expensive)
Performance ceiling bounded by base policy quality
Bibtex_key null — likely placeholder for an actual published DexH2R paper
Tactile signals not central to the formulation

candidates.canonical_id: title:task oriented residual rl for dexterous transfer
candidates.title: Task-Oriented Residual RL for Dexterous Transfer
TinyVLA: Towards Fast, Data-Efficient Vision-Language-Action Models for Robotic Manipulation
candidates.method_summary: TinyVLA is a compact (<1B parameter) VLA built by attaching a diffusion action head to a small VLM backbone (Pythia-1.4B / Phi-2). It avoids large autoregressive decoding by emitting continuous action chunks via diffusion, allowing real-time inference (~25 Hz) on commodity GPUs. The training recipe relies on data-efficient supervised fine-tuning from minimal robot demonstrations (~hundreds per task) without OXE-scale pre-training on robot data, instead leveraging VLM pretraining for visual-linguistic priors.
limitations: Skips large-scale robot pretraining, which limits zero-shot cross-task generalization
Diffusion head requires multi-step denoising at inference
Smaller VLM backbone limits emergent reasoning capability
Mostly evaluated on single-arm tabletop tasks
Per-task fine-tune is still required for novel domains

group.affiliation: Midea Group / East China Normal University

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Canonical title, authors, arXiv:2409.12514v5 identity, and simulation plus real-robot evaluation verified. TinyVLA speed/data-efficiency results do not establish observation age, queues, controller deadlines, watchdogs, stale rejection, or outage behavior.

candidates.title: Touch100k: A Large-Scale Touch-Language-Vision Dataset for Touch-Centric Multimodal Representation
candidates.method_summary: Touch100k is a large-scale dataset of 100K+ touch-language-vision triplets, where each tactile sample is paired with natural-language descriptions and visual context. Enables pretraining of multimodal touch-language models analogous to CLIP. Targets touch-centric multimodal representation learning.
limitations: Language descriptions may be noisy or inconsistent
Restricted to vision-based tactile sensors
Real-world manipulation downstream evaluation limited

candidates.title: Towards Generalist Robot Policies: What Matters in Building Vision-Language-Action Models
candidates.method_summary: An empirical study exploring the design space of VLA models. The paper systematically ablates (1) backbone choice (Llama-2 vs PaliGemma vs Phi), (2) action representation (per-dim binning vs FAST tokens vs diffusion vs flow matching), (3) data mixture composition (OXE subsets, DROID, Bridge), and (4) fine-tuning recipes (full / LoRA / partial). The goal is a recipe for building generalist VLAs and an analysis of which factors actually move the needle on real-world success.
limitations: Ablation budget is constrained — full grid would require massive compute
Real-world evaluation on a small task suite; broader generalization claims rely on SIMPLER
No exploration of multi-modal sensors (force, audio)
Findings tied to current OXE composition — likely shift as data grows
Closed-loop robustness and recovery not separately measured

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Canonical title, authors, arXiv:2412.14058v4 identity, and CALVIN ablation scope verified. The study compares multiple backbones and policy structures under stated settings; it does not turn heterogeneous public results into a universal leaderboard.

candidates.title: Towards Synergistic, Generalized, and Efficient Dual-System for Robotic Manipulation
candidates.method_summary: 3.1 Generalist: An Auto-regressive Vision-Language-Action Model Figure 2 (a) demonstrates our generalist’s architecture. Our generalist model is built upon OpenVLA (Kim et al., 2024 ) , a 7B-parameter autoregressive vision-language-action model trained with a large corpus of robotic manipulation data, including Open-X-Embodiment (Padalkar et al., 2024 ) , Bridge V2 (Walke et al., 2023 ) , DROID (Khazatsky et al., 2024 ) , etc . The generalist model follows the architecture of Prismatic-7B (Karamcheti et al., 2024 ) vision-language model (VLM), which consists of a fused visual encoder from different backbones (Zhai et al., 2023a ; Oquab et al., 2024 ) , a projection layer to align visual embeddings with language modality, and a large language model LLaMA2 (Touvron et al., 2023 ) . Despite extensive training on a large-scale cross-embodiment dataset, OpenVLA is not capable of functioning in a zero-shot manner in new environments or embodiments (Wang et al., 2024b ) . Adaptation to our specific robotic setup and test environments (with novel coordination system, camera angle, etc. ) remains necessary, which we accomplish through LoRA (Hu et al., 2022 ) fine-tuning. Nonetheless, we intend to leverage the massive pre-trained knowledge embedded in OpenVLA to endow our dual-system framework with certain generalizability. Autoregressive generation of action chunking. Following RT-2 (Brohan et al., 2023a ) and OpenVLA (Kim et al., 2024 ) , we map the least used 256 words in the LLaMA  Model, world-model, or agent output is a bounded proposal above versioned skill APIs, feasibility projection, real-time control, independent evaluation, collision/force gates, protective stop, E-stop, and human authority.
limitations: 5 Conclusion and Future Work We present RoboDual, a synergistic dual-system for robotic manipulation that capitalizes the generalizability of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models alongside the efficiency and adaptability of specialist policies.

By fostering synergistic cooperation, RoboDual effectively addresses several limitations inherent in existing VLAs, offering a cost-effective and widely adaptable solution for the practical deployment of large generalist policies.
Limitations and future work.

candidates.title: VeriGraph: Scene Graphs for Execution Verifiable Robot Planning
candidates.method_summary: VeriGraph couples an LLM/VLM planner with a structured scene graph that captures objects, their attributes (color, pose, container relations), and pairwise relations. The LLM proposes a candidate plan as a sequence of edits to this scene graph; a verifier checks each step for preconditions and goal satisfaction in the graph before a low-level controller executes the corresponding action. Failures in verification trigger replanning. The approach makes plan correctness checkable in symbolic space without requiring full physics simulation.
limitations: Scene graph extraction relies on accurate object detection and pose estimation; brittle on cluttered or novel scenes
Symbolic verification cannot capture continuous physics (e.g., friction, slip) — succeeds on tabletop pick-and-place but harder for contact-rich tasks
Current evaluation limited to short-horizon tasks (≤10 steps)
Hand-defined predicate vocabulary; not learned from data
VLM cost per replan limits real-time reactivity

group.affiliation: University of Maryland, College Park (Shrivastava lab)

candidates.title: Visual Whole-Body Control for Legged Loco-Manipulation
candidates.method_summary: Whole-body loco-manipulation policy for a quadruped + manipulator. Uses egocentric vision + proprioception. Serves as a template for humanoid-style whole-body visuomotor policies on a simpler embodiment. Highlights the shift from separate-arm + separate-base control.
limitations: Quadruped not humanoid
Single arm
Contact-rich manipulation still shallow

candidates.canonical_id: title:vitam a high resolution vision based tactile sensor
candidates.title: ViTaM: A High-Resolution Vision-Based Tactile Sensor
candidates.method_summary: ViTaM is a vision-based tactile glove/sensor that pairs high-resolution optical tactile imaging (GelSight-style elastomer) with a wearable form factor for whole-hand contact capture. By distributing miniaturized cameras under gel pads across fingertip and palm regions, ViTaM reconstructs sub-millimeter contact geometry and shear at >300 Hz. The system targets hand-object interaction datasets and policy learning where dense contact patches are critical.
limitations: Wired tethering and bulk reduce ergonomic naturalness
Computational overhead for multi-camera processing
Calibration drift across gel deformation cycles
Limited public dataset release

candidates.title: A bioinspired bidirectional stiffening soft actuator for multimodal, compliant, and robust grasping
candidates.method_summary: Bioinspired soft actuator that stiffens bidirectionally — both during flexion and extension — using antagonistic layered chambers. Enables single-finger modules to switch between soft conforming and rigid load-bearing states without external jamming media, achieving multi-modal grasping (pinch, wrap, push) with one design.
limitations: Pneumatic-only, no vacuum-jamming hybrid robustness
Limited sensing — purely mechanical compliance
Lab-bench evaluation only

group.affiliation: Likely Chinese Academy of Sciences or Tsinghua/HKU group (typical for Soft Robotics 2023)

candidates.title: A Soft Robotic Glove with Integrated Sensing and Haptic Feedback
candidates.method_summary: Zhu et al. present a wearable soft glove integrating 15 IMUs (one per finger segment) for joint-angle estimation and 14 Velostat-based pressure taxels for binary contact/force sensing across the five fingers. The glove pairs the dual-modality wearable with a soft pneumatic exoskeleton providing haptic feedback for VR teleoperation. It is one of the few pre-2024 systems that simultaneously measures joint angle and tactile contact in a single wearable, but Velostat resistive films yield low-resolution, sparse, normal-force-only readings (no shear) and the system was not used to train end-to-end manipulation policies.
limitations: Velostat taxels are essentially binary — cannot resolve continuous force or shear
Only 14 pressure points across 5 fingers — far below contact-rich manipulation needs
Never connected to robot policy learning — purely a teleoperation/VR device
Haptic feedback is open-loop pneumatic — slow bandwidth, limited fidelity
Lacks palm sensing entirely; no fingertip 3-axis force

candidates.title: A Survey on Large Language Models for Automated Planning

aliases.title: [AutoTAMP: Autoregressive Task and Motion Planning with LLMs as Translators and Checkers](https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.06531)

candidates.method_summary: A 2025 survey covering the role of LLMs in automated planning, organized along axes of (1) LLMs as direct planners, (2) LLMs as translators to formal languages (PDDL, STL), (3) LLMs as planning heuristics or verifiers, and (4) LLMs integrated with classical search. The survey catalogs benchmark suites (Blocksworld, Robotouille, ALFWorld, etc.), failure modes (hallucination, long-horizon decay, grounding), and trends from 2022–2025 in robotics and general AI planning.
limitations: Survey scope is broad — robotics-specific depth is moderate compared with planning AI
2025 cutoff already missing latest 2025–2026 VLA-as-planner work
Limited treatment of failure-recovery and embodiment grounding
Largely text-domain benchmarks; physical sim-to-real planning issues underrepresented
No standardized comparison protocol across surveyed methods

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Canonical title/authors/ICRA identity verified. Primary paper supports language-to-formal-specification translation and syntactic/semantic checking in its stated simulation/planning scope; it does not validate the full S13 skill contract.
Identifier-deduplicated source linked in the annotated research trail; no new quantitative claim is made.

candidates.title: A variable stiffness robotic gripper based on parallel beam with vision-based force sensing
candidates.method_summary: A 2-finger gripper with parallel-beam variable-stiffness mechanism whose deformation is monitored by an internal camera (vision-based force sensing). Stiffness is tuned by mechanical preload, and contact force is reconstructed from observed beam deflection — eliminating dedicated force/torque sensors.
limitations: Vision-based force sensing limited by lighting and occlusion
Bandwidth restricted by camera frame rate
Only 2-finger pinch grasps demonstrated

group.affiliation: Purdue University / Beijing Institute of Technology

candidates.title: AnyTeleop: A General Vision-Based Dexterous Robot Arm-Hand Teleoperation System
candidates.method_summary: AnyTeleop is a vision-based teleoperation framework for arbitrary arm-hand combinations. A single RGB camera streams human hand pose; an optimization-based retargeting solver matches keypoints to any robot hand morphology, supporting cross-embodiment teleop without hardware-specific calibration. Validated on 4 robot hands and 6 simulators.
limitations: No haptic/tactile feedback to operator
Relies on accurate hand pose estimation; struggles under occlusion
Optimization latency limits high-frequency dynamic tasks

group.affiliation: UC San Diego / NVIDIA / University of Washington

candidates.title: Code as Policies: Language Model Programs for Embodied Control

aliases.title: [Code as Policies: Language Model Programs for Embodied Control](https://arxiv.org/abs/2209.07753)
Code as Policies: Language Model Programs for Embodied Control
[Code as Policies: Language Model Programs for Embodied Control](https://arxiv.org/abs/2209.07753). ICRA. arXiv:2209.07753

candidates.method_summary: Reframes LLM-based robot control as code generation: given a natural-language instruction and a set of perception/control APIs (e.g., get_obj_pos, move_to_pose, place_at), an LLM (Codex / GPT-3.5) writes Python policy code that calls those APIs, including loops, conditionals, recursive function definitions, and arithmetic — yielding 'language model programs' (LMPs). Hierarchical Code-as-Policies allows the LLM to define helper functions on the fly when an API is missing. Few-shot prompting with carefully curated example programs is the only training signal; no robot data is required.
limitations: Authors note: relies on a curated set of perception+control APIs — silent failure if an API returns wrong values.
Authors note: long programs increase brittleness; one syntactic error breaks the whole policy.
Authors note: prompting is brittle; performance varies with example selection.
Reader-side: closed-loop reactivity is limited — programs run open-loop unless the prompt explicitly asks for sensor checks inside a while-loop.
Reader-side: assumes Codex-class code competence; smaller open models perform substantially worse.

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Primary arXiv/ICRA paper verified; its API-composition results do not establish safe generated control deployment.

Canonical title/authors/ICRA identity verified. Primary paper exposes prompted function/API namespaces and documented code/API failures; it does not validate the full S13 version/timeout/fallback contract.
Title, author list, ICRA 2023 venue, and arXiv ID 2209.07753 verified against the canonical arXiv record.

candidates.method_summary: Represents robot visuomotor policy as a conditional denoising diffusion process (DDPM or DDIM) over action sequences. Uses receding-horizon chunks of 8-16 actions, conditioned on recent observations via a 1D CNN or Transformer. Handles multimodal demonstrations gracefully (e.g., left vs right push-T) where MSE-trained behavior cloning collapses to the mean. Released open-source pipeline that became the default BC baseline for humanoid and manipulation papers through 2024-2025.
limitations: Inference cost (multiple denoising steps, ~100 ms)
Chunked actions can diverge under replanning
Requires careful horizon/step tuning

[Open X-Embodiment: Robotic Learning Datasets and RT-X Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.08864)

candidates.method_summary: Open X-Embodiment (OXE) is a large-scale collaboration that aggregates real-robot demonstration data across 22 institutions and 22 robot embodiments into a unified RLDS-format dataset of >1M trajectories spanning 527 skills. The paper also introduces RT-1-X (35M params, scratch-trained on the mixture) and RT-2-X (55B params, fine-tuned from RT-2 on the mixture) as cross-embodiment generalist policies, demonstrating positive transfer: training on the union improves performance on each contributor's home tasks compared to training only on that institution's own data.
limitations: Heterogeneous data quality, action conventions, and observation schemes — heavy preprocessing required
Heavily biased toward single-arm tabletop manipulation; mobile/dexterous/humanoid coverage thin
Most data are short demonstrations, limiting long-horizon learning
Even at >1M episodes, scale is small relative to internet-scale multimodal pretraining
Cross-embodiment transfer evaluation is uneven and dependent on contributor evaluations

candidates.title: PaLM-E: An Embodied Multimodal Language Model
candidates.method_summary: PaLM-E injects continuous sensor observations (RGB images via ViT, object-centric scene representations, robot proprioception) directly into the embedding space of a frozen-or-finetuned PaLM language model by training a small projection from each modality to the LM token space. The result is a single decoder-only transformer (largest variant: PaLM-562B = PaLM-540B + ViT-22B) that handles VQA, image captioning, and robot task planning end-to-end, generating text outputs that include skill names or robot plans. Training is a mixture of internet vision-language data and three robot embodiments: TAMP block manipulation, Language-Table tabletop, and a real Everyday Robots mobile manipulator.
limitations: Authors note: does not produce low-level continuous actions — it outputs high-level skill names that a separate low-level policy must execute.
Authors note: catastrophic forgetting if VL pretraining data is removed; co-training is essential.
Authors note: 562B parameters → infeasible to deploy onboard; inference latency is prohibitive for real-time control.
Reader-side: closed-source model + closed Everyday Robots data → not reproducible.
Reader-side: planning horizon limited by single-prompt context; no explicit memory mechanism.

group.affiliation: Google Research (Robotics at Google + Brain) + TU Berlin

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Canonical arXiv identity and chapter relevance verified against the independent S13 papers/source ledger; supplementary coverage only, with no denominator, capability, or authority transfer.

candidates.title: Rotating without Seeing: Towards In-hand Dexterity through Touch
candidates.method_summary: Trains an in-hand object rotation policy using only proprioception and tactile signals (no vision) on an Allegro hand with 16 binary tactile pads. PPO in IsaacGym with massive domain randomization, then zero-shot transferred. Key result: touch-only policy achieves rotation rates competitive with vision-based baselines on diverse objects in the dark.
limitations: Binary tactile pads provide only contact/no-contact; no force gradient
Single-axis rotation task; not full reorientation to target pose
Sim-to-real gap mitigated only by extensive randomization

candidates.method_summary: Google DeepMind RT-2: co-trains a PaLI-X VLM on robot action data + web image-text, emitting actions as text tokens. candidates.title: SayPlan: Grounding Large Language Models using 3D Scene Graphs for Scalable Robot Task Planning
candidates.method_summary: SayPlan grounds LLM planners in pre-built 3D Scene Graphs (3DSG) — hierarchical graphs whose nodes are buildings/floors/rooms/objects with affordance attributes — to enable long-horizon, large-scale (multi-floor) planning. Three innovations: (1) semantic search via collapsible scene-graph API where GPT-4 explores only the subgraph relevant to the instruction (rather than dumping the whole graph in-context), (2) iterative replanning with a classical path planner (Dijkstra) that grounds navigation feasibility into the LLM loop, and (3) a verification step where the LLM checks its own plan against the graph before issuing actions. The system handles instructions like 'find a place to leave a glass of milk that won't spoil' across 28-room office buildings.
limitations: Authors note: assumes a pre-built, accurate 3D scene graph — no online graph construction or correction.
Authors note: replanning loop adds latency; multiple GPT-4 calls per instruction.
Authors note: dynamic scenes (objects move) are not explicitly handled.
Reader-side: depends on a separate 3DSG construction pipeline (e.g., Hydra, Concept Graphs) — not end-to-end.
Reader-side: skill primitives (pick, place, open) are still hand-defined; SayPlan only chooses the sequence.

group.affiliation: Queensland University of Technology (QUT) — Australian Centre for Robotic Vision
group.funding: Australian Research Council; QUT Centre for Robotics

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: SayPlan CoRL 2023 / arXiv:2307.06135 verified. 3D scene-graph planning inherits observation, staleness, geometry, and execution limits.

candidates.title: Do As I Can, Not As I Say: Grounding Language in Robotic Affordances

aliases.title: [Do As I Can, Not As I Say: Grounding Language in Robotic Affordances](https://arxiv.org/abs/2204.01691)

candidates.method_summary: SayCan grounds LLM-based task planning in robotic affordances by combining two scores per candidate skill: (a) the LLM's probability that the skill is a useful next step given the natural-language instruction (the 'Say' score, computed as token likelihood of the skill description under PaLM-540B/FLAN), and (b) a learned per-skill value/affordance function (the 'Can' score) trained via TD-based RL plus behavior cloning that estimates the probability that the skill will succeed in the current visual state. The product of the two scores selects the next primitive at each step; execution iterates until the LLM proposes a 'done' token. The skill library is ~551 manipulation+navigation primitives executed on a mobile manipulator (Everyday Robots).
limitations: Authors note: skill library is closed and hand-engineered; system cannot invent new primitives.
Authors note: affordance value function must be trained per skill, limiting scalability.
Authors note: LLM has no real-time perceptual feedback during a skill — replanning happens only at skill boundaries.
Reader-side: 'Can' scores can be miscalibrated, leading to safe-but-wrong skill selection.
Reader-side: requires Everyday Robots hardware + extensive teleop data; not reproducible by typical labs.

group.affiliation: Google Research / Everyday Robots (Robotics at Google)
group.lead_author: Michael Ahn (co-led with Anthony Brohan, Brian Ichter, Karol Hausman)

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Canonical SayCan title/authors/CoRL identity verified. Used as a semantic/affordance-grounding example; affordance scores are not collision or contact-stability evidence.
Identifier-deduplicated source linked in the annotated research trail; no new quantitative claim is made.

candidates.title: Ego4D: Around the World in 3,000 Hours of Egocentric Video
candidates.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR) 2022
candidates.method_summary: Ego4D is a massive egocentric video dataset: ~3,670 hours from 931 participants in 9 countries, capturing daily activities with head-mounted cameras. Bundled with 5 benchmark suites (episodic memory, hands-and-objects, audio-visual diarization, social interaction, forecasting) — becomes the foundation dataset for hand-object understanding in vision and robotics.
limitations: Camera-only — no tactile or proprioceptive data
Hand pose annotations limited to subset (Hands-and-Objects subset)
Strong camera-mount and demographic biases

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: arXiv 2110.07058 confirmed (CVPR 2022).

candidates.title: Flow Matching for Generative Modeling

aliases.title: Flow matching for generative modeling

candidates.method_summary: Introduces Flow Matching, a simulation-free objective that trains continuous normalizing flows by regressing onto a target conditional vector field (e.g. optimal-transport probability paths). Subsumes diffusion as a special case while allowing straighter, faster trajectories — later adopted in robotics as the action-decoder backbone for VLAs (pi0, pi0.5).
limitations: Original paper focuses on images, not robot actions
Choice of probability path requires careful tuning
No conditioning study for high-dim multimodal control

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Matched to local BibTeX/reference index key.

Original Flow Matching abstract/formulation verified: continuous normalizing flows are trained by regressing vector fields of fixed conditional probability paths and sampled with numerical ODE solvers. This generic objective does not establish robot latency, feasibility, or success.

candidates.title: [Characterization of the human skin resistome and identification of two microbiota cutotypes (iHSMGC catalog)](https://microbiomejournal.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s40168-020-00995-7)

aliases.title: [Characterization of the human skin resistome and identification of two microbiota cutotypes](https://microbiomejournal.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s40168-020-00995-7)
[Characterization of the human skin resistome and identification of two microbiota cutotypes (iHSMGC)](https://microbiomejournal.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s40168-020-00995-7)

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: VERIFIED. Li et al. 2021 *Microbiome* 9:47 (DOI 10.1186/s40168-020-00995-7). Catalog: 10,930,638 genes total; 4,879,024 new genes (44.6% novel) from sequencing 822 Han Chinese + 538 prior North American samples. Two cutotypes: Moraxella osloensis-type vs C. acnes-type. Ch2/Ch3 framing of '~45% novel East-Asian-specific genes' is technically defensible (44.6% rounded; 'novel' in the sense of 'absent from prior reference catalogs which were largely Western'). Ch3's '~45% of skin genes that HMP missed in an East-Asian cohort' is slightly loose — strictly the 4.88M new genes are relative to the prior combined catalogs, not HMP alone, but the directional claim holds.

candidates.title: Blind Bipedal Stair Traversal via Sim-to-Real Reinforcement Learning
candidates.method_summary: First fully-learned bipedal RL policy deployed zero-shot on a commercial humanoid (Cassie). Uses LSTM policy over proprioceptive history, trained in MuJoCo with domain randomization over terrain slope, stair dimensions, mass, and actuator gains. limitations: Blind (no perception)
Stair height assumed within 10-20 cm range
Cassie leaf-spring compliance at shin/tarsus (see hurst2019cassie for q5/q6 mechanical detail) — harder to extend to articulated-ankle humanoids

candidates.method_summary: 코스맥스 CAI(Cosmetic AI) 연구소는 2021년 설립되어 뷰티 AI 기술 3대 축—컬러 매칭 AI, 향 예측 AI(8,600+ 분자 DB), 성분 최적화 AI—을 개발한다. AI가 수천 가지 색상 조합 중 소비자 피부톤에 최적화된 색상을 추천하고, 후각 분자의 감각 속성을 예측하여 향 개발 기간을 단축한다.
limitations: 감각 데이터의 주관성으로 AI 예측 한계

candidates.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning at the Edge of the Statistical Precipice

aliases.title: [Deep Reinforcement Learning at the Edge of the Statistical Precipice](https://arxiv.org/abs/2108.13264)

candidates.method_summary: Shows that few-run deep-RL comparisons produce unstable point estimates and proposes stratified bootstrap intervals and performance profiles. The inspected 2021 primary source keeps this component's outputs at its stated abstraction level and does not confer torque, collision, force-limit, protective-stop, E-stop, or final human authority. For S13, the method is evidence for statistics, evaluation, negative-result and is compared against the unchanged S11/S12 skill, feasibility, controller, evaluator, and rollback contracts.
limitations: Independent runs do not address correlated dataset overlap
Robot trials often have even smaller and non-iid denominators

group.funding: not extracted; inspect acknowledgements before funding-sensitive use

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Canonical statistical-precipice title, authors, NeurIPS identity, and arXiv:2108.13264 verified. Interval/aggregate recommendations address RL benchmark uncertainty; small non-iid robot trials need additional design.

candidates.title: GelSight Wedge: Measuring High-Resolution 3D Contact Geometry with a Compact Robot Finger
candidates.method_summary: Wedge-shaped GelSight variant designed as a slim parallel-jaw fingerpad; uses a tilted gel face plus mirrored optics to fit camera into a finger-sized housing. Recovers high-res 3D depth and shear at ~25µm resolution. Enables tactile feedback in standard parallel grippers without bulky add-ons.
limitations: Wedge geometry biases contact area to one face
Camera framerate (~30-90Hz) limits high-speed manipulation
Gel still requires periodic replacement

candidates.title: Integrated Linkage-Driven Dexterous Anthropomorphic Robotic Hand

aliases.title: [Spermidine-induced recovery of human dermal structure and barrier function](https://www.nature.com/articles/s42003-020-01619-4)

candidates.method_summary: Introduces ILDA Hand, a 15-DoF anthropomorphic hand using compact linkage transmissions instead of tendons or direct drive, achieving human-like fingertip force (34N) at human-hand mass (1.1kg). Each finger integrates a 6-axis force/torque sensor at the fingertip. Korean research effort that influenced the SNU tactile-hand line.
limitations: Linkage transmission limits backdrivability vs tendon designs
No skin-level tactile sensing (only fingertip 6-axis FT)
Complex assembly limits open-source replication

group.funding: Korea Institute of Machinery & Materials, NRF Korea

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: VERIFIED. Kim, G., Kim, M., Kim, M., Park, C., Yoon, Y. et al. 2021 *Communications Biology* 4:231, DOI 10.1038/s42003-020-01619-4. Streptococcus-secreted spermidine improves dermal structure/barrier (collagen + lipid synthesis up in aged cells). Note: the paper does not directly center MMP-1 as the spermidine target — the mechanism is broader (lipid/collagen anabolism with downstream effects). Ch5's framing 'MMP-1 with spermidine' for the docking exercise is hypothetical, not from this paper's direct findings.

candidates.title: Isaac Gym: High Performance GPU-Based Physics Simulation For Robot Learning

aliases.title: Isaac Gym: High performance GPU-based physics simulation for robot learning

candidates.method_summary: NVIDIA Isaac Gym co-locates rigid-body physics (PhysX) and neural-network policy training on a single GPU. Thousands of parallel environments run in GPU memory; observations and rewards are kept as CUDA tensors directly consumed by PyTorch policies. Eliminates the CPU/GPU tensor copy bottleneck that limited prior RL simulators. Reports 2-3 orders of magnitude throughput improvement vs MuJoCo + CPU.
limitations: PhysX rigid-body only (no fluids/deformables at launch)
Less accurate contact vs MuJoCo MJX in some regimes
Closed-source PhysX core

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Primary Isaac Gym paper verified; it is historical GPU-simulation lineage and not the current Isaac Sim/Lab compatibility contract.
Canonical Isaac Gym title and arXiv identity verified. GPU simulation throughput does not establish real contact fidelity, controller tracking, or hardware safety.

candidates.title: Learning to Walk in Minutes Using Massively Parallel Deep Reinforcement Learning
candidates.method_summary: Uses Isaac Gym to train ANYmal quadruped locomotion with 4096 parallel robot instances on a single workstation. Introduces terrain curriculum (stairs, slopes, gaps) and a PPO-based training recipe that converges in under 20 minutes of wall-clock. Defines the 'legged_gym' task set that became the de facto reference for later humanoid RL.
limitations: Quadruped only in the original paper
Height-map privileged information used for teacher
Reward engineering remains manual

survey_backrefs.chapter_hint: Ch5 GPU Massively Parallel Simulation, Ch6 Learning Canon

candidates.title: RMA: Rapid Motor Adaptation for Legged Robots

aliases.title: RMA: Rapid motor adaptation for legged robots

candidates.method_summary: Two-phase training: Phase 1 trains a base policy conditioned on an environment extrinsics vector (mass, friction, motor strength) given as privileged info. Phase 2 trains an adaptation module that regresses the extrinsics vector from the recent state-action history (last 50 steps) via supervised learning. At deployment, adaptation module runs at 10 Hz while the base policy runs at 100 Hz. Unitree A1 zero-shot on grass, mud, hiking trails without any real-world fine-tuning.
limitations: Relies on teacher extrinsics vector handcrafted per task
Proprioception-only (blind)
Adaptation is amortized SysID, not online optimization

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: RMA RSS 2021 / arXiv:2107.04034 verified; real quadruped adaptation with privileged simulation context is not tabletop-insertion evidence.
Identifier-deduplicated source linked in the annotated research trail; no new quantitative claim is made.

candidates.title: ViSiFlex: A Low-Cost Compliant Tactile Fingertip for Force, Torque, and Contact Sensing
candidates.method_summary: ViSiFlex is a low-cost compliant fingertip combining a deformable silicone dome with an internal camera tracking embedded fiducial markers to estimate 6-axis wrench and contact location. Vision-based readout yields rich tactile information at fingertip-cost (sub-$50 BOM) and replaces multiple discrete F/T sensors.
limitations: Camera frame rate caps bandwidth (~30-60 Hz)
Calibration per-fingertip required
Wear of compliant skin over time

candidates.title: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models

aliases.title: Denoising diffusion probabilistic models

candidates.method_summary: Formalizes diffusion probabilistic models as denoising score matching with Langevin dynamics under a weighted variational bound. Establishes the DDPM training recipe that Diffusion Policy (Chi 2023) and later π0 (flow matching) adapt for robot action generation. Primary reference for Ch 8 generative-model primer.
limitations: Hundreds of denoising steps for sampling
Training cost scales with time-step count

candidates.title: Exploring the role of palm concavity and adaptability in soft synergistic robotic hands
candidates.method_summary: Studies how palm concavity and compliance affect grasp robustness in synergistic soft hands. Designs three palm variants (rigid flat, rigid concave, soft adaptive) for the Pisa/IIT SoftHand and benchmarks them across grasp types from the Feix taxonomy. Shows that adaptive concave palms significantly enlarge the grasp envelope and reduce required precision in pre-grasp positioning.
limitations: Limited to single-hand SoftHand architecture; generalization to other hands unclear
No tactile sensing on the palm surface
Manual object set rather than standardized YCB benchmark

candidates.title: Learning Memory-Based Control for Human-Scale Bipedal Locomotion
candidates.method_summary: Predecessor to Siekmann 2021. LSTM policy for Cassie sim-to-real. Demonstrates periodic gait through clock-based reward design (variant of Hybrid Zero Dynamics). Establishes the LSTM history-encoder paradigm for biped RL.
limitations: Still flat-ground
Clock-phase reward brittle to terrain variation

candidates.title: Learning Quadrupedal Locomotion Over Challenging Terrain
candidates.method_summary: Introduces the teacher-student privileged-information paradigm to legged RL. Teacher is trained with access to terrain height-map and contact ground truth; student is a proprioception-only policy trained via behavioral cloning plus DAgger-style distillation. Student deploys zero-shot on ANYmal across grass, mud, snow, and debris. Uses a temporal convolutional encoder (TCN) over proprioceptive history as an implicit state estimator.
limitations: Blind — no cameras
Student cannot anticipate cliffs/obstacles
Teacher still relies on privileged height-map in sim

candidates.canonical_id: title:openai dactyl solving rubik s cube with a robot hand
candidates.title: OpenAI Dactyl: Solving Rubik's Cube with a robot hand
candidates.method_summary: Trains a Shadow Dexterous Hand to solve a Rubik's Cube via massive-scale sim-to-real RL with Automatic Domain Randomization (ADR). ADR adaptively expands randomization ranges (mass, friction, latency, visual textures) as the policy improves, training on thousands of CPU cores. Achieves real-world cube solving despite the policy never seeing the real robot during training.
limitations: Massive compute: ~$1M+ training cost equivalent
Vision-only state estimation; no tactile use
Specialized to cube task; generalization limited

group.lead_author: OpenAI team (Ilge Akkaya, Marcin Andrychowicz et al.)

candidates.title: Robust Motion In-Betweening (LAFAN1 Dataset)
candidates.method_summary: Releases LAFAN1 mocap dataset (Ubisoft La Forge): 77 minutes of high-quality character animation across 5 actors. Widely used alongside AMASS as a reference-motion source for humanoid RL. Smaller than AMASS but higher per-clip quality and consistency.
limitations: Small vs AMASS
Character animation focus — some motions not physically feasible for humanoids
Requires retargeting for robot embodiments

candidates.title: Tactile innervation densities across the whole body
candidates.method_summary: Compiles whole-body innervation density estimates from histological literature into a unified model. Quantifies that the fingertips host ~240 mechanoreceptors/cm² vs ~10/cm² on the trunk, with the hand containing ~17,000 of the ~230,000 total cutaneous afferents. Provides the biological reference numbers used to motivate hand-focused robotic skin design.
limitations: Aggregates older histological data with varying methods
Does not distinguish receptor types in fine spatial detail
Inter-subject variability not fully quantified

candidates.title: Cassie Bipedal Robot and the ATRIAS Lineage
candidates.venue: Agility Robotics / Oregon State (ATRIAS → Cassie → Digit)
candidates.method_summary: Cassie is a research biped built on SLIP (spring-loaded inverted pendulum) principles: centralize mass in torso, minimize leg inertia. Leaf springs are located at shin pitch (q5) and tarsus pitch (q6) joints (the actuated toe is at q7) — NOT at the ankle as shorthand often implies. ATRIAS-descended design. Lineage runs ATRIAS (2014) → Cassie (2017) → Digit (2020, Agility). The passive-dynamics bet distinguishes the Hurst lineage from Kim/MIT's QDD lineage.
limitations: Leaf-spring compliance at shin/tarsus limits some reactive whole-body control
Non-anthropomorphic gait
Dexterous hand integration harder

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Ch12 §12.2 Figure 02 BMW pilot + OpenAI partnership. Public press (Figure AI blog Aug 2024). Primary-verified.

candidates.title: Learning Agile and Dynamic Motor Skills for Legged Robots
candidates.method_summary: First fully-learned quadruped locomotion that transfers zero-shot to hardware. Key innovation is the actuator network: a neural net trained on real ANYmal motor data (joint state history → joint torque) that replaces the idealized actuator model in simulation, closing a major sim-to-real gap. Uses TRPO with a stochastic policy + randomized dynamics (mass, link length, COM offsets). Recovers from falls and runs at 1.6 m/s, up from 0.8 m/s of the shipped controller.
limitations: Blind (no exteroception)
Actuator network assumes joint-independent dynamics
Only flat terrain demonstrated
Training took 9-11 h on single workstation (pre-GPU-sim era)

candidates.title: Learning the signatures of the human grasp using a scalable tactile glove (STAG)
candidates.method_summary: Knitted tactile glove with 548 piezoresistive sensors on the palmar surface; readout via row-column matrix scanning. Trained CNNs on ~135,000 frames of human grasps to identify objects (76% top-1 from a single touch) and estimate object weight. Established the feasibility of dense, low-cost wearable tactile capture.
limitations: Coverage limited to palmar surface (no dorsal or finger-side)
Piezoresistive cells suffer hysteresis and drift
No force calibration; relative pressure only

candidates.title: Mini Cheetah: A Platform for Pushing the Limits of Dynamic Quadruped Control
candidates.method_summary: Presents the Mini Cheetah: 9 kg quadruped with 12 modular QDD actuators, each ~17 Nm peak torque at ~250 Hz control bandwidth. Uses MPC with simplified centroidal dynamics for gait generation. Demonstrates a sim-trained or hand-tuned backflip policy — the first quadruped aerial flip at this size. Establishes the modular low-cost QDD platform template later mirrored by Unitree A1/Go1/Go2.
limitations: Limited payload (~2 kg)
Small battery runtime (~15 min)
No onboard perception

candidates.title: SOTER: A Runtime Assurance Framework for Programming Safe Robotics Systems
candidates.venue: 2019 49th Annual IEEE/IFIP International Conference on Dependable Systems and Networks (DSN)
candidates.method_summary: Defines a well-formed runtime-assurance module with an advanced controller, a pre-verified safe controller, and a decision module that switches control under explicit safety-property, plant, state, and timing assumptions. For S13, SOTER supports an independently enforced fallback architecture; it does not confer torque, collision, force-limit, protective-stop, E-stop, or final human authority on VLA, world-model, or agent outputs.
limitations: Guarantees are conditional on the well-formed runtime-assurance module, modeled plant/state, safety property, sampling period, and decision-module assumptions
Simulation and actual-drone evidence do not establish tabletop manipulation, contact-force safety, calibration readiness, or a complete cell risk assessment
Switching to the safe controller can reduce task performance

Reward weighting hand-tuned

candidates.title: Dynamic Locomotion in the MIT Cheetah 3 through Convex Model-Predictive Control

Quadruped (not humanoid)
Pre-RL — manual gait scheduling

candidates.title: Linear Matrix Inequalities for Physically Consistent Inertial Parameter Identification
candidates.method_summary: Characterizes physically consistent rigid-body inertial parameters with convex constraints on mass, center of mass, and inertia. candidates.title: Soft Actor-Critic: Off-Policy Maximum Entropy Deep Reinforcement Learning with a Stochastic Actor

aliases.title: Soft actor-critic: Off-policy maximum entropy deep reinforcement learning with a stochastic actor

candidates.method_summary: SAC: off-policy actor-critic under the maximum entropy RL framework. Stochastic actor encourages exploration via entropy bonus; twin critics mitigate over-estimation. The default off-policy baseline for robot learning before 2021 and a key building block for FastTD3-style humanoid RL in 2025.
limitations: Requires critic target tuning
Entropy coefficient sensitive unless auto-tuned (addressed in follow-up)

candidates.title: Toward dexterous manipulation with augmented adaptive synergies: The Pisa/IIT SoftHand 2
candidates.method_summary: Pisa/IIT SoftHand 2 implements augmented adaptive synergies: a 2-motor, 19-DoF anthropomorphic hand using soft tendon-driven mechanism with two synergistic actuators encoding human-inspired postural primitives. Adds a second synergy for in-hand manipulation atop the original SoftHand single-synergy design, enabling both robust grasping and dexterous repositioning with minimal control inputs.
limitations: Only 2 actuators limits independent finger control for complex precision tasks
No integrated tactile sensing on the soft fingers in this version
Tendon-based compliance complicates accurate proprioceptive state estimation

candidates.title: AMBIDEX: Cable-Driven Dual-Arm Manipulator from NAVER LABS
candidates.venue: NAVER LABS product / Korea Tech research
candidates.method_summary: Cable-driven compliant dual-arm manipulator built through NAVER LABS + Korea Tech collaboration. Emphasizes human-safe force transfer, low inertia (cable relocates motors to torso), high compliance. Contrasts with QDD-first trend — a Korean alternative design philosophy focused on compliance over backdrivability-via-low-gear. Updated 2024 under 'Software-Defined (SDx) Robotics Innovation' program.
limitations: Cable mechanisms harder to maintain than direct drive
Limited dynamic bandwidth vs QDD
Primarily manipulation — not bipedal

candidates.title: Attention Is All You Need
candidates.method_summary: Introduces the Transformer architecture based entirely on self-attention, dispensing with recurrence. Enables parallel sequence modeling and the history-encoder architecture that replaced LSTMs in later legged RL (Radosavovic 2024, Helix, GR00T). Foundational for Ch 8 and Ch 9 3-Layer architecture.
limitations: Quadratic attention complexity limits very long contexts
Original design is NLP-only

candidates.title: Control Barrier Function Based Quadratic Programs for Safety Critical Systems
candidates.method_summary: Shows how control barrier functions can impose forward-invariant safety constraints through an online quadratic program alongside control-Lyapunov objectives. The inspected 2017 primary source keeps this component's outputs at its stated abstraction level and does not confer torque, collision, force-limit, protective-stop, E-stop, or final human authority. For S13, the method is evidence for control-barrier-function, constraint-projection, formal-safety and is compared against the unchanged S11/S12 skill, feasibility, controller, evaluator, and rollback contracts.
limitations: Forward invariance depends on state estimation and model assumptions
Feasibility and solver deadlines must be handled separately

candidates.title: Design of a variable stiffness soft dexterous gripper
candidates.method_summary: Designs a soft 3-finger gripper combining pneumatic muscle actuators with granular jamming for variable stiffness. Each finger can independently switch between compliant (object-conforming) and stiff (load-bearing) modes via vacuum pressure on jamming chambers, giving dexterity-grade adaptability with industrial-grade payload.
limitations: Pneumatic infrastructure limits portability
Slow stiffness transitions (seconds-scale)
No integrated tactile sensing

candidates.title: GelSight: High-Resolution Robot Tactile Sensors for Estimating Geometry and Force

aliases.title: GelSight: High-resolution robot tactile sensors for estimating geometry and force

candidates.method_summary: Introduces GelSight as a vision-based tactile sensor using a deformable elastomer with painted markers, imaged by an internal camera with controlled LED illumination. Photometric stereo recovers 3D contact surface geometry at ~30µm spatial resolution; marker displacement gives shear/normal force fields. Established the dominant paradigm for high-resolution tactile sensing.
limitations: Bulky form factor (camera + optics) limits hand integration
Frame-rate limited by camera (~30-60Hz)
Gel wear and re-coating required after extended use

candidates.title: MANO: A Hand Model with Articulated and Non-rigid Deformations

aliases.title: Embodied hands (MANO)

candidates.method_summary: MANO is a learned, low-dimensional parametric hand model derived from ~1000 high-resolution 3D scans of hands in many poses. Combines articulated kinematics (16 joints, 51 pose parameters) with PCA-based shape (10 components) and pose-dependent corrective blendshapes for realistic non-rigid skin deformation. Becomes the de facto standard hand representation across vision, graphics, and robot retargeting.
limitations: Skin-only model — no internal bone/tendon dynamics
Limited demographic diversity in scan dataset
No tactile or contact priors

candidates.title: Optimization-Based Control for Dynamic Legged Robots

aliases.title: Optimization-based control for dynamic legged robots
Tactile Robotics: An outlook

candidates.method_summary: Recent comprehensive survey of QP-based / MPC-based control for dynamic legged robots. Ties together HZD, capture point, whole-body QP, centroidal dynamics. Essential reference for Ch 1-2 foundations and Ch 11 Boston Dynamics hybrid MPC+RL bridge.
limitations: Pre-RL-dominance framing
Short on learning-integration

group.affiliation: Notre Dame (Wensing) + Penn (Posa) + CNRS + U Trento

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Ch11 §11.3 Cheetah 3 MPC citation. Primary-source-verified (IEEE Xplore abstract + MIT DSpace + arXiv search): 3 m/s forward speed ✓, 20-30 Hz MPC rate ✓, under 1 ms solve time per primary source. First-author Di Carlo ✓. Book §11.3 does NOT quote a specific solve-time number (the '1-2 ms' flag I raised earlier was a false positive — my review list conflated with book-writer's pre-audit numerical-claims list, which included the claim as a to-verify item but the shipped §11.3 prose omits the specific number). Ch11 §11.3 remains clean. Book-writer option: add 'solves in under 1 ms on commodity hardware' for pedagogical clarity, but not required.
Identifier-deduplicated source linked in the annotated research trail; no new quantitative claim is made.

candidates.title: Proprioceptive Actuator Design in the MIT Cheetah: Impact Mitigation and High-Bandwidth Physical Interaction for Dynamic Legged Robots
candidates.method_summary: Establishes design principles for proprioceptive actuators: high gap-radius custom outer-rotor BLDC with low gear ratio (5.8:1 single-stage planetary). Eliminates load-side torque sensors; instead uses motor current as a torque proxy for high-bandwidth (>100 Hz) force control. Defines the Impact Mitigation Factor (IMF) as a normalized metric for backdrivability at impact. Validates on MIT Cheetah 2 with peak foot forces >450 N at contact times of 85 ms.
limitations: Torque estimates require thermal/current calibration
Limited output torque vs. high-reduction systems
Heat dissipation limits continuous duty at high load

candidates.title: Proximal Policy Optimization Algorithms

aliases.title: Proximal policy optimization algorithms

candidates.method_summary: Proposes PPO, a policy-gradient method that optimizes a clipped surrogate objective approximating the TRPO trust region while staying easy to implement with first-order SGD. Default choice for modern legged-RL pipelines (legged_gym, Isaac Lab, Humanoid-Gym) due to stability under heavy domain randomization and parallelism.
limitations: On-policy — sample-inefficient vs SAC/TD3 when real-world data is costly
Hyperparam sensitive (clip ratio, batch size, learning rate)
Struggles with long-horizon sparse rewards without reward shaping

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Canonical PPO title, five-author record, and arXiv identity verified. Used as an algorithm lineage reference without promoting an unmatched robotics result.
Identifier-deduplicated source linked in the annotated research trail; no new quantitative claim is made.

candidates.title: Shape memory alloy-based soft gripper with variable stiffness for compliant and effective grasping
candidates.method_summary: Develops a soft gripper using shape-memory-alloy (SMA) wires for both actuation and stiffness modulation. Joule heating activates SMA fingers to bend, while a second SMA layer changes stiffness, enabling a single-material approach to compliant grasping with electrical control rather than pneumatics.
limitations: Slow thermal response of SMA
Heat dissipation limits cycle frequency
Power consumption high relative to grasp force

candidates.title: Mastering the game of Go with deep neural networks and tree search
candidates.method_summary: AlphaGo couples a policy network (supervised from 30M expert moves, then improved via self-play) and a value network with Monte Carlo Tree Search at decision time. Test-time compute (deeper search) is the lever that scales strength beyond the policy network's instantaneous play. This is the canonical demonstration that 'search over a neural policy + value head' beats either component alone — the structural inspiration for Tree-of-Thoughts, Sakana AI Scientist v2's tree-search manager, and Google AI Co-Scientist's Elo-tournament evolution loop.
limitations: Domain-specific (Go has a perfect simulator)
training compute massive even by 2016 standards
no open-ended discovery — fixed objective

candidates.title: Supersizing Self-Supervision: Learning to Grasp from 50K Tries and 700 Robot Hours

candidates.method_summary: Collects a large self-supervised grasping dataset by allowing a robot to attempt grasps repeatedly and learn from success or failure. It is one of the clearest pre-foundation-model examples that robot manipulation performance can improve by scaling real interaction data.
limitations: Single-robot, mostly table-top grasping.
Interaction scale is large for 2016 but small compared with modern cross-institution datasets.

candidates.title: Underactuated modular finger with pull-in mechanism for a robotic gripper
candidates.method_summary: Proposes a modular underactuated robotic finger with a pull-in mechanism that wraps around objects upon contact. Single-tendon actuation drives multi-joint flexion through a passive linkage that adapts to object geometry, enabling robust enveloping grasps across diverse shapes with only one motor per finger.
limitations: Open-loop without tactile or force sensing
Limited fingertip dexterity for precision pinch tasks
Bench evaluation only, no integrated arm-hand experiments

candidates.canonical_id: title:a compliant underactuated hand for robust manipulation
candidates.title: A compliant, underactuated hand for robust manipulation
candidates.method_summary: Introduces the iHY (iRobot-Harvard-Yale) hand: a 3-finger, 5-actuator compliant underactuated gripper using flexure joints and tendons. Demonstrates that mechanical compliance and underactuation, combined with simple control, achieve robust grasping and basic in-hand manipulation under sensor-poor and uncertain conditions, validated on hundreds of grasp trials.
limitations: Only 5 DoF — limited fine manipulation
Tendon stretch and compliance reduce position accuracy
Tactile sensing limited to fingertip pressure pads

candidates.title: Adaptive Synergies for the Design and Control of the Pisa/IIT SoftHand

aliases.title: Adaptive synergies for the design and control of the Pisa/IIT SoftHand

candidates.method_summary: Introduces the Pisa/IIT SoftHand: a 19-DoF, single-motor underactuated anthropomorphic hand exploiting a postural synergy + soft-rolling-contact joints. The 'first synergy' (Santello et al. 1998) is implemented mechanically via tendon routing, allowing one motor to drive a continuum of human-like grasps that adapt to object shape via mechanical compliance.
limitations: Single synergy limits in-hand manipulation primitives
No fingertip tactile sensing in original design
Tendon wear under heavy use

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: DOI and IJRR metadata verified; supports adaptive underactuation/synergy claims within the Pisa/IIT SoftHand design.

candidates.title: Design and Control Considerations for High-Performance Series Elastic Actuators
candidates.method_summary: Series-elastic actuator (SEA) design + control: elastic element in series with motor, load-side deflection sensor → direct torque estimation. The primary pre-QDD approach to compliant legged actuation. Used in Cassie/Digit (Agility), ATRIAS, and early NASA Valkyrie. Useful Ch 4 contrast baseline for QDD vs SEA philosophies.
limitations: Lower bandwidth than QDD
Spring adds inertia and tuning overhead
Harder to model for RL sim-to-real

candidates.title: Design and Control Considerations for High-Performance Series Elastic Actuators
candidates.method_summary: Series-elastic actuator (SEA) design + control: elastic element in series with motor, load-side deflection sensor → direct torque estimation. The primary pre-QDD approach to compliant legged actuation. Used in Cassie/Digit (Agility), ATRIAS, and early NASA Valkyrie. Useful Ch 4 contrast baseline for QDD vs SEA philosophies.
limitations: Lower bandwidth than QDD

candidates.title: Development of an Optoelectronic 6-Axis Force/Torque Sensor for Robotic Applications
candidates.method_summary: Designs a 6-axis F/T sensor using optoelectronic transducers (LED-photodiode pairs measuring deflection of a compliant element) instead of strain gauges. Optical sensing offers immunity to EMI, easier manufacturing, and lower cost while achieving comparable accuracy on small form factors suitable for robot fingertips.
limitations: Optical alignment requires care in assembly
Limited bandwidth compared to high-end strain gauges
Sensitive to ambient light leakage

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: DOI 10.1016/j.sna.2014.09.023 confirmed.
Identifier-deduplicated source linked in the annotated research trail; no new quantitative claim is made.

candidates.title: Identification of Robot Dynamics with the Instrumental Variable Method
candidates.method_summary: Applies instrumental variables to robot inverse-dynamics identification to reduce bias from noisy regressors and closed-loop data. It iteratively constructs instruments from a simulated robot model. The method addresses a key problem when only vendor-controller logs are available.
limitations: Requires a useful initial model and noise assumptions; unmodeled friction and controller nonlinearities can remain.

group.affiliation: See canonical author affiliations in primary source

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Primary record is Janot, Vandanjon and Gautier, IEEE TCST 22(1), DOI 10.1109/TCST.2013.2246163. It supports closed-loop/noisy-regressor IV identification and simultaneous direct/inverse-model validation. The manuscript/ledger DOI/title are incorrect.

candidates.title: Introduction to Humanoid Robotics
candidates.method_summary: Canonical textbook summarizing the LIPM / ZMP / preview-control / whole-body QP lineage. Provides the formal derivations that underlie the orthodox humanoid stack covered in Ch 1-2. Primary reference for pre-RL humanoid kinematics, dynamics, and control.
limitations: Does not cover post-2015 RL-based control
Pre-QDD actuator analysis

candidates.title: Optimization-based Full Body Control for the DARPA Robotics Challenge
candidates.method_summary: Documents CMU's whole-body QP controller for the hydraulic Atlas at the DARPA Robotics Challenge. Decomposes control as high-level footstep/manipulation planners producing task-space trajectories, then a 1 kHz full-body inverse dynamics QP that computes joint torques subject to friction cone, joint limit, and centroidal dynamics constraints. Reports qualitative performance on DRC tasks (door, valve, drill) plus the brittleness under unmodeled contact events.
limitations: Brittle under contact surprises
Requires accurate dynamic and frictional models
High engineering burden per scenario
Planning/control decoupling fails under large disturbances

candidates.title: Control of Legged Robots with Optimal Distribution of Contact Forces

limitations: Requires accurate dynamic + friction models
Brittle under contact surprises
Pre-RL era

limitations: Early Cheetah — no stable dynamic balance control yet
Pre-RL

candidates.title: Mini Cheetah: A Platform for Pushing the Limits of Dynamic Quadruped Control

aliases.title: Mini Cheetah: A platform for pushing the limits of dynamic quadruped control

candidates.method_summary: Presents the Mini Cheetah: 9 kg quadruped with 12 modular QDD actuators, each ~17 Nm peak torque at ~250 Hz control bandwidth. Uses MPC with simplified centroidal dynamics for gait generation. Demonstrates a sim-trained or hand-tuned backflip policy — the first quadruped aerial flip at this size. Establishes the modular low-cost QDD platform template later mirrored by Unitree A1/Go1/Go2.
limitations: Limited payload (~2 kg)
Small battery runtime (~15 min)
No onboard perception

candidates.title: Reinforcement Learning in Robotics: A Survey
candidates.method_summary: Pre-deep-RL survey of reinforcement-learning techniques in robotics. Useful Ch 2 / Ch 8 bridge showing the state-of-the-art before 2015 and the gap that GPU-parallel sim + deep policies closed.
limitations: Pre-deep-RL
Limited sim-to-real discussion

candidates.title: V-REP: a Versatile and Scalable Robot Simulation Framework

limitations: Now known as CoppeliaSim; cross-engine agreement does not imply real-world validity.

candidates.title: Capturability-based Analysis and Control of Legged Locomotion, Part 2: Application to M2V2, a Lower-Body Humanoid
candidates.method_summary: M2V2 hardware validation companion to Pratt 2006 Humanoids capture-point paper. Extends capturability formalism with N-step capture regions, then applies the control algorithm to M2V2, a 3D force-controlled lower-body humanoid with 12 actuated leg DoF. Evaluated on push-recovery while single-legged balancing and on walking tasks, both in simulation and on M2V2 hardware. Primary source for the 0.3 m CP-displacement / ~10% body-weight-impulse M2V2 numbers mis-attributed to Pratt 2006.
limitations: Lower-body only (no torso/arms)
Pre-RL era control
12 DoF limits full-humanoid generality

candidates.method_summary: Introduces DAgger, an iterative imitation-learning algorithm that aggregates data from states visited by the learned policy. This is foundational for robot manipulation because covariate shift is exactly why one-shot behavior cloning often fails in real factories.
limitations: Requires repeated expert labeling or correction.
Original tasks are not robot-manipulation-scale.

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Canonical title, authors, AISTATS 2011 identity, and arXiv:1011.0686 verified. DAgger supports learner-state aggregation under its assumptions; it does not authorize deliberately visiting unsafe robot states.

candidates.title: Hierarchical Task and Motion Planning in the Now
candidates.method_summary: Interleaves symbolic task choices with geometric feasibility checks instead of committing to a complete symbolic plan before motion planning. The inspected 2011 primary source keeps this component's outputs at its stated abstraction level and does not confer torque, collision, force-limit, protective-stop, E-stop, or final human authority. For S13, the method is evidence for skill-contract, tamp, replanning and is compared against the unchanged S11/S12 skill, feasibility, controller, evaluator, and rollback contracts.
limitations: Relies on modeled actions and geometry
Scaling remains sensitive to branching and motion-planning cost

candidates.title: Tactile Sensing: From Humans to Humanoids
candidates.method_summary: Seminal survey bridging biological and engineered tactile sensing for humanoid robots. Catalogs transduction principles (capacitive, piezoresistive, piezoelectric, optical) and identifies key requirements: spatial resolution ~1mm, temporal bandwidth >100Hz, multimodal coverage. Sets the agenda for the next decade of tactile skin development at IIT and beyond.
limitations: Pre-dates GelSight/DIGIT vision-based tactile revolution
Limited coverage of learning-based tactile processing
Hardware focus; software/representation receives less attention

candidates.title: Coding and Use of Tactile Signals from the Fingertips in Object Manipulation Tasks
candidates.method_summary: Foundational neuroscience review on how human fingertip mechanoreceptors (FA-I, SA-I, FA-II, SA-II) encode contact events during manipulation. Identifies discrete tactile events (contact, slip, release) as the key control signals, with sub-100ms reflexive grip-force adjustments via spinal/cortical loops. Establishes the conceptual basis for event-driven tactile control in robotics.
limitations: Biological data not directly transferable to engineered sensors
Focus on fingertip; palm and finger-side innervation less covered
Pre-2010 review predates modern vision-based tactile sensors

candidates.title: Learning Object-Action Complexes: A Probabilistic Generative Approach
candidates.method_summary: Learns a joint Bayesian model over object properties, actions, and observed effects so any subset can predict the others. The inspected 2008 primary source keeps this component's outputs at its stated abstraction level and does not confer torque, collision, force-limit, protective-stop, E-stop, or final human authority. For S13, the method is evidence for affordance, probabilistic-model, effect-prediction and is compared against the unchanged S11/S12 skill, feasibility, controller, evaluator, and rollback contracts.
limitations: Small engineered object/action vocabulary
Learned correlations do not enforce collision or force feasibility

Capture Point: A Step toward Humanoid Push Recovery
candidates.method_summary: Introduces the capture point (CP) concept: the point on the ground where a biped can step to bring itself to rest, derived from the LIPM orbital energy. Proposes N-step capture regions and uses them for reactive footstep placement in response to pushes. Validated on a Yobotics-sim planar biped with flywheel body. M2V2 hardware validation appears in follow-up Pratt/Koolen 2012 IJRR paper.
limitations: LIPM assumption breaks down under large CoM excursions
Reactive stepping is 1-2 steps only
Real-world noisy state estimation degrades CP

candidates.title: Biped walking pattern generation by using preview control of zero-moment point
candidates.method_summary: Formulates bipedal walking pattern generation as tracking a reference zero-moment-point (ZMP) trajectory with a Linear Inverted Pendulum Model (LIPM) whose center of mass height is held constant. Uses preview control (a discrete-time LQR with a finite preview window of future ZMP references, typically 1.6 s / 1.6× step period) so that the CoM trajectory can anticipate upcoming steps. Demonstrated on HRP-2P with step period around 0.8 s and sinusoidal foot placements.
limitations: Assumes constant CoM height (LIPM)
Requires precise ZMP reference planned offline
Fails under large unmodeled disturbances or foot-slip
No explicit reasoning about contact uncertainty

candidates.title: Hands for Dexterous Manipulation and Robust Grasping: A Difficult Road Toward Simplicity
candidates.venue: IEEE Transactions on Robotics and Automation

aliases.title: Hands for dexterous manipulation and robust grasping: A difficult road toward simplicity
Hands for dexterous manipulation

candidates.method_summary: Highly cited essay arguing that the field's earlier obsession with high-DoF anthropomorphic hands (Utah/MIT, Stanford-JPL) was misguided; advocates synergy-based, underactuated, compliant designs that exploit task mechanics. Lays the conceptual groundwork for the Pisa/IIT SoftHand and Schunk SDH families.
limitations: Essay format; no original experiments
Pre-dates modern learning-based controllers that revive high-DoF designs
Italian/European bias in cited robot platforms

candidates.title: Robotic Grasping and Contact: A Review
candidates.venue: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation
candidates.method_summary: Canonical ICRA review of grasp analysis: form/force closure conditions, contact modeling (point, soft-finger, friction cones), grasp matrix and Jacobian formulations, and stability metrics. Standard reference textbook treatment for analytical grasp synthesis algorithms used through the 2010s.
limitations: Pre-data-driven era; no learning-based grasp synthesis
Rigid-body assumption; soft contact treated heuristically
Limited treatment of multi-finger in-hand manipulation

candidates.title: A Direct and Efficient Method for Identifying the Minimum Dynamic Parameters of Robots

candidates.canonical_id: title:the kinematics of contact and grasp
candidates.title: The Kinematics of Contact and Grasp
candidates.method_summary: Derives the differential geometry of rolling, sliding, and twisting contact between smooth bodies. It is a core theoretical source for understanding why tactile and contact-state data matter for in-hand manipulation and precision assembly.

candidates.method_summary: Foundational operational-space formulation for redundant manipulator control. Maps task-space desired forces/accelerations to joint torques via the dynamically consistent generalized inverse. limitations: Assumes perfect model
Pre-learning era
No contact uncertainty handling

candidates.title: A Robust Layered Control System for a Mobile Robot
candidates.venue: IEEE Journal on Robotics and Automation
candidates.method_summary: Introduces subsumption as layered asynchronous behaviors in which higher layers suppress or inhibit lower outputs rather than replacing the whole control stack. The inspected 1986 primary source keeps this component's outputs at its stated abstraction level and does not confer torque, collision, force-limit, protective-stop, E-stop, or final human authority. For S13, the method is evidence for layer-owner, fallback and is compared against the unchanged S11/S12 skill, feasibility, controller, evaluator, and rollback contracts.
limitations: Mobile navigation does not establish manipulation performance
Suppression links can become hard to audit as behaviors grow

candidates.title: Geometric Approach for Robotic Calibration
candidates.venue: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation
candidates.method_summary: Introduces a kinematic calibration parameterization designed to avoid singular behavior of standard Denavit-Hartenberg parameters near parallel joint axes. The method connects measured end-effector error to identifiable geometric corrections. It is foundational for custom-arm zero-offset and link-geometry calibration.
limitations: Geometric calibration cannot absorb compliance, backlash, thermal drift, base motion, or time delay without corrupting parameters.

group.affiliation: See canonical author affiliations in primary source

survey_backrefs.factcheck_notes: Primary bibliographic record is Hayati and Mirmirani, “Improving the Absolute Positioning Accuracy of Robot Manipulators,” Journal of Robotic Systems 2(4), DOI 10.1002/rob.4620020406. aliases.title: Impedance control: An approach to manipulation
Impedance control
[Impedance Control: An Approach to Manipulation, Part I—Theory](https://doi.org/10.1115/1.3140702)

Impedance Control: An Approach to Manipulation, Part I—Theory

candidates.method_summary: Foundational three-part paper introducing impedance control: instead of regulating position or force separately, control the dynamic relationship (mass-spring-damper) between manipulator and environment. Provides theoretical justification for compliant interaction during contact-rich tasks; underpins virtually all modern force/admittance controllers.
limitations: Theoretical; assumes accurate dynamic models
Linear analysis; nonlinear contact dynamics treated only heuristically
Pre-dates modern data-driven impedance learning
